searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? many radio programs from the 1930s have been lost. these losses include several programs created by the first wave of latina radio hosts. we know about these missing broadcasts only from small pieces of evidence. for example, a flyer advertising rosita fernandez's variety show una hora de música still exists. there's a mention in a magazine of lydia mendoza's el ranchito musical . and carmen salinas's news show voces del sur is referenced in a letter and a newspaper review, with one photograph of the broadcast booth turning up in the 1980s.

preview: Sandra Cisneros is best known for The House on Mango Street , her 1984 novel about a young Mexican American girl growing up in Chicago. The…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? sandra cisneros is best known for the house on mango street , her 1984 novel about a young mexican american girl growing up in chicago. the novel grew out of a series of short prose pieces cisneros had written years earlier. building the novel wasn't easy. most novels rely on a steady, continuous narrative, but cisneros's early sketches were brief and lyrical, more like poems than chapters. to transform the sketches into a novel, cisneros arranged them in an order that traced the narrator's emotional growth across a single year.

preview: Following the removal of the sea otter from California's coastal kelp forests in the early 1900s, the population of sea urchins—a primary p…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? following the removal of the sea otter from california's coastal kelp forests in the early 1900s, the population of sea urchins—a primary prey of the sea otter—grew larger than the ecosystem could support. sea urchin overpopulation led to overgrazing of kelp forests that many other ocean species relied on for food and shelter. as scientists began to see how essential the sea otter was to the kelp ecosystem, restoration strategies were carried out to return otters to the central california coast in the 1990s. the rebound effect in the kelp forests was noticed within a few years.

preview: The following text is adapted from a 2010 novel. The narrator discusses her relationship with her young nephew, Theo. Maren, the narrator's…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? the following text is adapted from a 2010 novel. the narrator discusses her relationship with her young nephew, theo. maren, the narrator's sister-in-law, is theo's mother. theo was as restless with babyhood as i had been. he tried at once to grow out of it. maren never saw that, because she, and only she, took comfort in theo's helpless softness. only maren was saddened by her son's fierce progress. week by week, theo grew stronger. strapped into his high chair, he reached and stretched until his arms tired, working again and again to grab the spoon for himself.

preview: Oral histories—whether they consist of conversations or recordings of traditional songs—can offer researchers a rich window into people's e…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? oral histories—whether they consist of conversations or recordings of traditional songs—can offer researchers a rich window into people's everyday experiences. for her book about textile workers in 1930s north carolina, historian maya reynolds therefore drew in part on interviews with retired mill workers and their families. through these conversations, she gained meaningful insight into the daily routines of mill life.

preview: Subscription meal-kit services are giving home cooks more options to receive ready-to-prepare ingredients than ever before. Whether orderin…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? subscription meal-kit services are giving home cooks more options to receive ready-to-prepare ingredients than ever before. whether ordering kits for weeknight dinners, quick lunches, or weekend recipes, customers can choose meals that match their schedules and tastes. companies profit too: studies have shown that subscribers are willing to pay a premium for the convenience of pre-portioned ingredients. still, it can be hard for companies to keep meal kits both fresh and affordable, as standard food-distribution systems may not handle small, customized orders efficiently.

preview: The following text is from a 1923 poem titled "The Traveler." I walk among the streets of foreign towns Where streetlamps stand in rows alo…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined lines in the text as a whole? the following text is from a 1923 poem titled "the traveler." i walk among the streets of foreign towns where streetlamps stand in rows along the curb and every doorway hums with passing crowds and all the city's noises never cease. but deep within the southern winds still spin— i feel the sun upon my native fields.

preview: In 2010, computer scientist Adriana Vargas was working on improving voice-recognition systems for smartphones. She found that the systems s…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? in 2010, computer scientist adriana vargas was working on improving voice-recognition systems for smartphones. she found that the systems struggled with some accents and background noises that humans could parse easily. based on that finding, vargas developed a simple online task that recorded volunteers reading short passages aloud. the first version of her project asked volunteers to read one familiar phrase and one phrase that the system had previously misheard. each correct recording added data to the voice-recognition training set and produced a more reliable assistant.

preview: In the early days of streaming video in the 2000s, many people thought that online video services would rely on the financial support of su…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined phrase in the text as a whole? in the early days of streaming video in the 2000s, many people thought that online video services would rely on the financial support of subscribers, much like cable television did. but subscribers hesitated to commit to a new service, particularly at a time when most households had limited broadband internet, which made smooth video playback unreliable. streaming companies, like netflix, needed to convince subscribers to pay for their service despite not knowing whether the technology would deliver a consistent viewing experience.

preview: The following text is from a 1925 poem titled "To My Daughter." Step forth, my child, Lifted by my fondest hope! Wide horizons, yet unseen,…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is from a 1925 poem titled "to my daughter." step forth, my child, lifted by my fondest hope! wide horizons, yet unseen, wait for your discovery. i cannot, though i wish, walk the road beside you, my journey nears its end, but the world is calling you!

preview: Built in the 1980s, the Bath County facility in Virginia is a pumped-storage hydropower plant (sometimes called a "water battery"). When re…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? built in the 1980s, the bath county facility in virginia is a pumped-storage hydropower plant (sometimes called a "water battery"). when regional electricity demand is low, surplus power from the area's coal and nuclear plants is used to pump water from a lower reservoir up through a tunnel to an upper reservoir at the top of the mountain, where the water is held as gravitational potential energy. when electricity demand peaks, water flows back down through the tunnel, spinning turbines and generating up to 3,000 megawatts of power—enough to power more than two million homes for several hours.

preview: In most retail environments, returned electronics that are damaged are crushed and sent to landfills. City officials in Austin, Texas, want…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? in most retail environments, returned electronics that are damaged are crushed and sent to landfills. city officials in austin, texas, wanted to reduce this waste. the officials passed a law requiring retailers to refurbish certain returned devices instead. refurbishment involves carefully testing and repairing items so they can be resold or donated. damage to the components is avoided so that they can be reused in working products. a 2022 study found that 34 percent of returned electronics in austin were able to be refurbished. the remaining items were broken down for recycling instead of being landfilled.

preview: The following text is adapted from a 1903 nonfiction essay. Those who chase happiness as the sole purpose of life never know when they have…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined question in the text as a whole? the following text is adapted from a 1903 nonfiction essay. those who chase happiness as the sole purpose of life never know when they have arrived. they cannot know. in one sense it is of course necessary to seek joy, for that is a basic pursuit of every age. but to acknowledge that contentment itself is fleeting and unsteady is the wiser truth. the final puzzle is the heart. when one has counted every coin in the chest, named every star in the dome of night, and traced every road on every map, there still remains the heart. who can chart the weather of his own joy?

preview: The following text is from a 2015 memoir. The narrator, Lucia, recalls a time when she and her younger brother Sam were children. A trading…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text? the following text is from a 2015 memoir. the narrator, lucia, recalls a time when she and her younger brother sam were children. a trading card is a small printed card collected for its picture or stats. i asked sam to come trade cards with me. he wasn't as interested in my collection as he had been only a month before, which made me sad because that was the one thing sam had admired me for . usually, it was me doing the admiring. after i asked him again and again, he finally set down his book and came over to me.

preview: In the 1920s, scientists didn't know much about the surface of Venus. Many scientists at the time believed that Venus's surface was lush an…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? in the 1920s, scientists didn't know much about the surface of venus. many scientists at the time believed that venus's surface was lush and tropical. but astronomers carl sagan and james pollack later showed that this idea was wrong. using radio telescope data and atmospheric measurements, sagan and pollack demonstrated that venus's surface is in fact incredibly hot, with temperatures high enough to melt lead.

preview: Bachata is a music form that was first developed in Dominican communities. Bachata was heavily influenced by Cuban bolero, which became wid…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? bachata is a music form that was first developed in dominican communities. bachata was heavily influenced by cuban bolero, which became widely popular in latin america in the mid-twentieth century. bachata musicians were inspired by bolero's romantic themes and the way bolero singers shaped phrases with feeling. as bolero continued to evolve in the 1960s and 1970s, bachata evolved with it. because of bolero's influence, bachata quickly developed into a music form quite different from earlier styles of caribbean folk music.

preview: The following text is adapted from a 2006 novel. Aunt Imelda was as cheerful as my mother was solemn. Even when she wasn't laughing, she ma…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence? the following text is adapted from a 2006 novel. aunt imelda was as cheerful as my mother was solemn. even when she wasn't laughing, she made the room feel light, humming under her breath and tapping her foot against the chair leg.

preview: Philadelphia is known for the bold murals painted on many of its walls. The densest collection of murals is found along South Street in the…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? philadelphia is known for the bold murals painted on many of its walls. the densest collection of murals is found along south street in the bella vista neighborhood. in the 1980s, local artists painted vivid scenes of community life along this stretch of road. as the original murals have weathered, later generations of artists have painted new ones over them. as a result, south street has become a living showcase of philadelphia's artistic spirit, with its murals reflecting changes in the cultural life of the city.

preview: The following text is from a 1908 novel. Anya is walking down the lane behind the orchard that she ran through often as a child. She walked…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? the following text is from a 1908 novel. anya is walking down the lane behind the orchard that she ran through often as a child. she walked along the path she knew so well. the blossoming trees seemed to lean over her like familiar neighbors greeting an old friend. they greeted her as an old friend. soon she reached the orchard gate.

preview: Most discarded fishing nets today wind up in the ocean or are, at best, sent to landfills. To address this problem, materials engineer Hana…
searchText: which choice best states the function of the underlined portion of the text? most discarded fishing nets today wind up in the ocean or are, at best, sent to landfills. to address this problem, materials engineer hana mori and colleagues designed a process that breaks down nylon fishing nets and reshapes them into pellets. the pellets can then be used to make thermoplastic ( a polymer that softens when heated and can be molded ). with this promising new method, abandoned fishing nets could be turned into a range of useful plastic products.

preview: The following text is adapted from a 2018 novel. In the village where Elena lives, there is an old lighthouse on the cape. Children in the…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from a 2018 novel. in the village where elena lives, there is an old lighthouse on the cape. children in the village say the lighthouse is haunted. for as long as she could remember, elena had begged mamá [her mother] to take her to see the lighthouse and the rocky cape up close. she'd be a hero among her friends if she was the first girl to step inside its haunted door! just because mamá didn't believe in spirits didn't mean they weren't there. maybe this autumn mamá would finally take her. she was almost thirteen.

preview: The following text is adapted from a 1908 novel. The narrator is a young college student writing letters describing her weekly experiences.…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from a 1908 novel. the narrator is a young college student writing letters describing her weekly experiences. [the college is] putting together the freshman swim team and there's just a chance that i shall make it. i'm small, of course, but i'm strong and quick in the water. while the others are still finding their breath on the wall, i can push off and reach the far end before they're halfway there.

preview: Historians Carla Ruiz and David Tang have both documented the assistance Quaker families gave to fugitives from slavery in the years before…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence? historians carla ruiz and david tang have both documented the assistance quaker families gave to fugitives from slavery in the years before the us civil war. much of the historical evidence of this help comes from family letters preserved by quaker meetinghouses and from narratives published by the fugitives themselves. one such narrative is henry bibb's 1849 autobiography, which describes how a quaker family near cincinnati provided him with food, shelter, and clothing during his journey north.

preview: Archaeologists studying the ancient city of Herculaneum in Italy recently uncovered a well-preserved bakery. The site contains stone ovens,…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? archaeologists studying the ancient city of herculaneum in italy recently uncovered a well-preserved bakery. the site contains stone ovens, milling tools, and charred loaves. these items give researchers a clearer picture of what daily food production in herculaneum may have looked like. for example, the archaeologists found a stone mill that they believe was used to grind grain for the bakery's breads.

preview: The following text is adapted from a 1905 short story. The narrator and his sister have recently moved to a small New England town, and Wal…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from a 1905 short story. the narrator and his sister have recently moved to a small new england town, and walter is their gardener. walter [was] very useful when we settled into our new home. he had a thorough knowledge of the area, was familiar with the streams and stone walls, knew the qualities of the various soils and what would grow best in them, and where the best produce stands and woodlots were to be found. he was a marvelous hand at managing chickens and bees.

preview: Composer Margaret Bonds won first prize for her song cycle Three Dream Portraits at the 1944 Wanamaker Foundation Awards. The work was perf…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? composer margaret bonds won first prize for her song cycle three dream portraits at the 1944 wanamaker foundation awards. the work was performed the following year by the robert shaw chorale, a significant recognition of its quality. bonds continued to compose many pieces throughout her career, blending traditional spirituals and the rhythms of african american song with european classical forms. in recent years, bonds's choral works and art songs have been performed and recorded by several major ensembles, further preserving her music for others to enjoy.

preview: The following text is adapted from a 1908 novel. Hannah, a teenager, is visiting her cousin Beatrice in the city. Beatrice's neighbors did…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from a 1908 novel. hannah, a teenager, is visiting her cousin beatrice in the city. beatrice's neighbors did not interest hannah much; she was rather wary of them [because] they seemed so much more polished and worldly than herself, even those younger in years. they talked about things of which she knew little, and when beatrice tried to explain, she didn't find them interesting; indeed, some of them rather alarmed and puzzled her.

preview: The following text is from a 2010 memoir. In the text, the author reflects on her childhood home. In Vermont we heated our houses by feedin…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? the following text is from a 2010 memoir. in the text, the author reflects on her childhood home. in vermont we heated our houses by feeding the woodstove. we fed the stove even on cool autumn mornings, because the mist crept in through every window. the fire said we were home, we had closed the door against the world, we were warm against the long night.

preview: Most everyday batteries lose their charge within hours or days when in use, as their internal chemicals are consumed during discharge. But…
searchText: which choice best states the purpose of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? most everyday batteries lose their charge within hours or days when in use, as their internal chemicals are consumed during discharge. but chemist linda nazar and her team developed a new lithium-sulfur battery formulation that can endure many more charge cycles than typical batteries. for example, lithium polysulfides tend to dissolve and migrate within the cell, so a battery whose cathode contains a porous host material can trap these dissolved compounds and thereby prevent the capacity loss that usually follows.

preview: The following text is adapted from Charlotte Brontë's 1849 novel Shirley . Caroline is visiting her cousin Robert, whom she has not seen fo…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from charlotte brontë's 1849 novel shirley . caroline is visiting her cousin robert, whom she has not seen for some time. the conversation began warmly. caroline, perceiving that robert seemed tired from the day's work at the mill, offered at once to delay her questions until the morning and to read quietly by the fire instead. this unselfish willingness to set aside her own curiosity moved robert deeply; he warmly thanked her, and within minutes the cousins had passed from polite reserve into the easy familiarity of children who had once played together.

preview: The following text is adapted from Oscar Wilde's 1895 play The Importance of Being Earnest . Lady Bracknell has just arrived at her sister'…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from oscar wilde's 1895 play the importance of being earnest . lady bracknell has just arrived at her sister's home, where guests are expected. lady bracknell: really, augusta, i cannot understand it. whenever you invite algernon, half my acquaintances refuse to come. augusta: nonsense! algernon is charming; people adore him. lady bracknell: adore him! what about the trouble he caused at the duchess's last week? really, augusta, you must not let him stay.

preview: Animals and humans usually move pollen between flowers, but there is another way for pollen to travel. Botanists call this process "anemoph…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? animals and humans usually move pollen between flowers, but there is another way for pollen to travel. botanists call this process "anemophily." in anemophily, wind currents carry pollen grains over potentially great distances without the help of any animal pollinator. ecologist hiroshi tanaka and his team studied pollen captured by sensors on remote islands to find out whether anemophily could move pollen across the open ocean. they discovered that some of the pollen matched grasses from a continent more than a thousand kilometers away.

preview: The following text is from Marion Hayworth's 1921 poem “After the Storm.” In the poem, the speaker stands by a window at dawn. A trembling…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? the following text is from marion hayworth's 1921 poem “after the storm.” in the poem, the speaker stands by a window at dawn. a trembling drop slips from the broken bough — so falls my courage now, so leaves my hope. the grey rain has gone, yet within me it pours. i am hollowed by its weight. i am emptied like the shaken, dripping bough.

preview: Today choreographer Pearl Primus is mainly remembered for her bold solo dances “Strange Fruit” and “Hard Time Blues.” However, by overlooki…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? today choreographer pearl primus is mainly remembered for her bold solo dances “strange fruit” and “hard time blues.” however, by overlooking her lesser-known group works, audiences will miss the full scope of primus's vision. for instance, her ensemble piece “fanga” and her staged suite african ceremonial richly weave west african dance traditions with american modern technique. these compositions deserve as much recognition as primus's most famous solos.

preview: The following text is from Elena Carrasco's 1888 poem “To My Detractors.” THEY murmur that I scorn the customs of my fathers; But in truth…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? the following text is from elena carrasco's 1888 poem “to my detractors.” they murmur that i scorn the customs of my fathers; but in truth i neither honor nor despise such customs (for what have i to do with them?—or what with their unmaking?), only i will plant in every plaza of these mountain towns, on the high pastures and along the rivers, and in the markets, and above every cart laden with grain that creaks the road, without altars, or codes, or wardens, or any decree, the custom of free fellowship among the people.

preview: Why do mountain bluebirds hover before striking at prey but eastern bluebirds dart from a perch? Researchers suggest that this difference b…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? why do mountain bluebirds hover before striking at prey but eastern bluebirds dart from a perch? researchers suggest that this difference between the two songbird species may be partly due to a small muscle group in the chest called the supracoracoideus. mountain bluebirds, which often forage over open meadows, have a relatively large supracoracoideus that powers the rapid upstroke needed for hovering. by contrast, eastern bluebirds, which usually hunt from low branches, have a smaller version of this muscle group and rely instead on quick lateral movement. these traits allow eastern bluebirds and several other woodland species to dart efficiently between perches. the same traits may also make sustained hovering more difficult for them.

preview: The following text is from Sarah Orne Jewett's 1896 novel The Country of the Pointed Firs . Mira, a young visitor, has come to spend the su…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is from sarah orne jewett's 1896 novel the country of the pointed firs . mira, a young visitor, has come to spend the summer at a coastal house in maine with an older woman named mrs. todd. mira drank in every shade of the marshes spread before her. “oh, mrs. todd,” she cried one early morning, sweeping into the kitchen with her apron full of damp goldenrod, “i'm so glad i live where there are septembers. wouldn't it be dreadful if we leapt straight from august into october? look at these dripping stalks. don't they thrill you—over and over? i'm going to set them on every windowsill.” “weedy stuff,” said mrs. todd, whose feeling for adornment had never much developed. “you overrun the kitchen, child, with bog stuff. kitchens were meant for cooking in.”

preview: The following text is from James Weldon Johnson's 1917 poem “The Lantern.” A lantern is so slight a thing, And yet what cheer its wick can…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is from james weldon johnson's 1917 poem “the lantern.” a lantern is so slight a thing, and yet what cheer its wick can bring! in days of work it warms my breast, and when at dusk i crave for rest; when carts come home along the lane, and in the yard i hear the gate, as dusk, the keeper, tends his flame, i light my lantern, and all is right.

preview: Archaeological excavation of Sonora Town, a nineteenth-century Mexican American neighborhood in Los Angeles, California, provided the first…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? archaeological excavation of sonora town, a nineteenth-century mexican american neighborhood in los angeles, california, provided the first physical evidence that mexican ceramic goods were imported to the united states in the mid-1800s: shards from a glazed earthenware jar style native to jalisco. local casas comisionistas —san diego–based commission-merchant houses—likely coordinated the jars' shipment from kilns in central mexico, through pacific coastal ports, to inland california markets. this route reveals the (often overlooked) cross-border dimensions of the supply chains linking mexican migrant communities.

preview: Using the European Southern Observatory's powerful Very Large Telescope (VLT), Adriana Marcos and colleagues measured the spectra of light…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? using the european southern observatory's powerful very large telescope (vlt), adriana marcos and colleagues measured the spectra of light reflected from the surface of polyhymnia, an unusual asteroid in the main belt. different elements reflect different wavelengths of light, and the spectral measurements revealed unexpectedly heavy elements consistent with a dense metallic composition in polyhymnia's interior. this finding not only offers the first direct spectral evidence of such material on a main-belt asteroid but also illustrates how the vlt could enable a new wave of compositional surveys of small bodies in the solar system.

preview: A study by Dr. Linnea Forsberg and colleagues concluded that elementary school students retain information from a lesson more accurately wh…
searchText: which choice best describes the main purpose of the text? a study by dr. linnea forsberg and colleagues concluded that elementary school students retain information from a lesson more accurately when they take a short walk outdoors before the lesson begins. study participants were divided into two groups. the experimental group spent ten minutes walking through a tree-lined school courtyard before listening to a fifteen-minute lesson on basic geology. the control group spent ten minutes sitting quietly indoors before listening to the same lesson. forsberg and colleagues found that the experimental group answered significantly more questions correctly on a follow-up quiz than the control group did.

preview: The following text is adapted from Sui Sin Far's 1912 short story “In the Land of the Free.” Sammy and his mother have recently arrived at…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from sui sin far's 1912 short story “in the land of the free.” sammy and his mother have recently arrived at a busy port in san francisco. [sammy] was dizzy with delight and with a hunger to be part of all that the great harbor meant. in the afternoon he watched the dockworkers passing by carrying their bright bundles, and he wondered with a kind of envy what bright thing each one might be carrying home. in the village there had been little worth seeing on the road, except the temple steps and one or two neighbors' doors.

preview: In the early 1900s, Yiddish-language theaters thrived in cities across the northeastern United States. Philadelphia alone supported seven m…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? in the early 1900s, yiddish-language theaters thrived in cities across the northeastern united states. philadelphia alone supported seven major yiddish theaters between 1905 and 1915. but new york city eclipsed every other city in the region. this city supported more than twenty yiddish theaters during that period. new york city was home to the country's largest population of yiddish speakers and a dense network of jewish cultural organizations. thus, it is unsurprising that this city became such a vibrant center for yiddish drama.

preview: A team of researchers found that naked mole-rats can detect when an injured colony member's wound is becoming infected and then treat that…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? a team of researchers found that naked mole-rats can detect when an injured colony member's wound is becoming infected and then treat that infection by applying a thin layer of saliva containing antimicrobial compounds the rodents produce in their salivary glands. the team reported that the mortality rate for naked mole-rats with infected injuries dropped by roughly 75 percent with this treatment, and the researchers are hopeful that this discovery could guide the development of new antimicrobial agents for use in human medicine.

preview: Generally it takes blackpoll warblers about three days to complete their fall departure from the Atlantic coast of North America. From thei…
searchText: which choice best states the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? generally it takes blackpoll warblers about three days to complete their fall departure from the atlantic coast of north america. from their staging grounds in coastal new england, the birds begin by flying out over the open atlantic, keeping roughly 200 miles east of the carolina shore . they climb to high altitudes to pick up favorable tailwinds, then turn southwest toward hispaniola, before finally arriving at their wintering range in northern south america. in 2018, however, it took the warblers nearly twice as long to make the journey from new england to south america. according to researchers, the reason was a series of unusually persistent headwinds.

preview: Vocal mirroring occurs when one individual matches the pitch or rhythm of another individual's vocalization shortly after hearing it. Studi…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the first sentence in the text as a whole? vocal mirroring occurs when one individual matches the pitch or rhythm of another individual's vocalization shortly after hearing it. studies of this behavior in cetaceans have focused mostly on bottlenose dolphins in captivity, but marine biologist hiroko tanaka and her colleagues have shown that it can also occur among wild populations. in their study, which focused on a wild pod of short-finned pilot whales ( globicephala macrorhynchus ) off the coast of madeira, the researchers further reported that vocal mirroring most commonly occurred among adolescent males and between members of different family groups rather than within a single family group.

preview: The following text is from Mary Elizabeth Braddon's 1862 novel Lady Audley's Secret . The visitor still stood by the parlour window, where…
searchText: which choice best states the function of the underlined sentence in the overall structure of the text? the following text is from mary elizabeth braddon's 1862 novel lady audley's secret . the visitor still stood by the parlour window, where he had first taken his post. his oddness compelled a more careful inspection. a small, restless figure. hair gingery and close-cropped, pressed flat above his temples. his hooded sockets of eyes were rimmed by a pale, smudged shadow, and gleamed with a guarded sort of curiosity: the watch without the look. the whole man was dusted with road grit. he stood in a faint cloud on the worn carpet: his curious silver-tipped cane upright at his side.

preview: On composer Florence Beatrice Price's move from Chicago to Saint Louis in 1931, her musical style underwent an abrupt shift. Setting aside…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? on composer florence beatrice price's move from chicago to saint louis in 1931, her musical style underwent an abrupt shift. setting aside dense, late-romantic orchestral writing—a style that often involves richly chromatic harmonies and tightly interwoven counterpoint to express the composer's sense of inherited european tradition—price began crafting concert works rooted in black american spirituals in a strikingly new way. drawing on the rhythmic patterns of juba dance and the modal melodies of southern hymns, these works feature lean orchestrations and a deliberately limited harmonic palette that highlight a single, singing voice within the ensemble.

preview: The geomagnetic equator—the line along which Earth's magnetic field lines run horizontally relative to the surface—is detectable only with…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? the geomagnetic equator—the line along which earth's magnetic field lines run horizontally relative to the surface—is detectable only with specialized instruments. inspired by the orientation strategies of certain sea turtles and salmon, researchers devised a method for tracing the geomagnetic equator at sea using polarized starlight, which becomes faintly aligned as it passes through the upper atmosphere. a wide-aperture star tracker captures images of polarization patterns, which shift as earth rotates through the night; temporal variations across images can then be used to determine a navigator's magnetic latitude and bearing relative to the geomagnetic equator.

preview: When ancient cedar beams were uncovered during waterfront repairs in Alexandria, Egypt, Yasmin Rashid and her team analyzed the isotopic si…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? when ancient cedar beams were uncovered during waterfront repairs in alexandria, egypt, yasmin rashid and her team analyzed the isotopic signatures in the wood to determine where the beams had originated. by comparing the isotopes in the beams to records of cedars from across the eastern mediterranean, the team traced the wood to the bekaa valley in present-day lebanon, hundreds of kilometers from alexandria. because cedar of that quality could only have been moved from the inland bekaa valley to alexandria by ship, the team's findings hint at the reach of ancient mediterranean trade networks.

preview: Urban infrastructure is often assumed to be hostile to wildlife, but that isn't always the case. Consider the peregrine falcon ( Falco pere…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? urban infrastructure is often assumed to be hostile to wildlife, but that isn't always the case. consider the peregrine falcon ( falco peregrinus ) : as old-growth cliffs have become scarce along the eastern seaboard of north america, many of these falcons have abandoned remote cliff habitats and are now nesting on tall office towers and bridge piers. in a survey of multiple downtown high-rises and patches of preserved coastal cliff, an ornithologist found peregrine falcons on 96% of the high-rises but only 41% of the cliff patches. moreover, broods raised on the high-rises were on average larger than those raised on the cliffs.

preview: Very little is known about the role nocturnal mammals, such as bats, play in cacao tree pollination because it is difficult to track them i…
searchText: which choice best states the function of the underlined sentence? very little is known about the role nocturnal mammals, such as bats, play in cacao tree pollination because it is difficult to track them in dense canopy at night. to address this problem, a team of biologists installed infrared motion-triggered cameras to record visits to cacao flowers throughout the day and night. the footage showed that while most pollinator visits were made by midges, nearly a quarter of visits were made by small fruit bats. additionally, flowers visited by both bats and midges set more fruit than flowers visited only by midges.

preview: Works of political theory, such as Hobbes's Leviathan or Locke's Second Treatise of Government , are partly concerned with how a legitimate…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? works of political theory, such as hobbes's leviathan or locke's second treatise of government , are partly concerned with how a legitimate state should be organized. but theorist mira caldwell argues that works that prescribe a structure of legitimate government without also setting out the obligations of those who would inhabit it are inherently useful only to readers who already share the author's political ideals—readers indifferent to those ideals will see no reason to follow the proposed rules. however, some works of political theory attempt to describe what constitutes a legitimate state while also offering reasons that citizens, on their own terms, would have to support it.

preview: The following text is adapted from Arnold Bennett's 1908 novel The Old Wives' Tale . Bursley is a fictional town in England. [Bursley] cont…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from arnold bennett's 1908 novel the old wives' tale . bursley is a fictional town in england. [bursley] contained several broad lanes all very like one another, and many narrow lanes still more like one another, lined by houses equally like one another, whose occupants all rose and slept at the same hours, with the same step upon the same cobblestones, to perform the same labour, and for whom every day was the twin of yesterday and tomorrow, and every season the duplicate of the last and the next.

preview: For her 1979 album Mirror Garden , vocalist Linda Sharrock wrote songs grounded in traditional blues and gospel, then layered them with the…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? for her 1979 album mirror garden , vocalist linda sharrock wrote songs grounded in traditional blues and gospel, then layered them with then-unusual tape loops, prepared piano, and free-jazz horn arrangements. the result was so unfamiliar, so out of step with the era's mainstream, that the album attracted little attention when first released. in recent years, however, a younger generation of singers has embraced the genre-blending experimentation of mirror garden . avant-soul vocalists yves tumor and serpentwithfeet, among other contemporary artists, point to the album as a touchstone.

preview: The following text is from Frances Harper's 1872 poem “On the Cliffs.” Oh, deep delight to watch the gladsome winds Exultant rush among the…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? the following text is from frances harper's 1872 poem “on the cliffs.” oh, deep delight to watch the gladsome winds exultant rush among the bending pines; ever turned aside, yet ever pressing on— filled with a force that will not be denied; to see the brilliant hues of dawn and sea. the far white sails, glide spirit like, into a wider world, to feel the sweet enchantment of the height steal through the soul, clearing the burdened brain, making the heart leap joyous as the bright, ascending winds!

preview: The following text is from Zora Neale Hurston's 1948 novel Seraph on the Suwanee . Lila, a young girl, often visits the music room of a nei…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is from zora neale hurston's 1948 novel seraph on the suwanee . lila, a young girl, often visits the music room of a neighbor's house. lila thought that all the music in the world lived inside that piano bench and she had a plan about playing every piece in the world. she was working through the music a piece a day in the order the bench held it, and not skipping the dull ones. she remembered that the first piece had been an andante. she had been playing one piece a day for many months now and she was still in the b pieces. already she had practiced about bourrees and berceuses, barcaroles and bagatelles. for all her eagerness, she had to admit that some of the b pieces had been tough sledding. but lila was a player.

preview: In 1822, French scholar Jean-François Champollion completed an extensive notebook of hieroglyphic signs drawn from inscriptions in temples…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? in 1822, french scholar jean-françois champollion completed an extensive notebook of hieroglyphic signs drawn from inscriptions in temples and tombs along the nile. this volume, which he called the tableau des hiéroglyphes , was compiled at the request of the duc de blacas, who supported champollion’s research. to assemble the notebook, champollion copied carvings from monuments brought to paris and corresponded with travelers who had visited egypt firsthand, recording their descriptions of the inscriptions they had seen. he arranged these sign drawings alongside short explanatory notes on each entry.

preview: Filmmaker Agnès Varda, who was also a still photographer, drew on photographic techniques in her films to underscore themes she returned to…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? filmmaker agnès varda, who was also a still photographer, drew on photographic techniques in her films to underscore themes she returned to throughout her career. in her 2000 documentary the gleaners and i , varda repeatedly inserted long, lingering close-ups that recall her early portrait photography of working-class subjects. these quiet framings call attention to the film’s central concern with people who have been overlooked—a concern that varda also explored in her later video installations.

preview: Parts of the Namib Desert in southern Africa support unexpectedly diverse plant communities despite extremely scarce rainfall. Coastal fog…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? parts of the namib desert in southern africa support unexpectedly diverse plant communities despite extremely scarce rainfall. coastal fog provides moisture that established plants can absorb, but the sand surface is often packed so tightly that few seeds can take root. termites that build mounds in this terrain appear to play a helpful role: they loosen the surrounding soil and pull organic matter from deeper layers up to the surface, where seeds can settle and germinate . in a 2018 study, ecologist norbert jürgens found that the soil immediately around termite mounds contained roughly twice the density of seedlings as nearby undisturbed soil.

preview: The following text is adapted from Sara Teasdale’s 1920 poem “The Storm.” The Wind comes striding down the hill In boots of leather and a c…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? the following text is adapted from sara teasdale’s 1920 poem “the storm.” the wind comes striding down the hill in boots of leather and a cloak of grey, and shakes the trees with restless hand as though impatient with the day. he stoops beside the silver pool to wash the dust of distance from his brow, and lifts his voice to call the rain like one who summons servants now.

preview: The following text is from Mary E. Wilkins Freeman’s 1891 short story “A New England Nun.” Louisa Ellis has been keeping house alone for ma…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is from mary e. wilkins freeman’s 1891 short story “a new england nun.” louisa ellis has been keeping house alone for many years. louisa stood at the front window, her white apron unstained, her hands folded gently before her. the supper-table was laid in the kitchen with its single china cup, the bread freshly cut, the preserves in their old glass dish. out in the lane the boy who brought the mail was whistling on his slow way home, and the bees still hummed in the lilac by the gate. down the dusty road, a buggy at last appeared in the distance, raising a thin gold cloud against the evening sun.

preview: The following text is adapted from Edith Wharton’s 1916 short story “Bunner Sisters.” An elderly bookseller is studying a clock that he has…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from edith wharton’s 1916 short story “bunner sisters.” an elderly bookseller is studying a clock that he has lately taken in trade. it seemed plain enough that the little clock did not belong among his books. a talkative stranger who claimed he was clearing out his attic had let the bookseller have it for what he called a trifle. the shop was only a narrow basement room which lived chiefly on the sale of cheap reprints. the old man cast his eye over his rows of leather spines, his shelves of yellowed almanacs, his stacks of forgotten sermons. “hasn’t any place here,” he grumbled. and yet the old man was quietly pleased with his purchase. there was a half-hidden satisfaction in his frown as he shuffled about deciding on the least foolish shelf for the clock.

preview: The following text is from the 1921 poem “A Heron at Dawn” by Jessie Redmon Fauset, a Black American writer. I have just seen a quiet thing…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? the following text is from the 1921 poem “a heron at dawn” by jessie redmon fauset, a black american writer. i have just seen a quiet thing, long and slim, against a pale, pale shore, a solitary heron, watchful, patient, a grey question mark bent on the water. why, slender silent question, are you grey? and what do you ask of the morning tide?

preview: By drawing on both flamenco and Andean folk traditions, Peruvian guitarist and composer Renata Flores Quispe creates pieces that reflect th…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? by drawing on both flamenco and andean folk traditions, peruvian guitarist and composer renata flores quispe creates pieces that reflect the layered cultural history of the andes. for her album caminos tejidos , flores quispe wrote new compositions in the style of traditional huayno songs that were accompanied by flamenco-influenced guitar arrangements played by a small ensemble. but where a flamenco guitarist would normally lead the rhythm, the musicians on caminos tejidos follow the pulse of a traditional andean drum.

preview: Researchers have long hypothesized that the moai statues of Rapa Nui were transported across the island on wooden rollers and sledges by la…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? researchers have long hypothesized that the moai statues of rapa nui were transported across the island on wooden rollers and sledges by large work crews. one archaeologist set out to test this hypothesis. using ropes attached to a precise replica of a moai, his team attempted to walk the upright statue forward by tilting it from side to side along a prepared dirt path. the replica advanced steadily over hundreds of meters without rollers or sledges. this led the archaeologist to conclude that hauling statues over wood was likely not the principal method of transport.

preview: During the Bracero Program of 1942–1964, which brought millions of Mexican workers to the United States on temporary agricultural contracts…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? during the bracero program of 1942–1964, which brought millions of mexican workers to the united states on temporary agricultural contracts, labor organizer and el malcriado writer ernesto galarza was instrumental in helping returning braceros establish themselves once their contracts ended. many men hoped for steadier work back home because, on the u.s. side of the border, they faced exploitative wages and growers often dismissed them at the end of the harvest. to aid with this transition, galarza connected returning workers with cooperatives and legal aid groups before their journeys south of the border had even begun.

preview: Wilma Mankiller, a historian and citizen of the Cherokee Nation, has identified a difficulty inherent to research on the political role of…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? wilma mankiller, a historian and citizen of the cherokee nation, has identified a difficulty inherent to research on the political role of cherokee women in the 1700s and 1800s: the surviving primary sources from that era, treaty records and accounts by male u.s. agents, understate the influence cherokee women held in clan councils and land decisions. mankiller argues that cherokee oral histories and recent ethnographic fieldwork in the tribe’s eastern communities supplement the written record by recovering crucial evidence of those decision-making roles.

preview: The following text is adapted from Anzia Yezierska’s 1925 novel Bread Givers . As a young woman, the narrator emigrated from Poland to New…
searchText: which choice best describes the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from anzia yezierska’s 1925 novel bread givers . as a young woman, the narrator emigrated from poland to new york. the ways of my old country and of my new one were so unlike in many things, and my feeling for each was so deeply my own, that at times i had the strange sensation of standing on a high bridge in midair, looking down with measuring eyes upon two different worlds. at first i was always trying to fit, by the rules i had grown up with, the unfamiliar happenings that pressed on me every day; for no one around me seemed to know why the customs of the new place were as they were, or where they had come from.

preview: The following text is from the 1922 poem “Harvest Round” by Lola Ridge, an Irish-American poet. There shall be other harvests rising, A new…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is from the 1922 poem “harvest round” by lola ridge, an irish-american poet. there shall be other harvests rising, a new turning of the wheel— our hands shall have a different gleaning, pale fields renew their grain— but under the new moon’s meaning, repeating what dusk began, there’ll be the same shoulders aching, the same tired hands shall reel— when this dusk has done its breaking and dawn shall come again.....

preview: The following text is from E. M. Forster’s 1910 novel Howards End . The text describes a literary club where members deliver short essays i…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portions in the text as a whole? the following text is from e. m. forster’s 1910 novel howards end . the text describes a literary club where members deliver short essays in turn. member by member they rose, and, as though with a dull pencil, tried to trace the figure of their thought upon the air a shade more sharply, and sat down with the feeling that, by some cause they could not lay hold of, their lines had blurred . as they sat down, they turned without fail to the person beside them and continued, in a whisper, what they had just attempted aloud.

preview: The following text is from the 1898 poem “Lowlands at Evening” by Sarah Piatt, an American writer. Beside the slow brook in the failing lig…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? the following text is from the 1898 poem “lowlands at evening” by sarah piatt, an american writer. beside the slow brook in the failing light, the frog repeats his slow refrain at night. the marsh hen, settling, finds a sheltered glade, where cat-tails sway, and dim green mosses fade. last terns with idle wing and heavy flight drift through the silence into deeper night. and like a shadow, dragged by some soft wind, steals dusk along the reed-bed’s thinning rind. still lie the rushes, and the mists arise, dim, cool and damp, while all the lowland sighs.

preview: The Tirumalai Nayak murals, from seventeenth-century South India, depict more than 60 court scenes across the long inner walls of a palace…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? the tirumalai nayak murals, from seventeenth-century south india, depict more than 60 court scenes across the long inner walls of a palace audience hall. they were likely produced by teams of painters working in vertical sections and then aligning the resulting panels along the wall. it’s plausible that the workshop had not previously executed murals on so long a wall, and some art historians claim that a close examination of the seams—the places where the panels meet—suggests that the painters refined their alignment process over the course of production. for instance, the earliest seam completed shows a clear mismatch of the painted border between the two panels, whereas the later seams are nearly imperceptible.

preview: Antarctica’s McMurdo Dry Valleys are one of the coldest, driest places on Earth. Jill Mikucki and other astrobiologists search for life, or…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? antarctica’s mcmurdo dry valleys are one of the coldest, driest places on earth. jill mikucki and other astrobiologists search for life, or its remains, in this harsh place because the valleys closely mirror the icy subsurface environment thought to lie beneath the moon europa. the microbes living below the salty waters of the valleys may offer clues about possible europan life. by studying how these organisms survive such extreme conditions on earth, mikucki’s team hopes to assess whether similar life might exist beneath europa’s ice and to design the best tools to look for evidence of it.

preview: The following text is from Sandra Cisneros’s 1991 autobiographical short fiction collection Woman Hollering Creek . The narrator is a young…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? the following text is from sandra cisneros’s 1991 autobiographical short fiction collection woman hollering creek . the narrator is a young girl living near san antonio. i rode the bus. my grandmother had let me ride alone once i could recite our home address without missing. i rode and learned the streets. i drew a map in my head and placed myself on it. at night under my blanket i quizzed myself on the city’s plan and set tasks: reach the library transferring only buses that pass the river. picture a route from the school to my cousin’s house. ©1991 by sandra cisneros

preview: The following text is adapted from Eliza Haywood’s 1725 novel The Fatal Secret . Anziana and Bellario are neighbors who have been secretly…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? the following text is adapted from eliza haywood’s 1725 novel the fatal secret . anziana and bellario are neighbors who have been secretly exchanging notes through lisetta, anziana’s maid. [anziana] gave the small folded paper to lisetta; who slipped at once into the orchard with it, where she still found bellario beneath the laurel, gazing at the gravel in deep thought. she held out the note, but feared to drop it through the close-laid hedge; so he ran and brought a slim reed, which he split at one end, and held it while she fixed the paper into the cleft, and would not stay to hear his words to it. but never was a youth so transported with joy as he was at the reading of this short note; it inflamed his hopes anew; for to the generous, nothing kindles affection like affection.

preview: The following text is adapted from Tommy Orange’s 2018 novel There There . Edwin Black is collecting interview pledges for an oral-history…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from tommy orange’s 2018 novel there there . edwin black is collecting interview pledges for an oral-history project from fellow members of his urban native community in oakland. edwin black tended the clipboard like a garden. he kept it with him at all times. around the neighborhood, his eyes brightened when he spotted a community member who hadn’t yet signed up. wherever they were—at the laundromat, the corner store, at the powwow committee meeting, on the sidewalk, or outside the rec center—edwin cornered them. if they were holding a sleeping baby, he’d have them sign up. if they were laughing, if they were arguing. if they were walking a child home from after-school, they signed up. ©2018 by tommy orange

preview: The following text is adapted from Anthony Trollope’s 1855 novel The Warden . Mr. Crowther is an attorney in the town of Barchester. By the…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? the following text is adapted from anthony trollope’s 1855 novel the warden . mr. crowther is an attorney in the town of barchester. by the townsfolk of barchester and its precincts, [mr. crowther] was esteemed as a man of quite singular acuteness and judgment, who must produce a notable impression in the chambers and committee-rooms of london upon his occasional visits to the capital; and by his fellow attorneys he was regarded as an obliging and unobtrusive colleague. mr. crowther never permitted himself any sharp dispute; he proposed what might be considered, but seldom uttered what he himself considered; he never let either gentleman or lady perceive that he was amused at their expense, and he never afforded anyone an occasion to be amused at his .

preview: The following text is from Henry James’s 1908 novel The Reverberator . Mr. Probert is making his way across Paris on his way to a private a…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined phrase in the text as a whole? the following text is from henry james’s 1908 novel the reverberator . mr. probert is making his way across paris on his way to a private appointment. before reaching the quai d’orsay, mr. probert turned aside out of the crowded boulevard, busy with the clatter of fiacres and the shuffle of porters, into the almost silent, swift current of black coupés. under his hat, set at a slight forward angle, his hair had been combed into respectable smoothness; for his business was with a legation. and mr. probert, firm as a column— a slightly pliant kind of column —advanced now along a street that might in every propriety be called retired.

preview: The people of Edo-period Japan have sometimes been seen as indifferent to popular reading and the spread of new ideas, but recent research…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion? the people of edo-period japan have sometimes been seen as indifferent to popular reading and the spread of new ideas, but recent research has shown that this is largely a misconception. according to historian mary elizabeth berry, most edo-period cities had at least one circulating lending library, which often offered both expensive bound volumes and—more affordably—loose printed sheets. while such services were available mainly to city residents , regular borrowing of books from itinerant peddlers or sharing of pamphlets among neighbors was common practice throughout rural japan.

preview: Lateral antibiotic-resistance gene transfer occurs when a bacterium of one strain acquires genetic material from a bacterium of another str…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? lateral antibiotic-resistance gene transfer occurs when a bacterium of one strain acquires genetic material from a bacterium of another strain through nonreproductive means. the genetic material can then be passed along “vertically” in the second strain—that is, through ordinary cell division. microbiologist lakshmi chandran and her team have hypothesized infection by bacteriophages as a mechanism of resistance-gene transfer between bacterial strains found in soil: while reproducing in a host cell, a phage could pick up a resistance gene from one strain, then move on to a cell of a different strain and deposit the gene into it in turn.

preview: The following text is from Ina Coolbrith’s 1895 poem “The Mountain’s Word.” No matter how we terrace up the slope, Hedging the meadow and t…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? the following text is from ina coolbrith’s 1895 poem “the mountain’s word.” no matter how we terrace up the slope, hedging the meadow and the orchard close; no matter how we channel down the rain, bidding the dry hill flower at our hand, we must always leave the shoulders of the peak; the unmeasured reaches of the windy snow-swept beaches, the lonely upper margin of the peak.

preview: In her 1934 treatise Composition , dance pioneer Doris Humphrey insisted that modern choreography should arise from the natural arc of fall…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? in her 1934 treatise composition , dance pioneer doris humphrey insisted that modern choreography should arise from the natural arc of fall and recovery rather than from imitations of classical ballet, since the body, she argued, gains expressive power from yielding to and resisting gravity. the clearest demonstration of this principle is her 1936 work with my red fires , in which dancers repeatedly collapse toward the floor only to rise again in surging diagonal lines. critics have long observed that the piece’s rhythmic alternation of descent and ascent translates humphrey’s theoretical writings directly into staged movement.

preview: Most paleontologists have long held that the dramatic body plans of the Cambrian Period emerged only after oxygen levels in Earth’s oceans…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? most paleontologists have long held that the dramatic body plans of the cambrian period emerged only after oxygen levels in earth’s oceans rose above a critical threshold roughly 540 million years ago. however, fresh analyses of trace fossils preserved in shale beds along the coast of newfoundland have complicated this account. using high-resolution geochemical assays of iron-bound sediments, a team led by paleontologist naila reyes argued that complex burrowing animals were already active in seafloor mud some 30 million years earlier, well before the oxygen rise that conventional models had treated as a prerequisite.

preview: In 1968, weaver Kahlil Robles began hosting Saturday workshops in his garage in San Antonio for fellow Tejano fiber artists who, like him,…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? in 1968, weaver kahlil robles began hosting saturday workshops in his garage in san antonio for fellow tejano fiber artists who, like him, wanted a place to share traditional rio grande techniques alongside experimental designs. demand grew quickly, and within two years robles had moved the workshops into a rented storefront on the city’s west side, where the gatherings became known as the casa de telar. relocating to a permanent studio in 1979, the casa de telar broadened its work beyond weaving to include natural-dye instruction and exhibitions of tejano textile art. more than five decades later, it continues to train new generations of weavers across south texas.

preview: The following text is adapted from Elizabeth Gaskell's 1855 novel North and South . Margaret, the narrator, has recently moved to the manuf…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from elizabeth gaskell's 1855 novel north and south . margaret, the narrator, has recently moved to the manufacturing town of milton. i went about my appointed duties in the parlour with composure enough; and yet every now and then half-formed plans for some other manner of life would steal across my mind unbidden, and i caught myself shaping out, in spite of better resolutions, what i might say in a letter of inquiry to some distant household, and turning over how a change of place might suit me. i let the thoughts come and go; perhaps they would take root and bear something, perhaps they would wither, but i would not stop them yet.

preview: The following text is adapted from an early nineteenth-century novel. Edmund, the speaker, is trying to persuade his cousin to take part in…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from an early nineteenth-century novel. edmund, the speaker, is trying to persuade his cousin to take part in an informal musical evening at home. it is to be the merest trifle, only ourselves and two or three cousins; no strangers, no audience to dread. we can be relied upon, i think, to choose airs perfectly genteel; and i cannot imagine that anyone could come to harm by spending an hour at the pianoforte with a few familiar pieces, when we should very willingly devote the same hour to far less profitable amusement.

preview: In Edo-period Japan, prior to the mid-nineteenth century, lending libraries were operated mostly by traveling booksellers who carried bundl…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? in edo-period japan, prior to the mid-nineteenth century, lending libraries were operated mostly by traveling booksellers who carried bundles of titles from house to house and charged subscribers per volume. beginning in the 1870s, however, a wave of state-sponsored public reading rooms opened in major cities, intended to spread literacy and civic knowledge. as modern scholar aiko tanizaki points out, the two systems are less opposed than they appear, since both the traveling booksellers and the new public reading rooms operated, in essence, as instruments of cultivation, differing chiefly in whom each was cultivating and on what scale.

preview: The following text is from a nineteenth-century stage tragedy. Alceo has lived for many years in the court of King Renaut, far from his nat…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion (" it may be...absent subject ") in the text as a whole? the following text is from a nineteenth-century stage tragedy. alceo has lived for many years in the court of king renaut, far from his native city of lirona, and has asked the king for leave to return there. renaut: i pray thee, dear alceo, urge me no further. to deny thee is a wound; to grant thee, ruin. alceo: it is twelve summers since i saw lirona. though through all that time i have served thee well, and though, it may be, my old prince retains some kindly memory of his absent subject, still i cannot tell whether my returning would prove a comfort to him or no thing at all.

preview: Histories of the Asian American movement—a coalition that pressed for political and cultural recognition and that crested in the late 1960s…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? histories of the asian american movement—a coalition that pressed for political and cultural recognition and that crested in the late 1960s and 1970s— tend to spotlight the most confrontational student organizers in major coastal cities , leaving the impression of a movement uniformly radical. sociologist mai-linh tran has argued, however, that if attention is shifted to less-publicized initiatives in suburban community centers, mutual-aid clinics, and language schools, one finds participants espousing a wide span of political stances—reformist, religious, and liberal as well as radical—within the same broad cause.

preview: Public-health historians have argued that one decisive factor in the spread of vaccination across rural India in the 1970s was the Smallpox…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? public-health historians have argued that one decisive factor in the spread of vaccination across rural india in the 1970s was the smallpox eradication program's field trainee initiative, which brought aspiring health workers to its three-week residency at the patna training center. run by veteran organizers such as indira saxena and vikram bhatt, the trainees —over 9,400 in total— completed sessions ranging from cold-chain logistics to community outreach before returning to their districts.

preview: According to the Brazilian sociologist Heloisa de Castro (1902–1981), the European frameworks underlying mid-twentieth-century urban planni…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? according to the brazilian sociologist heloisa de castro (1902–1981), the european frameworks underlying mid-twentieth-century urban planning—for instance, the assumption that residential, commercial, and industrial zones must be cleanly separated—had limited usefulness in brazilian cities. making urban planning genuinely responsive to brazilian life, de castro maintained, called for new analytic categories that reflected the dense, mixed-use neighborhoods historically rooted in the country's ports and hillsides. to put these convictions to work, she helped design a community-mapping project in central salvador that catalogued how families actually used a single block—living, selling, repairing, and worshipping—and used the resulting maps to lobby municipal authorities against a planned demolition.

preview: The following text is adapted from a 1908 novel. Constance and a friend are pausing at a lookout above a coastal town. Constance had no spe…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? the following text is adapted from a 1908 novel. constance and a friend are pausing at a lookout above a coastal town. constance had no special communion with the sea, but she had an unfailing instinct for the becoming, and could be exquisitely alive to a setting that flattered her own emotion. the bay laid out beneath her struck her as the natural amplification of her present feeling, and she lingered upon each long arc of the shoreline as if she herself had drawn it. her companion, less practiced at being seen, murmured only that the wind was sharp and that it would soon be time to descend.

preview: A study by a team led by behavioral economist Priya Subramanian suggests that high indoor humidity in the hours before a negotiation can le…
searchText: which choice best states the function of the underlined sentence in the overall structure of the text? a study by a team led by behavioral economist priya subramanian suggests that high indoor humidity in the hours before a negotiation can lead to noticeably more conciliatory offers. drawing on transaction-level data from 2007 to 2018 for a chain of mid-sized real-estate brokerages, the researchers paired more than 14,000 listed prices with the final agreed prices and matched each negotiation to weather-station humidity readings for that morning. they found that bargainers who had worked in humid conditions accepted prices, on average, three to five percent farther from their initial demand than those in drier offices.

preview: Naomi Kessler and Stephen Ortega found that elevated background noise depresses the foraging activity of the eastern red bat ( Lasiurus bor…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? naomi kessler and stephen ortega found that elevated background noise depresses the foraging activity of the eastern red bat ( lasiurus borealis ), a result they explain in cost-benefit terms: louder ambient conditions mask the echolocation cues bats rely on, so the gains from continued hunting do not outweigh the increased risk of misjudging obstacles. most other small insectivorous bats respond to ambient noise much as eastern red bats do; the silver-haired bat ( lasionycteris noctivagans ), however, has been documented foraging unabated in noisy environments. researchers tentatively trace this divergence to the silver-haired bat's preference for larger, slower prey whose detection appears to rely less on faint acoustic cues.

preview: Plant biologists Diego Salgado, Mei-Lin Chao, and Robert Tanaka grew seedlings of Brassica rapa in samples of simulated Martian regolith an…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? plant biologists diego salgado, mei-lin chao, and robert tanaka grew seedlings of brassica rapa in samples of simulated martian regolith and, as a control, in standard greenhouse loam. they found that although most seeds in both groups germinated, the regolith-grown plants developed shorter taproots, accumulated higher levels of anthocyanin pigments in their leaves, and produced fewer rosette leaves overall than the controls. the researchers interpret these symptoms—stunted roots, red coloration, and reduced leaf number—as classic stress responses, suggesting that, while germination on a mars-analog substrate is possible, sustained growth would require substantial soil amendment.

preview: The following text is adapted from a 1923 short story. Mira is a child who lives in a small village beside a tea estate. Perhaps nine-year-…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentences in the text as a whole? the following text is adapted from a 1923 short story. mira is a child who lives in a small village beside a tea estate. perhaps nine-year-old mira was perplexing to the elders in the village, for she was a dreamy child and given to inventing companies and capitals. the karuna river curled past, scarcely two hundred yards from her grandmother's gate. along its lower bank stood mossy banyans, brilliant flame-of-the-forest trees, and dense clusters of bamboo; on the upper bank, where the soil was thinner, only a sparse fringe of wild jasmine survived the dry months, lending the slope a faint, perpetual sweetness in the heat. from a flat shelf of rock near the bend, mira could watch barges drift toward the coast and could imagine herself, in time, drifting after them. she had never in her short life left the valley, but the river suggested itself to her as a way out.

preview: The following text is adapted from a 1908 memoir by a Tlingit writer recalling how the women of the village preserved salmon during her chi…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? the following text is adapted from a 1908 memoir by a tlingit writer recalling how the women of the village preserved salmon during her childhood. now the women began to inspect the racks of fish set out in the morning sun—walking unhurriedly along the shore, knives in hand, and pressing a thumb just behind the gill to test the firmness of the flesh. the salmon, like people, have their humors: some yielded readily under the touch and were carried to the smokehouse at once; others held tight and shining, scarcely changed from the moment they had been pulled from the river, and would need another half-day in the sun before they were ready. the women spoke softly among themselves as they worked, and there was no haste in any of it.

preview: The following text is from an 1899 poem titled "The Quiet Hills." My weary heart yet cries, and all my breath Aches for the slow green sile…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is from an 1899 poem titled "the quiet hills." my weary heart yet cries, and all my breath aches for the slow green silence of the hills. hold me, ye ridges, from the noise of streets, and let me lie beneath the patient pine, where the soft drip of resin shall be all the speech i hear, mingled with little winds that bear me lightly on between the world and the dim country of forgetfulness.

preview: Cyanea kohalae is a species in a family of flowering plants known collectively as the rosette lobelias, all of which grow only in the Hawai…
