searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? cyanea kohalae is a species in a family of flowering plants known collectively as the rosette lobelias, all of which grow only in the hawaiian islands. members of this family show an unusual breadth of form, with some species developing into low rosettes barely six inches across and others into multi-branched candelabra-shaped trees more than twenty feet tall. all these species descended from a single ancestral bellflower that reached the islands roughly three million years ago. the bellflower's descendants spread into separate species as they settled into different elevations, soils, and pollinator communities across the archipelago.

preview: In response to concerns that energy bills are routinely misunderstood by household consumers, regulators in several European countries have…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? in response to concerns that energy bills are routinely misunderstood by household consumers, regulators in several european countries have required utilities to add side-by-side comparison charts showing each household's monthly consumption against that of similar homes nearby. marco linares and colleagues studied a year's worth of such charts sent to thousands of customers and found that the comparisons produced only small and short-lived reductions in consumption. linares and colleagues note, however, that the comparison charts cost very little to print and to distribute alongside existing bills, so even the modest savings they generated arguably justified continuing the policy.

preview: Comet 21P/Giacobini-Zinner has shown repeated, irregular outbursts of activity since at least the late 1990s. Unlike many comets with recur…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? comet 21p/giacobini-zinner has shown repeated, irregular outbursts of activity since at least the late 1990s. unlike many comets with recurring outbursts, however, its episodes do not cluster near perihelion, where solar heating is greatest, so direct sublimation of surface ices is unlikely to be the cause. and as mira adachi and colleagues observe, the inherently one-off nature of a violent collision with another body cannot plausibly account for multiple outbursts of similar duration and intensity spaced years apart. some researchers have suggested that gradual cracking of an icy crust could occasionally expose fresh volatiles to space, but adachi and colleagues conclude that, for now, no proposed mechanism convincingly fits the full pattern.

preview: The tamarisk shrub evolved in central Asia, where the leaf beetle Diorhabda elongata feeds on its foliage. In 1823, tamarisks were brought…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the third sentence in the overall structure of the text? the tamarisk shrub evolved in central asia, where the leaf beetle diorhabda elongata feeds on its foliage. in 1823, tamarisks were brought to the american southwest as ornamentals, well beyond the natural range of d. elongata . but evolutionary partnerships between herbivores and their host plants can persist across continents and centuries. around 2001, after deliberate introductions for biocontrol, d. elongata populations established themselves along several southwestern rivers and now show damage rates on tamarisk strikingly similar to those documented in its native range—findings that a team led by sun jianhua treats as a textbook case of host–herbivore reunion.

preview: The field of comparative neurobiology searches for shared biological underpinnings of complex behaviors across very different animal groups…
searchText: which choice best describes the main purpose of the text? the field of comparative neurobiology searches for shared biological underpinnings of complex behaviors across very different animal groups. many such behaviors—learning, memory, decision-making—have been described in detail in vertebrates, where they are linked to specific brain structures and chemical pathways (for example, the formation of long-term memory by way of activity in the hippocampus). building on these vertebrate models, anika patel and her colleagues have recently shown that the common octopus ( octopus vulgaris ) forms durable place memories using circuits whose chemistry closely mirrors those of mammalian memory systems, even though the octopus brain is anatomically nothing like a mammalian one.

preview: Curious about whether mental rehearsal could improve physical performance, Hannah Okonkwo and her team recruited competitive springboard di…
searchText: which choice best states the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? curious about whether mental rehearsal could improve physical performance, hannah okonkwo and her team recruited competitive springboard divers for a multiweek study. the athletes were asked to spend twenty minutes a day visualizing specific dives in detail, and the researchers tracked the smoothness of their executed dives across the trial period. across the multiweek trial, the smoothness ratings improved steadily for divers who reported clear, detailed mental images, very much as one would expect from athletes engaging in additional physical practice. because the visualization sessions involved no physical movement at all, the team concluded that detailed mental rehearsal can drive measurable improvements in trained athletic skills.

preview: Since the early 2000s, several women of African descent have been elected to the United Kingdom's House of Commons through the major establ…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? since the early 2000s, several women of african descent have been elected to the united kingdom's house of commons through the major established parties. in parts of west africa, by contrast, women political organizers have founded their own parties to support candidates committed to advancing women's interests directly. this approach has been particularly visible in sierra leone, where femi bangura, a leader of the women-led party akoya, won a seat in the national parliament in 2022.

preview: The 1974 publication of Aurelia Halberstam's monograph Against the New Schools placed her at odds with almost every faction in the postwar…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? the 1974 publication of aurelia halberstam's monograph against the new schools placed her at odds with almost every faction in the postwar european architecture community—though those factions disagreed with each other, she nevertheless found grounds to disagree with them all. she judged the technocratic functionalists who treated buildings as mere machines for living to be impoverished in their imagination. but she also rebuked the romantic regionalists who, in her view, indulged in nostalgic ornamentation while ignoring the demands of contemporary urban life. halberstam's contemporaries, used to being praised by allies and condemned by rivals, found themselves uncertain of where she stood in the discipline.

preview: Bryozoans are tiny colonial filter-feeders that build delicate calcareous skeletons by secreting calcium carbonate. These animals are widel…
searchText: which choice best describes the main purpose of the text? bryozoans are tiny colonial filter-feeders that build delicate calcareous skeletons by secreting calcium carbonate. these animals are widely considered vulnerable to ocean acidification, since calcium carbonate dissolves more readily at lower ph. marisol aldana and colleagues, however, recently demonstrated that a thin organic veneer covering the skeleton of the common bryozoan membranipora membranacea shields the underlying mineral from dissolution. the team was further surprised to find that even when this veneer was experimentally damaged, colonies in seawater chemistries projected for the year 2100 nonetheless laid down new skeleton at rates indistinguishable from those in present-day controls—suggesting that the acidification threat to this bryozoan, at least, has likely been overstated.

preview: Individual giraffes and many large modern antelopes tend to remain within stable home ranges over the course of their lives, which had led…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined statement in the text as a whole? individual giraffes and many large modern antelopes tend to remain within stable home ranges over the course of their lives, which had led some paleontologists to speculate that sivatherium giganteum , an extinct giraffid of the asian plains, might have behaved the same way. sivatherium teeth grew in distinct enamel layers, incorporating dietary minerals as they formed, so each layer records the chemical fingerprint of the food the animal consumed during a particular interval of its life. by comparing the strontium isotope ratios across these layers to the ratios characteristic of various regional bedrocks, mehmet gunay and colleagues showed that several sivatherium individuals had moved across hundreds of kilometers during their lifetimes, contrary to the assumption of a fixed range.

preview: Ilya Kaminsky, a celebrated poet and essayist, recently released his first audio poetry project, Sounds From the Other Side of the Wall , i…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? ilya kaminsky, a celebrated poet and essayist, recently released his first audio poetry project, sounds from the other side of the wall , in collaboration with composer nadia sirota. the project pairs kaminsky's recited poems with sirota's layered viola textures and field recordings to convey the felt experience of deafness, both its silences and its unfamiliar resonances. several critics have suggested that the project asks listeners to reconsider how deafness is represented in mainstream contemporary art—arguing that, unlike documentaries that treat deafness from the outside, this audio work renders it from within without ever attempting to simulate impaired hearing literally.

preview: In Henry James's novel The Portrait of a Lady , a barely perceptible hesitation in the voice of suitor Gilbert Osmond causes the protagonis…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? in henry james's novel the portrait of a lady , a barely perceptible hesitation in the voice of suitor gilbert osmond causes the protagonist isabel archer to flush; her self-consciousness deepens when she suspects that he has registered her flush. this moment—in which isabel not only reads osmond's mental state from a minute vocal cue but also infers that he is reading her own response—exemplifies what literary scholar caro vincenti terms "recursive interiority": fictional minds attending to other minds attending to them.

preview: According to historian Lan Nguyen, Vietnamese American women played a pivotal role in the West Coast electronics-assembly industry during t…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? according to historian lan nguyen, vietnamese american women played a pivotal role in the west coast electronics-assembly industry during the late cold war. at the time, major contractors had committed to delivering circuit boards on tight timelines to defense and telecommunications customers, and increased production targets handed considerable leverage to employees on the lines—a substantial portion of whom were vietnamese american women: workers demanded paid sick leave and on-site language classes, and supervisors, fearful of missed shipments, frequently agreed.

preview: The following text is from a late seventeenth-century English verse translation of an ancient pastoral poem. The shepherdess Phyllis has ju…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is from a late seventeenth-century english verse translation of an ancient pastoral poem. the shepherdess phyllis has just listened to the wanderer daphnis describe his many sorrows. but silent griefs were stirring in her breast: a flame unsought had robb'd her of her rest. his travel'd words, his sun-touch'd brow, his eyes— all these together did her heart surprise. she turn'd her wheel, but knew not what she spun; and watch'd the flock, yet saw it scarce by one.

preview: The following text is adapted from a nineteenth-century novel. Caroline Frye, a celebrated naturalist, was renowned for her published lectu…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? the following text is adapted from a nineteenth-century novel. caroline frye, a celebrated naturalist, was renowned for her published lectures on butterflies. some years past, a serious young geologist, being in paris, observed at a sunday salon a chatty woman in an ill-cut shawl, talking with great gaiety to a circle of admirers who, in his view, did her too much credit. he pitied her hosts and was preparing to retire with disdain, when—chancing upon her name in the conversation—he discovered, to his considerable confusion, that the woman whose manner he had judged so trivial was the very caroline frye whose writings on butterflies he had carried with him across the atlantic.

preview: Engineer and computer scientist Grace Hopper is widely viewed as a foundational figure in early software design. Her best-known paper, "The…
searchText: which choice best describes the overall structure of the text? engineer and computer scientist grace hopper is widely viewed as a foundational figure in early software design. her best-known paper, "the education of a computer," published in 1952 while she was working at remington rand, articulated the idea of a "compiler"—a program that translates human-readable instructions into the machine's own code—allowing programmers to write in something far closer to english than to raw binary. computer historian janet abbate has called hopper's compiler work one of the conceptual breakthroughs that turned computing from an arcane craft into a profession.

preview: Some studies have suggested that brief, controlled bursts of background noise can sharpen creative problem-solving, but we should not overs…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? some studies have suggested that brief, controlled bursts of background noise can sharpen creative problem-solving, but we should not overstate this phenomenon. consider, for instance, a 2017 study by bo-yeong park and hannah voss in which participants worked on insight puzzles in silence, in a moderate-noise condition, and in a high-noise condition. park and voss expected that the moderate-noise group would solve the most puzzles, since some research suggests mild distraction can broaden attention. in the study, however, the moderate-noise group's mean number of solved puzzles was only marginally above that of the silent group, and well within the margin of statistical noise.

preview: "Does it sound real?" Many sound designers for video games make this question their organizing concern as they engineer everything from the…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined question in the text as a whole? "does it sound real?" many sound designers for video games make this question their organizing concern as they engineer everything from the ring of footsteps on stone to the snap of a closing door. as a rule, while the speech of game characters is exaggerated for legibility, ambient and incidental sounds are crafted to mimic the physical world. some sound designers, however, such as akira watanabe at studio tsuru, are guided by a different question altogether. rather than asking first whether a sound matches its real-world counterpart, watanabe asks whether it carries the emotion the scene is supposed to deliver—designing footsteps that are physically wrong but dramatically inevitable.

preview: In 2022 musicologist Sofia Reinhart warned executives at streaming platforms who confidently predict that traditional concert recordings wi…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? in 2022 musicologist sofia reinhart warned executives at streaming platforms who confidently predict that traditional concert recordings will soon be supplanted by short-form interactive video formats. reinhart pointed out that the notion of an "interactive" musical recording is much older than these executives assume, reaching back at least to the early twentieth-century player piano, whose rolls allowed performers and listeners alike to manipulate tempo and dynamics in real time . moreover, she noted, the newer interactive formats so prized by the streaming companies still depend heavily on conventionally recorded performances as their underlying material.

preview: The following text is adapted from a nineteenth-century novel. Jonas is a young man making his way through the back country of upstate New…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole? the following text is adapted from a nineteenth-century novel. jonas is a young man making his way through the back country of upstate new york during the late eighteenth century. he contracted himself out for six months, at the end of which he was to receive in payment a hundred and forty acres of cleared land lying near the black river. [...] his employer proving false to the bargain in the matter of the land, and there being no court within fifty miles that would compel him to honor it, jonas— too easily contented, perhaps, with whatever cheer the road and a kind stranger could supply —at length resigned the dispute and turned his face once more toward the south.

preview: Examining artifacts from an early twentieth-century cannery and adjacent boarding-house site in coastal Washington, archaeologist Mei-Lin R…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? examining artifacts from an early twentieth-century cannery and adjacent boarding-house site in coastal washington, archaeologist mei-lin reyes documented that japanese american women employed in the salmon-canning lines wore custom-sewn cotton tunics with concealed waist ties that produced a silhouette resembling the slim, belted dresses then in fashion in nearby seattle—a silhouette normally achieved by garments far less practical for cannery work. reyes argues that this sartorial choice shows that these women aspired to participate in mainstream urban fashion even as their daily labor demanded garments that could endure brine, knives, and long shifts on slick floors.

preview: In 2022, documentary filmmaker and historian Nasreen Aliyu released the first peer-reviewed scholarly film about West African market women,…
searchText: which choice best states the main purpose of the text? in 2022, documentary filmmaker and historian nasreen aliyu released the first peer-reviewed scholarly film about west african market women, titled counters and conversations . typically in peer review, experts evaluate scholarly articles before publication. for aliyu's film, dubbed a "film/essay/archive," peer review involved both academic historians and market traders who appear in the film. by weaving footage of daily market exchanges together with archival photographs and embedded scholarly essays that connect those exchanges to longer histories of west african trade, the film opens an entirely new mode for publishing historical research about everyday economic life.

preview: Several studies have found negligible long-term effects on incumbent mayors who raise local property taxes, yet a number of recent mid-size…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole? several studies have found negligible long-term effects on incumbent mayors who raise local property taxes, yet a number of recent mid-sized us cities have seen incumbents soundly defeated after enacting precisely such tax increases. political scientists diego ramos and helena wirth attribute this incongruity to mayors' usual tendency—not followed in the recent cases—to time tax increases for early in their terms, bundle them with visible service improvements, and pair them with explicit sunset provisions. the recent losing incumbents, by contrast, raised rates late in their first terms with no accompanying service expansion or sunset clause, triggering precisely the kind of voter backlash the older studies failed to anticipate.

preview: Astronomers are confident that the red supergiant Antares will eventually exhaust the elements in its core and end as a supernova. They are…
searchText: which choice best describes the function of the second sentence in the overall structure of the text? astronomers are confident that the red supergiant antares will eventually exhaust the elements in its core and end as a supernova. they are much less confident, however, about when this will occur, since the timing depends on internal properties of antares that remain largely unmeasured. astrophysicist mira petrov and colleagues recently asked whether subtle brightness oscillations on the star's surface could be used to infer those internal properties—in effect, applying to antares a technique pioneered in the study of the sun. early results suggest that the oscillations may indeed constrain the rate at which the star is fusing carbon, narrowing the range of plausible supernova dates.

preview: Differences in coastal management practices—such as the construction of seawalls, the planting of dune grasses, and the routing of stormwat…

preview: To show that the structural soundness of bridge cables can be evaluated without removing their protective casings, engineer Mira Tanaka and…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? to show that the structural soundness of bridge cables can be evaluated without removing their protective casings, engineer mira tanaka and her team ______ blank the tendency of certain steel alloys' acoustic signatures to shift under tension: the researchers demonstrated that these subtle shifts can be detected from outside the casing and can reveal areas of fatigue inside the cables.

searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? although researchers have long suspected that ancient mediterranean civilizations exchanged building practices, concrete proof of such exchanges is hard to come by. however, recent fieldwork at a port settlement in carthage uncovered a temple complex displaying striking ______ blank greek architecture—including a colonnade that closely mirrors examples from corinth—providing tangible evidence of cultural exchange in parts of carthage.

searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? the historian's account of trade routes connecting medieval west africa and the mediterranean is ______ blank , as it overlooks several important archaeological discoveries from the past decade. to be persuasive, the account would need to address recent excavations of trans-saharan caravan stations, such as those at tadmekka and sijilmasa.

searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? given that the chaotic atmospheric conditions on hot jupiters should make stable weather patterns nearly impossible, it's not surprising that the persistence of certain banded cloud structures on these planets has lacked ______ blank explanation. astrophysicist vivian park and her colleagues shed new light on the subject when they used three-dimensional simulations to identify a delicate interplay of jet streams that could sustain such bands.

searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? researcher linh pham and colleagues showed that processing fees—the charges merchants must pay to accept card payments—reduce the appeal of accepting small purchases. retailers can absorb these fees to encourage card use, but those fees, though variable, are largely ______ blank transaction size, so retailers that absorb the fees will likely focus on attracting a few large purchases over many small ones.

searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? one leading theory of the origin of saturn's rings holds that a moderate-sized icy moon spiraled inward and was shredded by tidal forces, leaving a halo of debris that settled into the ring plane. until recently, this lost moon was ______ blank , but researcher jacob wisdom and colleagues now claim to have identified its likely orbital signature in the precession of saturn's spin axis.

searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? anadromous fish, like atlantic salmon, are born in freshwater, migrate to the ocean to mature, and return to freshwater to spawn. the migration's strict pattern is why anadromous fish should be ______ blank those that are merely catadromous (which spawn in the sea but otherwise live in fresh water, like european eels): both move between water types, but the direction and timing of the migrations are reversed.

preview: In 1903, designer Sarah Mae founded the Heath Workshop, which produced furniture using only traditional joinery and locally sourced hardwoo…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? in 1903, designer sarah mae founded the heath workshop, which produced furniture using only traditional joinery and locally sourced hardwoods. historians argue that mae's rejection of mass-production techniques is ______ blank the heath pieces' use of hand-cut dovetails and slow-aged finishes reminiscent of preindustrial workshops: these meticulously made elements showcase the labor involved.

preview: The following text is adapted from Virginia Woolf's 1925 novel Mrs. Dalloway . The narrator is describing London at dusk. The day was slidi…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? the following text is adapted from virginia woolf's 1925 novel mrs. dalloway . the narrator is describing london at dusk. the day was sliding now into that translucent half-light that precedes the lamps, and the river beneath the bridge had begun to take on the colors of the hour. the sun, already low behind the warehouses, was apparent only in the streaks of copper laid across the water, brightening and fading and brightening again, each gleam thinner and more weary than the last, and condemned no sooner kindled to be extinguished. ©1925 by virginia woolf

preview: Despite claims of comprehensive scope, much major research on global trade flows performed in the 1990s suffered from a narrow focus on a h…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? despite claims of comprehensive scope, much major research on global trade flows performed in the 1990s suffered from a narrow focus on a handful of large economies in north america and western europe, partly because customs data from elsewhere were difficult to obtain. researchers would later ______ blank this shortcoming after gaining access to digitized customs archives from countries in southeast asia, such as vietnam, and south america, such as chile.

preview: Diné filmmaker Sydney Freeland ______ blank film's tendency to depict Indigenous characters through a lens of tragedy: this refusal is evid…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? diné filmmaker sydney freeland ______ blank film's tendency to depict indigenous characters through a lens of tragedy: this refusal is evident in her movie drunktown's finest , which follows three young people with complex inner lives and ordinary ambitions rather than archetypal hardships.

preview: The following text is adapted from the 1990 novel So Far from God by Chicana writer Ana Castillo. The main character has become widely soug…
searchText: as used in the text, what does the word "determine" most nearly mean? the following text is adapted from the 1990 novel so far from god by chicana writer ana castillo. the main character has become widely sought after for her culinary creations. more and more often she was asked to alter her recipes, to lighten this, to omit that, to make her dishes acceptable to one diner or another, and more often than not she did so, for she was eager to please and slow to refuse; it was difficult for her to disappoint a customer. those who praised her cooking, who recommended her to friends, and who arranged for her catering began to determine it. ©1990 by ana castillo

preview: The work of Cree composer Cris Derksen draws its power in part from the tension among her ______ blank influences: classical cello training…

preview: Despite how ______ blank they may seem on first reading, the late poems of Wallace Stevens have proven to be remarkably rewarding. Readers…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? despite how ______ blank they may seem on first reading, the late poems of wallace stevens have proven to be remarkably rewarding. readers who manage to push past stevens's tangled syntax and elaborate symbolism often discover that his startlingly original meditations transform the way they see the world.

preview: Scholarly discussions of class in Jane Austen's novels often celebrate the small rebellions of the writer's heroines against the rigid expe…

searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? the construction of walter pichler's 1972 sculptural environment tor zum garten was ______ blank undertaking. over the course of nearly four years, pichler and his collaborators painstakingly carved hundreds of stone components by hand and repeatedly had to devise new tools and techniques to realize the structural details pichler envisioned.

searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? a glancing summary of yinka shonibare's 2010 installation nelson's ship in a bottle can make the work seem ______ blank —a sculpture that consists of a scaled-down royal navy vessel inside a glass bottle may sound merely decorative—but in fact the work is a layered, provocative meditation on imperialism and identity that demands sustained attention.

searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? original research conducted by olufemi akande and jeanne lin is motivated by their observation that although many studies have explored the effects of arctic conditions on human cognition, there is a ______ blank studies examining the effects on cognition of living in extremely arid desert environments, such as the atacama villages of toconao and san pedro.

preview: The following text is adapted from Marcel Proust's 1913 novel Swann's Way (translated by C. K. Scott Moncrieff). The narrator is recalling…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? the following text is adapted from marcel proust's 1913 novel swann's way (translated by c. k. scott moncrieff). the narrator is recalling an afternoon in the village of combray. the villagers, returning from the fields and the workshops at that hour when the church bells call the day to a close, seemed always to be dressed in shades of brown and dust except on feast days, when, for a few hours, they put on a determined and improbable bright. there they were now, in their dust-colored cloaks, contesting , foot by foot, our passage, with their handcarts, their barrows, their bursting baskets borne stoutly upon the shoulder. ©1913 by marcel proust

preview: The results of controlled laboratory experiments testing the effectiveness of certain educational interventions sometimes fail to ______ bl…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? the results of controlled laboratory experiments testing the effectiveness of certain educational interventions sometimes fail to ______ blank conclusions that teachers reach based on their day-to-day observations of students. while there are several possible reasons for this, one is that teachers may overlook background variables that account for the gains they attribute to the interventions in question.

preview: Social media platforms with openly partisan moderation policies became a significant force in US public discourse during the 2010s. While s…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? social media platforms with openly partisan moderation policies became a significant force in us public discourse during the 2010s. while some commentators welcomed this development, claiming that such platforms had a refreshing candor missing from traditional news, less ______ blank observers countered that such platforms tended toward ideological echo chambers that intensified political polarization to problematic levels.

preview: Within certain deep-sea fish species, some individuals develop an enlarged swim bladder—an apparent overdevelopment of the gas-filled organ…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? within certain deep-sea fish species, some individuals develop an enlarged swim bladder—an apparent overdevelopment of the gas-filled organ used to control buoyancy. given the bladder's greater prevalence among fish that make rapid vertical ascents, some researchers hypothesize that its role isn't ______ blank ; rather, the enlarged bladder may actively assist with quick depth changes.

preview: The familial structure of the laysan albatross ( Phoebastria immutabilis ) shows tremendous ______ blank : long-paired males and females so…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? the familial structure of the laysan albatross ( phoebastria immutabilis ) shows tremendous ______ blank : long-paired males and females sometimes part for a season to rear chicks with another partner, and while female-male pairs are most common at some colonies, female-female pairs frequently rear chicks too, achieving a higher rate of fledging success than is sometimes observed at neighboring colonies.

preview: Proposals to extend the duration of paid parental leave are generally discussed in terms of the effects on new parents, but Diane Carringto…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? proposals to extend the duration of paid parental leave are generally discussed in terms of the effects on new parents, but diane carrington has argued that lengthening such leave could ______ blank savings rates among working adults by shortening the period during which they are simultaneously earning income and contributing to retirement accounts.

preview: Some labor historians ______ blank that mid-twentieth-century factories in the American Midwest experienced steady gains in worker producti…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? some labor historians ______ blank that mid-twentieth-century factories in the american midwest experienced steady gains in worker productivity—they assumed that productivity rose smoothly with each year of expanded automation. historian marisol gutiérrez showed, however, that a close look at the available shop-floor records disproves this supposition.

preview: The following text is adapted from Edith Wharton's 1905 novel The House of Mirth . [Mr. Trenor] had passed his early manhood at his club an…

[mr. trenor] had passed his early manhood at his club and in the company of horses, and was held among the older families of new york to have contracted a too speculative habit of mind. mr. trenor's investments were as difficult to foresee as the markets themselves.

preview: While most flowering plants rely on pollen transfer between separate individuals to produce viable seeds, the autogamous orchid Holcoglossu…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? while most flowering plants rely on pollen transfer between separate individuals to produce viable seeds, the autogamous orchid holcoglossum amesianum presents an intriguing ______ blank : in a recent study, researchers observed that this orchid's pollen-bearing structures actively curve back to deposit pollen directly onto the plant's own stigma, producing fully viable seeds without any external assistance.

preview: It is by no means ______ blank to recognize the influence of Italian Baroque composer Claudio Monteverdi on Hannah Lash's chamber works; in…

preview: As an undergraduate researcher in archaeology, Priya Subramanian contributed to a landmark study challenging the long-standing view that am…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? as an undergraduate researcher in archaeology, priya subramanian contributed to a landmark study challenging the long-standing view that among bronze age communities of the aegean, women's participation in long-distance trade was ______ blank . the research team's reassessment of grave goods at sites in mycenae and tiryns revealed that, in fact, women were buried with merchants' weights and seals at rates comparable to those of men in the same populations.

preview: Some researchers have argued that birds during the early Cenozoic era were not a very ______ blank group, but paleontologist Daniel Field's…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? some researchers have argued that birds during the early cenozoic era were not a very ______ blank group, but paleontologist daniel field's recent work shows that the first 10 million years after the dinosaurs' extinction saw birds rapidly fill an extraordinary range of ecological roles. fossils of various plant-eating, seed-cracking, and even wading birds have been recovered from north america and europe, including specimens like asteriornis maastrichtensis , which field considers a near-universal common ancestor of modern landfowl and waterfowl.

preview: For her 2020 art installation Ocean Without a Shore , choreographer Bintou Diallo collaborated with sound designer Pavel Krupin to create a…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase? for her 2020 art installation ocean without a shore , choreographer bintou diallo collaborated with sound designer pavel krupin to create a piece that critics found genuinely ______ blank : they praised diallo for transforming a working harbor warehouse into a kinetic stage by suspending dancers from cables 30 feet above the floor and saturating the space with underwater recordings of whale song and tidal currents.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Novelist Zora Neale Hurston was raised in Florida and graduated from Ba…
searchText: the student wants to identify the setting of hurston's novel their eyes were watching god. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: novelist zora neale hurston was raised in florida and graduated from barnard college in new york city. several of hurston's most acclaimed novels are set in florida. one such novel is their eyes were watching god (1937). hurston's novels often portray the lives of black communities in the rural south.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Cornelia Parker is a British conceptual artist. One of her installation…
searchText: the student wants to describe how the fragments in cold dark matter: an exploded view are arranged. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: cornelia parker is a british conceptual artist. one of her installations is cold dark matter: an exploded view (1991). the work consists of the fragments of a garden shed that she had blown up. the pieces are suspended from the ceiling by wires. a single light bulb is placed in the center so that the fragments cast dramatic shadows on the gallery walls.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: “The Lesson” is a short story. It was written by African American autho…
searchText: the student wants to indicate where the short story takes place. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: “the lesson” is a short story. it was written by african american author toni cade bambara. it was first published in her book gorilla, my love in 1972. it is narrated by a young girl named sylvia. it takes place primarily in new york city.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The contact process is an industrial process used to manufacture sulfur…
searchText: the student wants to provide an overview of the contact process. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the contact process is an industrial process used to manufacture sulfuric acid (h 2 so 4 ). it was developed by british vinegar merchant peregrine phillips in 1831. the process's key reaction combines sulfur dioxide (so 2 ) with oxygen (o 2 ). it requires a vanadium oxide catalyst and elevated temperatures. most of the sulfuric acid produced by this process is used in the manufacture of phosphate fertilizers.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Sasha Petrov is a Brooklyn-based ceramicist. She uses the term “flora-w…
searchText: the student wants to provide an explanation and example of “flora-ware.” which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: sasha petrov is a brooklyn-based ceramicist. she uses the term “flora-ware” to describe the plant-inspired vessels that she sculpts. “flora-ware” is a combination of the words “flora” and “earthenware.” her piece bloom is a teapot shaped like an open hibiscus flower. her piece tendril is a vase formed from intertwined ivy stems.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The Atacama Desert in northern Chile is one of the driest places on Ear…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize how dry the conditions can be in the atacama desert. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the atacama desert in northern chile is one of the driest places on earth. some weather stations there have recorded average annual rainfall of less than 0.04 inches. specialized microbes that can survive long stretches without water inhabit the desert. during rare wet periods, average rainfall can reach a relatively generous 0.6 inches. about 200 different kinds of flowering plants are known to grow in the atacama.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The human body uses vitamin C to maintain healthy connective tissues. V…
searchText: the student wants to identify which of the three foods has the highest vitamin c content. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the human body uses vitamin c to maintain healthy connective tissues. vitamin c is a nutrient found in many fresh foods. oranges contain 70 milligrams per 100 grams (mg/100g) of vitamin c. red bell peppers contain 128 mg/100g. guavas contain 228 mg/100g.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 1903, two brothers, Pedro and Manuel, set off on a horseback journey…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize how far the brothers traveled. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 1903, two brothers, pedro and manuel, set off on a horseback journey from buenos aires, argentina, to new york city. the 13,000-mile journey took three years. the aim was to demonstrate the endurance of argentine creole horses. in 2003, argentine veterinarian jorge coro retraced the route to mark the journey's centennial. coro wanted to call attention to the original ride's significance for south american horsemanship.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The Cumberland Road was a federally built highway begun in 1811 and com…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the distance covered by the cumberland road. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the cumberland road was a federally built highway begun in 1811 and completed in 1837. it was the first major improved highway in the united states built by the federal government. it linked cumberland, maryland, with vandalia, illinois. it stretched 620 miles.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Easter Island, or Rapa Nui, lies about 2,300 miles west of Chile in the…
searchText: the student wants to specify who, according to the 2020 study, may have arrived at rapa nui after the original polynesian settlers. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: easter island, or rapa nui, lies about 2,300 miles west of chile in the pacific ocean. researchers have long believed that the first humans to settle rapa nui were polynesian seafarers from the western pacific around 1200 ce. a 2020 genetic study coauthored by alexander ioannidis identified evidence of native american ancestry in rapa nui inhabitants dating to roughly 1380 ce. the researchers suggested that indigenous south americans may have reached rapa nui shortly after polynesian settlement. sweet potato remains found on the island also support contact between polynesians and south americans.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The Akashi Kaikyō Bridge is a suspension bridge in Japan. It connects t…
searchText: the student wants to compare the main span lengths of the two suspension bridges. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the akashi kaikyō bridge is a suspension bridge in japan. it connects the city of kobe to awaji island. its main span is roughly 1.24 miles long. the great belt bridge is a suspension bridge in denmark. it connects the islands of zealand and funen. its main span is about 1.03 miles long.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Iznik in northwest Turkey was a major center of ceramic production from…
searchText: the student wants to contrast the two ceramic-decoration techniques. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: iznik in northwest turkey was a major center of ceramic production from the fifteenth to the seventeenth centuries. iznik ceramics are known for their distinctive decorative techniques. two key techniques are underglaze and overglaze painting. in the underglaze technique, designs are painted directly on the unfired clay before a clear glaze is applied. in the overglaze technique, designs are painted on top of an already-fired glaze and then re-fired at a lower temperature.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Oceanographers use traditional research ships to study the open ocean.…
searchText: the student wants to explain an advantage of argo floats. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: oceanographers use traditional research ships to study the open ocean. research ships are expensive to operate and cannot be deployed in many locations at once. research ships are also limited to areas safely navigable by large vessels. argo floats, autonomous drifting sensors that weigh only about 60 pounds, can collect ocean data continuously and travel freely on currents. argo floats have been proposed as a complement to traditional research ships.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Photogrammetry is the process of measuring real-world distances and sha…
searchText: the student wants to define the term “photogrammetry.” which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: photogrammetry is the process of measuring real-world distances and shapes from overlapping photographs. this process enables researchers to construct accurate three-dimensional models from sets of two-dimensional images. a 2019 project by adam lowe and colleagues used photogrammetry to digitally reconstruct the tomb of tutankhamun in egypt. the resulting model was used to build a full-scale replica of the tomb. the replica has been displayed to the public, helping protect the original tomb from heavy visitor traffic.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The coelacanth is a deep-sea fish. Up until 1938, it was believed to ha…
searchText: the student wants to define the term “lazarus species” and provide an example of one. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish these goals? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the coelacanth is a deep-sea fish. up until 1938, it was believed to have been extinct for about 65 million years. that year, a live coelacanth was caught off the coast of south africa. the coelacanth is considered a lazarus species. “lazarus species” is a term for living species of organisms that were once believed to be extinct.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The capybara is a species of large rodent. It is native to wetlands acr…
searchText: the student wants to specify the average weight of the capybara. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the capybara is a species of large rodent. it is native to wetlands across south america. the capybara mainly feeds on grasses and aquatic plants. it has an average weight of about 50 kilograms.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Sui Sin Far (1865-1914) was a Chinese English writer. Throughout her ca…
searchText: the student wants to introduce sui sin far to an audience unfamiliar with her career. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: sui sin far (1865-1914) was a chinese english writer. throughout her career, she wrote pioneering short fiction about chinese american communities. her collection mrs. spring fragrance was published in 1912. it is considered one of the first books of fiction by an author of chinese descent published in north america.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The Khmer Empire lasted about 600 years (802 CE to 1431 CE). The Khmer…
searchText: the student wants to specify the location of angkor. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the khmer empire lasted about 600 years (802 ce to 1431 ce). the khmer controlled an area spanning about 1 million square kilometers. this area included present-day cambodia and parts of thailand, laos, and vietnam. the empire's capital was the city of angkor. angkor was located near present-day siem reap, cambodia.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The inventor Elias Howe patented the lockstitch sewing machine in 1846.…
searchText: the student wants to indicate how howe, indiana, got its name. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the inventor elias howe patented the lockstitch sewing machine in 1846. he patented it in massachusetts. the lockstitch was the first widely successful sewing machine mechanism. the town of howe, indiana, is named after elias howe. howe street in new haven, connecticut, is also named after him.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Lemur catta is a primate found on the island of Madagascar. Indri indri…
searchText: the student wants to compare the extinction risk faced by l. catta with that faced by i. indri . which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: lemur catta is a primate found on the island of madagascar. indri indri is a primate also found on madagascar. conservation organizations evaluate the risk that species will become extinct in the near future. l. catta faces an elevated risk of extinction. i. indri faces a critically high risk of becoming extinct in the near future.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Some atoms have a very large nucleus. The nucleus contains protons and…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the diameter of uranium-238's nucleus. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: some atoms have a very large nucleus. the nucleus contains protons and neutrons bound together. an atom of uranium-238 has a particularly massive nucleus. the diameter of its nucleus is approximately 15 femtometers.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Mei-Lin Tanaka is a scientist. Tanaka studies coral reefs. A scientist…
searchText: the student wants to identify what type of scientist tanaka is. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: mei-lin tanaka is a scientist. tanaka studies coral reefs. a scientist who studies coral reefs is called a coral reef ecologist. staghorn, brain, and elkhorn are examples of coral species.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Maimonides was a Sephardic Jewish philosopher and physician. His book T…
searchText: the student wants to identify the year that the guide for the perplexed was published. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: maimonides was a sephardic jewish philosopher and physician. his book the guide for the perplexed reconciled religious and philosophical thought of his era. it was published in 1190 ce. it influenced jewish, christian, and islamic scholars for centuries.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Banff is a national park in western Canada. In 2003, grizzly bears were…
searchText: the student wants to specify the number of grizzly bears in banff. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: banff is a national park in western canada. in 2003, grizzly bears were reintroduced into surrounding habitats. since then, the number of grizzly bears in the park has stabilized at roughly 65. this number is believed to be the park's carrying capacity. carrying capacity describes the maximum number of a species that an environment's resources can sustain over time.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Stan is the nickname of a dinosaur fossil specimen housed at the Abu Dh…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two specimens. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: stan is the nickname of a dinosaur fossil specimen housed at the abu dhabi natural history museum. the abu dhabi natural history museum is located in abu dhabi, united arab emirates. stan is a member of the genus tyrannosaurus . trix is the nickname of a dinosaur fossil specimen housed at naturalis biodiversity center. naturalis biodiversity center is located in leiden, netherlands. trix is a member of the genus tyrannosaurus .

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Octavia Butler was a US writer known for her science fiction. Butler's…
searchText: the student wants to indicate the title of a novel by octavia butler that received a hugo award nomination. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: octavia butler was a us writer known for her science fiction. butler's science fiction novel parable of the sower was published in 1993. the novel won a hugo award nomination in 1994. the hugo awards are given each year to the best works of science fiction and fantasy.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 1903, African American inventor Mary Kenner invented an improved san…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between kenner's invention and morgan's invention. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 1903, african american inventor mary kenner invented an improved sanitary belt. it improved on the existing design of personal hygiene products. it made daily life more convenient for many women. in 1923, african american inventor garrett morgan invented a three-position traffic signal. it improved on the existing design of two-position traffic signals. it made urban intersections safer for drivers and pedestrians.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Wifredo Lam was an Afro-Cuban artist who lived in Havana and Paris. The…
searchText: the student wants to indicate the year the jungle was completed. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: wifredo lam was an afro-cuban artist who lived in havana and paris. the jungle is a notable painting by lam. it blends elements of surrealism, cubism, and afro-cuban imagery. this painting was completed in 1943.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Dr. Lin Wei studies fossils at the Yunnan University of Paleontology. W…
searchText: the student wants to indicate where the arthropod fossil was found. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: dr. lin wei studies fossils at the yunnan university of paleontology. wei's research team recently found a 520-million-year-old arthropod fossil. it is the oldest fossil from the arthropod group ever recovered with preserved soft tissue. it was found in the chengjiang shale beds in southern china.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Linens are woven flax fabrics with a variety of uses. Damask linen is a…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference between the two linens. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: linens are woven flax fabrics with a variety of uses. damask linen is a handwoven fabric produced in northern ireland. it has an intricate jacquard pattern and is primarily used for fine table coverings. burlap linen is a machine-made fabric produced in factories. it has a coarse, loose weave and is primarily used to package goods and reinforce wall plaster.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: On February 14, 1912, Arizona became the 48th state to join the US. On…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the order in which arizona and alaska became us states. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: on february 14, 1912, arizona became the 48th state to join the us. on january 3, 1959, alaska became the 49th state to join the us. a new 49-star us flag was unveiled later that year.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Galapagos tortoises are the largest tortoises in the world. They live o…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize how many islands in the galapagos archipelago have galapagos tortoises living on them. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: galapagos tortoises are the largest tortoises in the world. they live on seven islands in the galapagos archipelago, ecuador. the archipelago has a total of nineteen islands.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Marcus Brown is a prominent American cellist who graduated from the Jui…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize brown's most well-known achievement. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: marcus brown is a prominent american cellist who graduated from the juilliard school. he has won multiple international cello competitions. he is best known for a 2016 youtube video that has been viewed over five million times. the video shows him performing cello loops no. 3 , an original work by composer henry park. brown combines cello playing and electronic looping in the video.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Visions in Bloom was a 2019 exhibition at the Brooklyn Museum in New Yo…
searchText: the student wants to describe how kente cloth is used in threads of memory . which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: visions in bloom was a 2019 exhibition at the brooklyn museum in new york. it featured ten artworks by ghanaian artist akosua mensah. one of these works is a painting titled threads of memory . threads of memory depicts four seated figures. the figures wear scarves made of woven kente cloth strips stitched to the painting's canvas.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Echolocation is the use of sound waves to detect objects by listening f…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference between the behavior of bats and that of toothed whales. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: echolocation is the use of sound waves to detect objects by listening for their echoes. this process involves emitting clicks or calls and interpreting the returning signals. bats emit ultrasonic clicks while flying. this behavior helps them locate flying insects in total darkness. toothed whales emit click trains while swimming. this behavior helps them navigate around obstacles in murky waters.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In World War I, US soldiers who were members of the Cherokee Nation in…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between the cherokee code talkers and the comanche code talkers. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in world war i, us soldiers who were members of the cherokee nation in north carolina participated in the cherokee code talkers program. the cherokee code talkers were trained to relay coded military information in their native language. in world war ii, the us marine corps recruited comanche soldiers to transmit coded messages in their native language. these soldiers were known as the comanche code talkers.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: "Longplayer" is a musical composition by experimental musician Jem Fine…
searchText: the student wants to indicate how long jem finer's musical piece will last. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: "longplayer" is a musical composition by experimental musician jem finer. a specially designed computer system at trinity buoy wharf in london, england, began playing the piece in 2000. it is scheduled to stop playing the piece in 3000. the performance will last 1,000 years. it will be among the longest continuous musical performances ever planned.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The painter Amrita Sher-Gil is one of the most important artists of twe…
searchText: the student wants to introduce sher-gil to an audience unfamiliar with the artist. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the painter amrita sher-gil is one of the most important artists of twentieth-century south asia. she was born in budapest, hungary, in 1913, to a hungarian mother and a sikh indian father. she is best known for her bold and emotionally expressive portraits of rural indian life. three girls (1935) features three young women seated together against an ochre background. one woman wears a red sari, while the other two wear muted greens and grays.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The standard QWERTY keyboard layout was designed in the 1870s to preven…
searchText: the student wants to explain an advantage of the dvorak keyboard. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the standard qwerty keyboard layout was designed in the 1870s to prevent the keys of early typewriters from jamming. because common letter pairs are spaced apart, typists' fingers must travel longer distances on a qwerty keyboard. the dvorak keyboard layout, developed as an alternative to qwerty, places the most common letters on the home row. because typists' fingers stay on or near the home row, the dvorak layout supports faster typing with less hand fatigue.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 2017, paleontology professor Steve Brusatte and his students from th…
searchText: the student wants to explain the origin of the species' name. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 2017, paleontology professor steve brusatte and his students from the university of edinburgh in scotland made a discovery. the team found a partial dinosaur skeleton at a site on scotland's isle of skye. the skeleton belonged to a dinosaur species that lived approximately 166 million years ago. the new species was named dearc sgiathanach to honor the location where it was found, using a scottish gaelic phrase meaning "winged reptile from skye."

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Archaeologist Dr. Maya Rodriguez founded the Andean Heritage Project to…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between the toro muerto paintings and the cerro mulato paintings. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: archaeologist dr. maya rodriguez founded the andean heritage project to preserve the cultural history of regions in peru. the andean heritage project has overseen a digital documentation effort to create photographic records of ancient rock art in peru. paintings found at the toro muerto site are included in the documentation. the toro muerto paintings feature geometric patterns and dance figures. paintings found at the cerro mulato site are included in the documentation. the cerro mulato paintings feature what are thought to be the earliest depictions of agricultural calendars in the region.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: A tagine is a cooking vessel that originated in North Africa during the…
searchText: the student wants to describe the tagine's shape. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: a tagine is a cooking vessel that originated in north africa during the medieval era. the tagine's shallow, circular base helps to braise food slowly. the tagine's tall, conical lid helps to trap steam and return condensed moisture to the dish. paula wolfert is a cook and culinary historian. her book the food of morocco (2011) traces the history of the tagine.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The Kingdom of Aksum was a powerful trading civilization in the Horn of…
searchText: the student wants to present the gateway hypothesis to an audience unfamiliar with the kingdom of aksum. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the kingdom of aksum was a powerful trading civilization in the horn of africa that flourished from roughly the first to the seventh century ce. aksum issued its own gold and silver coinage and dominated commerce along the red sea. some scholars endorse what is called the gateway hypothesis. the gateway hypothesis holds that aksum served as the principal commercial link between the roman mediterranean and india. archaeologist stuart munro-hay cites finds of roman amphorae and indian beads at aksumite sites as supporting evidence.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Stomata are tiny pores on the surface of plant leaves. Stomata allow ga…
searchText: the student wants to specify how many stomata per square millimeter the common bean has on its lower leaf surface. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: stomata are tiny pores on the surface of plant leaves. stomata allow gases such as carbon dioxide and oxygen to enter and exit the leaf. members of the same plant species typically have similar stomatal densities. the common bean ( phaseolus vulgaris ) and the soybean ( glycine max ) are species of legumes. the common bean has roughly 280 stomata per square millimeter on its lower leaf surface. the soybean has roughly 150 stomata per square millimeter on its lower leaf surface.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Some plants have evolved structures that recruit other organisms for he…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference in how the bullhorn acacia and nepenthes lowii use mutualism. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: some plants have evolved structures that recruit other organisms for help with survival. this is known as mutualism. the bullhorn acacia produces hollow thorns and protein-rich food bodies. these resources house and feed colonies of acacia ants that fiercely attack browsing herbivores. the pitcher plant nepenthes lowii secretes a sweet exudate on the underside of its lid. this exudate attracts tree shrews whose droppings supply the plant with nitrogen.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Volcanoes erupt at a point called a vent and release material both upwa…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between tephra and lava. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: volcanoes erupt at a point called a vent and release material both upward and outward. two of the materials commonly released during volcanic eruptions are tephra and lava. tephra rises higher into the atmosphere than lava does. tephra consists of fragments of rock and ash propelled by gas, falling back to the ground after being ejected. lava flows downhill as a liquid stream of molten rock.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Pachinko is a multigenerational novel by Korean American author Min Jin…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity in how critics responded to lee's novel. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: pachinko is a multigenerational novel by korean american author min jin lee that traces a family across twentieth-century korea and japan. in a review, a critic for the new york times wrote that the novel is "an old-fashioned epic whose simple, captivating storytelling draws on the tropes of family saga." a critic for the guardian called it "a quietly devastating story, told with grace and verve." a critic for the san francisco chronicle dubbed it "a sweeping work of historical fiction."

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Telegraph operators relayed crucial messages over long-distance wires i…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the order in which the two telegraph operators began their careers. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: telegraph operators relayed crucial messages over long-distance wires in the nineteenth century. before widespread electrification, telegraph offices were staffed by skilled operators who keyed and decoded morse code. sarah bagley was the chief operator at the lowell telegraph office in massachusetts. she held this position from 1846 to 1855. mary macaulay was the chief operator at the halifax telegraph office in nova scotia. she held this position from 1869 to 1898.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Faith Ringgold is an American artist known for making many story quilts…
searchText: the student wants to describe ringgold's tar beach to an audience already familiar with story quilts. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: faith ringgold is an american artist known for making many story quilts. a story quilt is a pieced fabric work that incorporates handwritten or stenciled text panels alongside painted imagery. when viewed, the text panels narrate a story while the imagery illustrates the setting and characters. her story quilt tar beach (1988) depicts a harlem rooftop scene viewed from above. it is painted in acrylic on canvas with hand-dyed and pieced cotton borders.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: José Martí (1853–1895) was a Cuban writer and political activist. Ismae…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize josé martí's accomplishments before settling permanently in new york. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: josé martí (1853–1895) was a cuban writer and political activist. ismaelillo (1882) and versos sencillos (1891) are two acclaimed poetry collections that martí published before settling permanently in new york. martí relocated to new york city in 1881 but traveled extensively before making it his long-term base in the early 1890s. he is best known for his role in organizing the cuban independence movement during his new york years. his most famous later writings include the essay collection nuestra américa (1891) and the founding documents of the cuban revolutionary party (1892).

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Yaa Gyasi is a Ghanaian-born American author of novels. She published t…
searchText: the student wants to introduce the novel homegoing to an audience already familiar with yaa gyasi. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: yaa gyasi is a ghanaian-born american author of novels. she published the novel homegoing in 2016. a central character in the novel is a young woman living in eighteenth-century ghana. another central character is her half-sister, sold into slavery and taken to the american south.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Honey is a natural — and economically important — product that is obtai…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize how caucasian honey differs from italian and carniolan honey. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: honey is a natural — and economically important — product that is obtained from bees. china is a leading producer of honey. the flavor of honey varies across honeybee subspecies and the flowers they forage. italian honeybees produce mild, pale honey that is used in baking. carniolan honeybees produce mild, pale honey that is used in baking. caucasian honeybees produce dark, robust honey that is used in barbecue sauces and glazes.
