preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Coins recovered by archaeologists are divided into two categories. A co…
searchText: the student wants to contrast the number of stratified roman coins with the number of surface roman coins. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: coins recovered by archaeologists are divided into two categories. a coin discovered at a documented archaeological excavation is known as a stratified coin. all other coins recovered are known as surface coins. there have been about 8,000 recorded stratified roman coins in a recent british survey. there have been over 350,000 recorded surface roman coins in the same survey.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Scientists have long sought to determine the origin of the polished sto…
searchText: the student wants to describe how scientific understanding about the spheres' origin has evolved. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: scientists have long sought to determine the origin of the polished stone spheres found in costa rica's diquís delta. a 1943 study concluded that the spheres had been carved from gabbro boulders by indigenous artisans using stone hammers and abrasives. in 2014, a different study revealed that the surface microstructure of several spheres was consistent with the surface microstructure of obsidian samples in regional collections. that study concluded that some of the spheres had been shaped from naturally fractured volcanic glass rather than from gabbro.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Katsuko Saruhashi developed the first reliable method for measuring car…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the significance of saruhashi's development. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: katsuko saruhashi developed the first reliable method for measuring carbon dioxide in seawater in 1955. the method, known as saruhashi's table, is involved in both routine ocean monitoring and the calibration of newer techniques. saruhashi initially believed her method's main value would be limited to japanese coastal studies. her method proved essential for tracking the global movement of radioactive fallout across the pacific. tracking that fallout has led to a much fuller understanding of how ocean currents redistribute pollutants worldwide.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Generally, a metal will expand when heated. The expansion of a material…
searchText: the student wants to contrast the two samples. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: generally, a metal will expand when heated. the expansion of a material due to heating is known as thermal expansion. a 2018 study led by hiroshi tanaka and lourdes garcía tested the thermal expansion of various rods. when a 200-millimeter-long sample of copper rod was heated by 100°c, its length increased by 0.34 millimeters. when a 250-millimeter-long sample of aluminum rod was heated by 100°c, its length increased by 0.58 millimeters.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 1847, the novel Agnes Grey was published in England. According to th…
searchText: the student wants to identify the real author of agnes grey . which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 1847, the novel agnes grey was published in england. according to the novel's title page, the author's name was acton bell. acton bell was widely assumed to be a pseudonym. a pseudonym is a fake name used to conceal an author's identity. a woman named anne brontë later revealed herself as the novel's real author.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The international Fair Trade movement was founded in 1988 with the laun…
searchText: the student wants to introduce the fair trade movement to a new audience. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the international fair trade movement was founded in 1988 with the launch of the max havelaar certification mark in the netherlands. the movement promotes guaranteed minimum prices and stable contracts for smallholder farmers. it calls for environmentally responsible production practices that protect soil, water, and biodiversity on participating farms. it advocates for democratic cooperative governance among producer organizations. the fair trade usa organization was founded in 1998.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 2007, geologist Marjorie Chan studied the differences in age between…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize how young the wingate sandstone is relative to nearby formations. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 2007, geologist marjorie chan studied the differences in age between the layered sandstone formations of the colorado plateau and other regional rock units. her team dated all sandstone formations within a 100-kilometer radius of moab, utah. the wingate sandstone is roughly 200 million years old, which classifies it as early jurassic. 32 of the 41 formations in the study were classified as permian or triassic, with ages greater than 220 million years (older than the wingate sandstone). 3 of the 41 formations in the study were classified as middle or late jurassic, with ages less than 180 million years (younger than the wingate sandstone).

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In the years following World War II, Toyo Miyatake reopened his photogr…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the uniqueness of miyatake's accomplishment. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in the years following world war ii, toyo miyatake reopened his photography studio in los angeles. he began documenting the lives of japanese american families resettling on the west coast. in 1944, he published a portfolio of photographs taken inside the manzanar incarceration camp. his portfolio was the only contemporaneous photographic record of camp life made by a japanese american incarceree. it is still available to researchers in archives and online.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Hedy Lamarr (1914–2000) was a Hollywood film actress. She was known for…
searchText: the student wants to use a quotation to support a claim about lamarr's contribution to wireless technology. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: hedy lamarr (1914–2000) was a hollywood film actress. she was known for her glamorous on-screen presence and her work outside of film as an inventor. in 2014, lamarr was inducted into the national inventors hall of fame for her major contribution to wireless technology. according to film critic yvonne tasker, “lamarr could play sophistication and danger in the same glance.” according to engineer david hughes, “lamarr is the unsung pioneer of modern frequency-hopping communications.”

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Wapikoni Mobile is a traveling video studio founded in Quebec. Wapikoni…
searchText: the student wants to specify the documentary's name in atikamekw. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: wapikoni mobile is a traveling video studio founded in quebec. wapikoni means “flower” in atikamekw. it supports indigenous youth in creating short films and music recordings. it produced a documentary about caribou hunting ( atihko kweskweweh in atikamekw). it produced a song about northern lights ( wawatay nokin ) by a young anishinaabe artist.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In geology, a fluvial landform is one that has been shaped by flowing w…
searchText: the student wants to provide an explanation and an example of fluvial landforms. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in geology, a fluvial landform is one that has been shaped by flowing water. in roman mythology, fluvius is a god associated with rivers. fluvial landforms are created when rivers erode, transport, or deposit sediment. an oxbow lake is a curved, isolated body of water cut off from a river. an oxbow lake can form when a river's looping bend is bypassed by a new, straighter channel.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Sgraffito is a ceramic technique in which a layer of slip is applied ov…
searchText: the student wants to provide a quotation from odundo that explains why she used sgraffito in “untitled, asymmetric series ii.” which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: sgraffito is a ceramic technique in which a layer of slip is applied over a contrasting clay body and selectively scratched away to reveal an image. magdalene odundo is a kenyan-born british ceramicist who uses sgraffito in some of her work. “by incising lines through the surface, a sense of the breath of the vessel emerges,” she says about sgraffito. her work often draws on female figural forms, which she describes as “quiet witnesses of generational memory.” her sgraffito vessel “untitled, asymmetric series ii” (1994) features incised vertical lines that ripple along a leaning ovoid form.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Sardine is a fish that contains cobalamin, an essential nutrient for hu…
searchText: the student wants to provide an example of a fish that contains vitamin b12. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: sardine is a fish that contains cobalamin, an essential nutrient for humans. every 100 grams (g) of sardine contains 8.9 micrograms (mcg) of cobalamin. many bacteria can synthesize cobalamin, but humans cannot. humans must get cobalamin from foods, including fish and shellfish. cobalamin is also known as vitamin b12.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In the music world, the term symphony orchestra traditionally refers to…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference between the internet symphony and traditional symphony orchestras. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in the music world, the term symphony orchestra traditionally refers to a large ensemble that performs western classical repertoire in a concert hall. such orchestras are based in vienna, london, and boston. in 2007, composer tan dun founded the unconventional internet symphony. the internet symphony invites musicians worldwide to submit video performances that are assembled into collaborative virtual concerts. the internet symphony has produced installments on an uneven schedule, with major projects in 2009, 2011, and 2018.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 1912, German geophysicist Alfred Wegener proposed that the continent…
searchText: the student wants to present the study and its findings. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 1912, german geophysicist alfred wegener proposed that the continents had once been joined together. since that time, the timing of the supercontinent's breakup has been the subject of debate. in 2018, researchers conducted a study to analyze the magnetic alignment of mineral grains in ancient lava flows from southern africa. their analysis revealed that the grains preserve a magnetic signature dating to roughly 183 million years ago. the researchers concluded that the breakup of gondwana began in the early jurassic period.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: A pulley is a simple machine consisting of a grooved wheel and a rope o…
searchText: the student wants to contrast fixed pulleys and movable pulleys. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: a pulley is a simple machine consisting of a grooved wheel and a rope or belt. the wheel turns about an axle while the rope runs through the groove. a fixed pulley is attached to a stationary support and does not move with the load. a movable pulley is attached directly to the load and moves with it. in a fixed pulley, the rope changes the direction of the applied force but does not reduce the force needed to lift the load. in a movable pulley, the rope is anchored at one end so that the applied force can be roughly half the weight of the load.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Cajamarca is a province in northern Peru. Provinces are administrative…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the size of cajamarca. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: cajamarca is a province in northern peru. provinces are administrative divisions responsible for coordinating local services such as public transportation. cajamarca covers an area of roughly 2,979 km². public transportation is one example of a service provinces help organize. the cajamarca region is divided into 13 provinces.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Wharton Esherick (1887–1970) was an American sculptor and furniture mak…
searchText: the student wants to describe the music stand to an audience unfamiliar with wharton esherick. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: wharton esherick (1887–1970) was an american sculptor and furniture maker. he trained as a painter before turning to woodworking in the 1920s. he received the gold medal from the american institute of architects in 1958. the philadelphia museum of art owns a music stand that esherick built from cherry wood. the stand has three curving legs that taper to delicate points and a slender, asymmetrical top.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: From Earth, the meteors in a meteor shower appear to stream outward fro…
searchText: the student wants to explain the origin of the leonid meteor shower’s name. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: from earth, the meteors in a meteor shower appear to stream outward from one small region of the sky. that region is called the meteor shower’s radiant. the leonid meteor shower is visible across much of earth in november. most meteor showers, including the leonids, are named after the location of their radiant. the leonid radiant lies within the constellation leo.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Engineers have developed a thermal-storage brick that can retain about…
searchText: the student wants to specify how the wax enables heat storage. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: engineers have developed a thermal-storage brick that can retain about 88% of its heat after ten days. the brick is made from a paraffin-based wax (a substance that melts when heated and hardens at cool temperatures). when the wax is in a liquid state, heat flows into and out of the brick. when the wax is in a solid state, heat stops flowing and remains locked inside the brick. the stored heat can be released later by warming the brick above the wax’s melting point.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Termites can construct tall mounds that maintain a stable interior temp…
searchText: the student wants to begin a narrative about the creation of the building modules. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: termites can construct tall mounds that maintain a stable interior temperature even in scorching deserts. in 2019, engineer lucia patel was inspired by termite mounds to design self-cooling building modules. over the next two years, she designed, prototyped, tested, and refined her modules. each module includes a network of internal vents that release warm air through openings at the top. cooler air is drawn in through openings at the base, lowering the interior temperature without using electricity.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The Pan-African Heritage Repository (PAHR) is led by archivist Yaa Mens…
searchText: the student wants to explain an advantage of pahr’s repository being digital. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the pan-african heritage repository (pahr) is led by archivist yaa mensah. mensah directed a project to build an online repository of manuscripts related to the history of west african literary traditions. pahr digitized over 8,000 manuscripts, including the journals of west african poets and historians. building the repository didn’t require manuscripts to be removed from the libraries that housed them. researchers now have far broader access to these manuscripts.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Eight species of pangolin exist today. Four pangolin species live in su…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two pangolin species. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: eight species of pangolin exist today. four pangolin species live in sub-saharan africa. one of those species is the giant pangolin. its scientific name is smutsia gigantea . another of those species is the white-bellied pangolin. its scientific name is phataginus tricuspis .

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 1872, Hudson River School painter Albert Bierstadt completed Mount C…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two paintings. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 1872, hudson river school painter albert bierstadt completed mount corcoran . his expansive painting (61 × 96 inches) depicts a snow-capped sierra nevada peak above a calm alpine lake. in 2017, diné artist emmi whitehorse completed lake powell suite . whitehorse’s expansive painting (60 × 96 inches) was inspired in part by bierstadt’s tradition of grand landscape painting. it evokes the desert southwest through layered, semi-abstract forms.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Olms are a salamander species with two distinct populations. Black olms…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference between black olms and white olms. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: olms are a salamander species with two distinct populations. black olms live near the surface of streams and have darkly pigmented skin. white olms live in deep underwater caves and have lost most of their skin pigmentation. white olms have especially long, slender heads with many sensory pits along the sides. these pits help white olms detect prey in the absence of light.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The Long Road is a sculpture by Mexican American artist Rosalba Cordero…
searchText: the student wants to use a quotation from cordero to explain what the sculpture represents. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the long road is a sculpture by mexican american artist rosalba cordero. it is a tall column of woven copper wire shaped like a stalk of corn. corn has long been a central crop in many mexican farming communities. cordero says that the sculpture honors “the generations of farmers whose labor has sustained communities on both sides of the border.” overall, cordero wants her works to “invite viewers to consider the histories of labor, land, and migration.”

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Tomás Saraceno is an Argentine artist who uses suspended materials as h…
searchText: the student wants to describe cloud cities to an audience unfamiliar with tomás saraceno. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: tomás saraceno is an argentine artist who uses suspended materials as his primary medium of expression. he created his work cloud cities in 2011. cloud cities consisted of interconnected transparent spheres suspended high above the gallery floor. visitors could climb into the spheres and walk among them on a network of cables.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Materials scientist Nadia Okafor was developing a coating for satellite…
searchText: the student wants to contrast the thermal conductivity of aerogel composites with that of copper composites. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: materials scientist nadia okafor was developing a coating for satellite components used in cold and hot environments. she measured the thermal conductivity, or ability to transfer heat, of the materials she planned to use. okafor found that aerogel composites transferred almost no heat and had a thermal conductivity of 0.015 w/(m·k). she found that copper composites transferred large amounts of heat and had a thermal conductivity of 400 w/(m·k). a material with high thermal conductivity moves heat through it more readily than one with low thermal conductivity.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Neutrinos are tiny subatomic particles that travel through space and pa…
searchText: the student wants to present icecube’s aim and methodology. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: neutrinos are tiny subatomic particles that travel through space and pass through earth in vast numbers. the icecube neutrino observatory is a physics project that began operating in 2010. icecube’s goal is to detect and analyze high-energy neutrinos arriving from deep space. icecube uses thousands of light-sensitive detectors buried within a cubic kilometer of clear antarctic ice. in 2017, icecube researchers traced a high-energy neutrino back to its source for the first time.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Started in 1947, the South African Word Wizard Championship is a nation…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference in the origins of the two words. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: started in 1947, the south african word wizard championship is a national spelling competition. the words used in the competition come from many different language families. in 2014, sipho dlamini won by correctly spelling the word “fynbos.” “fynbos” derives from the afrikaans words for “fine” and “bush.” in 2017, aaliyah naidoo won by correctly spelling the word “bandobast.” “bandobast” derives from the persian word “band-o-bast.”

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Before 1862, the US federal government had no general system for granti…
searchText: the student wants to present the significance of the homestead act to an audience unfamiliar with the history of us land policy. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: before 1862, the us federal government had no general system for granting land to ordinary settlers. public land was often sold in large parcels that only wealthy buyers could afford. this kept land ownership concentrated among a small group of buyers. the 1862 homestead act allowed adult citizens to claim 160 acres of public land in exchange for living on and farming it for five years. by 1900, hundreds of thousands of settlers had filed claims under the act.

preview: In 2019, a research team led by Jian Li measured the average wingspans of adult individuals from various bat species. Pipistrellus pipistre…
searchText: which choice most effectively uses information from the given sentences to emphasize a difference between the wingspans of the two species? in 2019, a research team led by jian li measured the average wingspans of adult individuals from various bat species. pipistrellus pipistrellus is a species of bat in the family vespertilionidae. pipistrellus pipistrellus adults had an average wingspan of 21.3 cm. pteropus vampyrus is a species of bat in the family pteropodidae. pteropus vampyrus adults had an average wingspan of 152.4 cm.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 2020, researchers Lila Hartman, Diego Rivas, and Priya Chandran were…
searchText: the student wants to present the study’s results. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 2020, researchers lila hartman, diego rivas, and priya chandran were observing wild capuchin monkeys in a costa rican forest reserve. they saw capuchins approaching researchers and shaking branches when researchers carried packs that contained fruit. they designed a study to find out whether the capuchins were intentionally signaling to the researchers in an effort to obtain the fruit. in the study trials, capuchins frequently shook branches near researchers whose packs visibly contained fruit. in the study trials, capuchins shook branches near researchers carrying empty packs much less frequently.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In the early 1900s, over 7,000 unique varieties of pears were grown in…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the decline in unique pear varieties in the us and specify why this decline occurred. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish these goals? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in the early 1900s, over 7,000 unique varieties of pears were grown in the us. the expansion of long-distance shipping in the mid-1900s narrowed the range of pears grown for the commercial market. thousands of pear varieties considered too delicate or unprofitable for shipping were lost. today, only a few pear varieties dominate us grocery stores, making up the vast majority of pears sold. the heritage pear project, based in oregon, attempts to locate and cultivate forgotten pear varieties.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 2018, archaeologists examined dog bone fragments they had recovered…
searchText: the student wants to present the anatolia study and its conclusions. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 2018, archaeologists examined dog bone fragments they had recovered from a settlement in northern anatolia. the fragments were estimated to be about 9,000 years old. a chemical analysis of the fragments revealed that the dogs had consumed substantial amounts of grain and dairy products. the diet pattern is evidence that the dogs at the settlement may have been domesticated.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Spain’s annual Las Fallas festival is held each March in the Valencia r…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity in how people in valencia and gandia celebrate las fallas. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: spain’s annual las fallas festival is held each march in the valencia region. it marks the arrival of spring and the feast day of saint joseph. crowds gather to admire and then burn large, satirical papier-mâché sculptures called fallas . in valencia, hundreds of fallas are displayed throughout the city streets before being set ablaze. in gandia, dozens of fallas are displayed throughout the city streets before being set ablaze.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In the 1940s, the Brazilian National Coffee Institute sought to reduce…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize helena costa’s achievement. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in the 1940s, the brazilian national coffee institute sought to reduce the country’s losses from a destructive coffee-leaf disease. the institute enlisted plant breeder helena costa to develop a disease-resistant coffee plant. she crossbred the susceptible commercial species coffea arabica with wild coffee relatives from africa. she succeeded in producing coffee hybrids that resisted the disease while preserving the desired flavor profile.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Yayoi Kusama is a Japanese artist well known for her immersive mirror-r…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between the souls of millions of light years away and filled with the brilliance of life . which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: yayoi kusama is a japanese artist well known for her immersive mirror-room installations. her installation infinity mirrored room — the souls of millions of light years away uses hundreds of small led bulbs. it occupies approximately 400 cubic feet of space. her installation infinity mirrored room — filled with the brilliance of life also uses hundreds of small led bulbs. it occupies approximately 1,000 cubic feet of space.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In the early 1900s, labor activists organized large strikes for better…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the order in which the two strikes occurred. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in the early 1900s, labor activists organized large strikes for better working conditions. workers in lawrence, massachusetts, went on strike at the textile mills. this strike began on january 12, 1912, and ended on march 14, 1912. workers in paterson, new jersey, went on strike at the silk mills. this strike began on february 25, 1913, and ended on july 28, 1913.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Anne Hutchinson was one of the early settlers who joined the founding o…
searchText: the student wants to specify the reason the portsmouth compact was created. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: anne hutchinson was one of the early settlers who joined the founding of rhode island. the portsmouth compact was a legal agreement among settlers who left the massachusetts bay colony. it was signed in 1638 to establish a self-governing community on aquidneck island. it states that the signers wanted to form a “civil body politic” among themselves and submit to the laws they collectively chose. hutchinson became a leading figure in the new settlement’s civic life.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Hiroshi Sugimoto’s photograph Seascape: Bay of Sagami (1990) shows a ho…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two photographs. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: hiroshi sugimoto’s photograph seascape: bay of sagami (1990) shows a horizon dividing sea and sky in long exposure. the image uses extended exposure times to convey a sense of timeless stillness across the natural world. his photograph lake superior, eagle river (2003) shows another horizon between water and sky captured under similar conditions. this image also relies on long exposures and minimal compositional elements. both works are intended to evoke contemplative stillness through the unchanging horizon line.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: 2023: Researchers in Kenya convened a symposium to address increased cr…
searchText: the student wants to make and support a claim about african elephant behavior. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: 2023: researchers in kenya convened a symposium to address increased crop raiding by african elephants on smallholder farms. over 480 documented raids in the prior year involved herds trampling fields and consuming maize stores. studies show that expanding agriculture has reduced elephants’ access to traditional foraging corridors by roughly 70%. researchers believe the loss of foraging space has driven hungry elephants closer to villages. the symposium recommended pilot deployment of beehive fences, a nonlethal deterrent, pending further field testing.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Engineer Norma Merrick Sklarek trained in modernist and international-s…
searchText: the student wants to specify why historians believe sklarek led the design of most of the civic center’s buildings. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: engineer norma merrick sklarek trained in modernist and international-style architecture in new york. sklarek worked for a large architecture firm that was contracted in 1965 to design buildings for a major california civic center. most of the civic center’s buildings exhibit modernist and international-style design. at the time, sklarek’s contributions were not publicly credited. based on the buildings’ design vocabulary, historians believe sklarek led the design of most of the civic center’s buildings.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Roughly 89% of Madagascar’s estimated 11,000 plant species are flowerin…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the different families into which madagascar’s flowering plants are classified. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: roughly 89% of madagascar’s estimated 11,000 plant species are flowering plants. flowering plants of the family orchidaceae, which includes ground orchids and epiphytic orchids, are estimated to total 1,000 species in madagascar. flowering plants of the family fabaceae, which includes legumes and acacias, are estimated to total 700 species in madagascar. some of these flowering plants’ populations are threatened by deforestation and invasive insect species.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In hydrology, a watershed is an area of land that drains all its surfac…
searchText: the student wants to provide an example of a watershed. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in hydrology, a watershed is an area of land that drains all its surface water into a single outlet. watersheds are commonly named with a two-part designation. the first part identifies the receiving body of water, while the second part identifies a defining geographic feature. the mississippi delta watershed, for example, drains into the mississippi river near its alluvial outlet. the columbia plateau watershed drains into the columbia river across a basalt plateau region. this naming convention is based on the work of an american hydrographer named john wesley powell (1834–1902).

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Cyclotrons are circular particle accelerators that produce streams of h…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the location of the first cyclotron built to produce medical isotopes. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: cyclotrons are circular particle accelerators that produce streams of high-energy particles. cyclotron beams are among the most precisely controlled particle streams ever produced. cyclotron beams are valuable tools for researchers studying nuclear reactions. the first cyclotron built specifically to produce medical isotopes was completed in 1955. it was called crocker and was located near the university of california–berkeley.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Lina Iris Viktor is a British-Liberian conceptual artist. She is based…
searchText: the student wants to describe where naru is in the painting to an audience already familiar with viktor and naru. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: lina iris viktor is a british-liberian conceptual artist. she is based in london and in new orleans, louisiana. one of viktor’s paintings features dancer akua naru. in the painting, naru is standing in profile. three gilded sculptures are arranged on the floor in front of her.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: A sprint cycling race is a short, high-intensity event of less than 1 k…
searchText: the student wants to make a generalization about ultracycling races. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: a sprint cycling race is a short, high-intensity event of less than 1 kilometer. an ultracycling race is a long-distance cycling event of more than 200 kilometers. the race across the west is a one-day, 1,448-kilometer ultracycling race in the united states. the transcontinental race is a fifteen-day, 4,000-kilometer ultracycling race across europe.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Oregon defines a wetland as a parcel of land of at least 1 acre with se…
searchText: the student wants to contrast oregon’s definition of a wetland with washington’s. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: oregon defines a wetland as a parcel of land of at least 1 acre with seasonal saturation. washington’s definition of a wetland doesn’t take parcel size into account. by its own definition, washington has over 1.5 million acres of wetlands, many in plots smaller than 1 acre. by oregon’s definition, washington has only about 900,000 acres of wetlands.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Soil scientist Asmeret Asefaw Berhe studies peatlands. Peatlands are ca…
searchText: the student wants to introduce the scientist and her field of study to a new audience. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: soil scientist asmeret asefaw berhe studies peatlands. peatlands are carbon-rich wetland ecosystems that store about 30% of global soil carbon. peatlands can host hundreds of plant species, including sphagnum mosses, sedges, and cottongrasses. the vasyugan mire is a peatland in western siberia. it is one of the largest peatlands in the world.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Iridium is a scarce and costly metal. It is used as a catalyst in indus…
searchText: the student wants to explain an advantage of the new iridium catalyst developed by priya bhatnagar and her colleagues. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: iridium is a scarce and costly metal. it is used as a catalyst in industrial chemical reactions. iridium catalysts typically require a substantial loading of iridium to perform well. researcher priya bhatnagar and her colleagues developed an iridium catalyst that disperses iridium across a graphene substrate. their catalyst was highly active and required only trace amounts of iridium (0.0005% of the atoms in the mixture).

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Iconographic analysis and structural analysis are two approaches to fil…
searchText: the student wants to present iconographic analysis to an audience unfamiliar with the concept. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: iconographic analysis and structural analysis are two approaches to film criticism. structural analysis examines how a film’s narrative components are arranged across the work. such an analysis of akira kurosawa’s rashomon might consider how its layered flashback framework destabilizes the viewer’s sense of truth. iconographic analysis considers the recurring visual symbols a film uses and their cultural meanings. such an analysis of wong kar-wai’s in the mood for love might consider how the recurring image of clocks symbolizes the characters’ entrapment in time.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Patricia Grace is a Maori author from New Zealand. Potiki is one of her…
searchText: the student wants to introduce patricia grace and her novel potiki to a new audience. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: patricia grace is a maori author from new zealand. potiki is one of her most celebrated novels. grace published the novel in 1986. it is about a maori community resisting the development of their ancestral coastal land. some of potiki ’s themes also appear in grace’s later novel cousins .

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Helen Oyeyemi is a British author. In 2014, she published Boy, Snow, Bi…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two books by helen oyeyemi. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: helen oyeyemi is a british author. in 2014, she published boy, snow, bird , a novel of twelve interlocking chapters. the chapters reframe classic fairy-tale motifs through the experiences of three women in a new england town. in 2019, she published gingerbread , a novel of nine interlocking chapters. the chapters reframe classic fairy-tale motifs through the experiences of a family in a london household.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Roxanne Swentzell is a Santa Clara Pueblo sculptor, ceramicist, and ins…
searchText: the student wants to describe the distinctive style of swentzell’s sculptures. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: roxanne swentzell is a santa clara pueblo sculptor, ceramicist, and installation artist. many of her sculptures feature pueblo women. she depicts this subject matter using rounded clay forms with exaggerated facial expressions, developing a distinctive style that has influenced many other artists. her well-known sculpture mud woman rolls on was installed at the denver art museum.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Vivian Suter is a Swiss-Argentine painter who works in Guatemala. Her w…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two works. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: vivian suter is a swiss-argentine painter who works in guatemala. her work has been exhibited in europe, the united states, and latin america, among other places. many of her paintings incorporate natural elements gathered from the forest around her studio. her painting tintin’s sister (2017) incorporates volcanic ash and wind-blown leaves. her painting nisyros (2019) incorporates clay sediments and pine needles.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 2021, museum educators at Oberlin College developed an interactive,…
searchText: the student wants to explain an advantage of the “voices of reconstruction” format. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 2021, museum educators at oberlin college developed an interactive, online format to present an exhibit on reconstruction-era newspapers. their “voices of reconstruction” website featured an interactive digital map of the southern united states. visitors were asked to focus on a region of their choice and select from that region a newspaper headline. one click took them to scanned articles, audio readings, photographs, and contextual essays. the exhibit was popular with visitors because the format let them follow their own curiosity through the material.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 2021, researchers from Stanford University documented hydrothermal v…
searchText: the student wants to explain why hydrothermal vents are inhospitable to most deep-sea organisms. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 2021, researchers from stanford university documented hydrothermal vents along the galapagos rift. a hydrothermal vent is a fissure on the seafloor that releases superheated, mineral-rich water. the water emerging from these vents can reach temperatures of 400 degrees celsius. the combination of extreme heat and dissolved minerals makes the water inhospitable to most deep-sea organisms. specialized chemosynthetic microbes are able to thrive near the vents.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Os Lusíadas was an epic poem popular in sixteenth-century Portugal. The…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the role a misconception played in the naming of a place. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: os lusíadas was an epic poem popular in sixteenth-century portugal. the poem celebrated a mythologized vision of portuguese maritime exploration around an undiscovered “cape of storms.” that same century, portuguese navigators reached a “cape” off the southern tip of africa. they named it the cape of good hope after a hopeful detail in the poem. the “cape” was actually a peninsula now known as the cape peninsula, which extends southward from the city of cape town.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Some marble façades on historic buildings in Florence have been damaged…
searchText: the student wants to explain an advantage of the laser-ablation technique. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: some marble façades on historic buildings in florence have been damaged by graffiti. conservators can erase some graffiti using chemical solvents. for deeply absorbed graffiti, solvents are not always feasible because they can discolor or weaken the marble. conservators can use a laser-ablation technique, which removes pigment layer by layer using focused light pulses. this technique is precisely controlled and largely nondestructive.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Olga Tokarczuk was awarded the 2018 Nobel Prize in Literature. Tokarczu…
searchText: the student wants to introduce flights to an audience unfamiliar with the novel and its author. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: olga tokarczuk was awarded the 2018 nobel prize in literature. tokarczuk was born in sulechów in western poland and currently lives in wrocław. many readers have singled out tokarczuk’s 2007 book flights for praise. flights is a fragmentary novel about travel, anatomy, and the human body.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Andean alpacas are domesticated camelids native to the highlands of Sou…
searchText: the student wants to present the conclusion of marcela vilca and colleagues’ 2019 study. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: andean alpacas are domesticated camelids native to the highlands of south america. a mutation in their epo gene increases red blood cell production. a mutation in their vegfa gene improves the formation of capillaries in muscle tissue. these mutations enable the alpacas to thrive in hypoxic (low-oxygen) conditions at high altitudes. in a 2019 study, marcela vilca and colleagues noted that wild vicuñas’ dna contains the same epo and vegfa mutations. vilca and colleagues concluded that the alpacas first acquired these mutations through ancient domestication from vicuña populations roughly 7,000 years ago.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Augusta Savage (1892–1962) was an African American sculptor and educato…
searchText: the student wants to explain the sculpture’s specific historical context. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: augusta savage (1892–1962) was an african american sculptor and educator. the harp (1939) is a plaster sculpture by savage. it depicts a row of singing african american figures forming the strings of a harp. it was commissioned for the 1939 new york world’s fair. the sculpture took its inspiration from the song “lift every voice and sing,” written by james weldon johnson in 1900. art historian jeffreen hayes describes the sculpture as “a monument to black creative resilience.”

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Etel Adnan is a multidisciplinary artist. In 1959, her first solo art e…
searchText: the student wants to introduce the artist’s 1989 poetry collection. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: etel adnan is a multidisciplinary artist. in 1959, her first solo art exhibition, visions of beirut , was shown at the sursock museum in beirut, lebanon. her poetry collection the arab apocalypse was published in 1989 by post-apollo press. her poetry collection sea and fog was published in 2012 by nightboat books. she lived part time in lebanon, where she was born, and part time in paris and california.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Musicians around the world have used protest songs to call attention to…
searchText: the student wants to contrast the song “refazenda” with the song “took the children away.” which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: musicians around the world have used protest songs to call attention to environmental injustices. brazilian singer gilberto gil released the protest song “refazenda” in 1975. it celebrated traditional bahian farming practices threatened by industrial agriculture in northeast brazil. australian singer-songwriter archie roach released the protest song “took the children away” in 1990. it exposed the forced removal of indigenous australian children from their families during the twentieth century.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The Aral Sea, located in Central Asia, was once one of the largest inla…
searchText: the student wants to specify the reason the aral sea’s area changed. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the aral sea, located in central asia, was once one of the largest inland bodies of water on earth. ancient travelers described it as a vast lake stretching across the horizon. today, the aral sea covers only a fraction of its former area. beginning in the 1960s, government planners diverted the rivers that fed the sea in order to irrigate large cotton fields, causing the sea to shrink dramatically.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Lichens, organisms formed by a partnership between fungi and algae, gro…
searchText: the student wants to present the study and its findings. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: lichens, organisms formed by a partnership between fungi and algae, grow in many climates, including tropical and polar regions. lichen diversity has been poorly documented in the cold, windswept islands of the south atlantic. botanist tomas halvorsen led a study to determine how many lichen species inhabit the falkland islands. halvorsen and his team sampled lichens at coastal and inland sites over twelve months. the team recorded 156 lichen species at the surveyed sites.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Both alkaline and zinc-carbon batteries are widely used disposable batt…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference between alkaline batteries and zinc-carbon batteries. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: both alkaline and zinc-carbon batteries are widely used disposable batteries that convert chemical energy into electricity. alkaline batteries use potassium hydroxide as their electrolyte. zinc-carbon batteries use ammonium chloride as their electrolyte. to power high-drain devices like digital cameras, alkaline batteries deliver energy efficiently over a long period. zinc-carbon batteries are best suited to low-drain devices like remote controls and clocks.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Many soil microbes break down organic matter into compounds plants can…
searchText: the student wants to present the study to an audience already familiar with nutrient cycling. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: many soil microbes break down organic matter into compounds plants can absorb. this process is called nutrient cycling. researchers use nutrient cycling rates to assess soil health in farmland. soil ecologist mariana costa’s team measured nutrient cycling in coffee plantations in the highlands of costa rica to evaluate how shade-tree planting affects soil health. the study found that plots with shade trees had 38% higher nutrient cycling rates than open plots.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: 1872: The US Congress designated Yellowstone as the first national park…
searchText: the student wants to specify the order in which different federal entities were given the authority to manage us national parks. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: 1872: the us congress designated yellowstone as the first national park in the united states. 1890: congress established yosemite national park, transferring federal stewardship to the us army. 1916: congress created the national park service (nps), giving it authority over yellowstone, yosemite, and other federal parklands. the nps’s first director, stephen mather, expanded park infrastructure and visitor services. the nps remains the federal agency responsible for the national parks.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Coastal estuaries provide a steady supply of small fish, crabs, and shr…
searchText: the student wants to make and support a generalization about shorebirds. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: coastal estuaries provide a steady supply of small fish, crabs, and shrimp. this abundant prey supports the development of larger, healthier offspring for shorebirds that nest near estuaries. on average, 78 percent of shorebird chicks raised near coastal estuaries reach adulthood. inland wetlands are dominated by a narrower range of insect prey. on average, only 45 percent of shorebird chicks raised at inland wetlands reach adulthood.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Funicular railways are inclined cable railways that carry passengers up…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between the ages of the funiculars. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: funicular railways are inclined cable railways that carry passengers up and down steep slopes. many funiculars were built in the 19th and 20th centuries in mountainous cities around the world. the reisszug is a famous funicular in salzburg, austria. it has operated since at least 1515 ce. the niterói funicular is a famous funicular in valparaíso, chile. it has operated since 1883 ce.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Most, but not all, of the carbon stored in the deep ocean comes from si…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the aim of the research study. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: most, but not all, of the carbon stored in the deep ocean comes from sinking organic matter from surface waters. oceanographer lena brandt from stockholm university wondered if some of the unaccounted-for deep-ocean carbon could be coming from underwater volcanic vents. in 2017, she analyzed data from the deep-sea probe nereid. nereid measured dissolved gases and minerals it encountered while drifting near vents on the mid-atlantic ridge. based on the nereid data, brandt confirmed her suspicion that underwater vents contribute substantial carbon to the deep ocean.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Some 19th-century European reformers sought to redesign workplaces by f…
searchText: the student wants to explain the goal of new lanark using a quotation from robert owen. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: some 19th-century european reformers sought to redesign workplaces by founding cooperative villages. a cooperative village is a community in which workers jointly own factories and share the profits of their labor. one such community was new lanark in southern scotland. it was founded in 1786 and reorganized in 1800 by social reformer robert owen. owen wrote in an 1816 address that his goal for new lanark was “to form a new character in the rising generation, by surrounding them from infancy with circumstances calculated to produce only good and useful habits.”

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Beowulf is an Old English epic poem from early medieval Anglo-Saxon Eng…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize whose point of view the novel is told from. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: beowulf is an old english epic poem from early medieval anglo-saxon england. in beowulf , the character grendel is often portrayed as a monstrous villain. grendel is a 1971 novel by john gardner. the novel is a retelling of the epic poem from grendel’s point of view. it often portrays grendel as a misunderstood, philosophical outsider.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Ursula K. Le Guin’s 1968 novel A Wizard of Earthsea features two append…
searchText: the student wants to contrast the purposes of the two appendices in a wizard of earthsea . which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: ursula k. le guin’s 1968 novel a wizard of earthsea features two appendices. the novel opens with a chronological list of major historical events in the fictional archipelago of earthsea. this timeline orients readers to the long arc of history before they enter the story. the novel closes with a glossary defining terms in the invented old speech language. that glossary lets readers revisit and reflect on the meaning of phrases the characters used.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 2020, Carlos Mendoza and colleagues in Spain and Portugal studied th…
searchText: the student wants to summarize the study. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 2020, carlos mendoza and colleagues in spain and portugal studied the effect of ambient noise on swallows’ success in feeding chicks at urban nests. they found that as noise levels increased, the parent birds completed fewer successful feedings per hour. in quiet conditions, swallows averaged about 22 successful feedings per hour. in moderate traffic noise, the average dropped to roughly 14 feedings per hour. in heavy traffic noise, swallows averaged only around 7 feedings per hour.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Mineralogists classify quartz crystals based on the impurities that col…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference between amethyst and citrine. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: mineralogists classify quartz crystals based on the impurities that color them. amethyst is colored primarily by trace iron and natural radiation. it accounts for roughly 12 percent of recognized colored quartz varieties. citrine is colored primarily by trace aluminum and heat. it accounts for roughly 5 percent of recognized colored quartz varieties.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In hydrology, the volume of a lake can be described in units called cub…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the volume of lake superior. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in hydrology, the volume of a lake can be described in units called cubic kilometers. one cubic kilometer is equal to one billion cubic meters of water. the volume of lake tahoe is 156 cubic kilometers. the volume of lake superior is 12,100 cubic kilometers.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Cetaceans, which include whales, dolphins, and porpoises, live entirely…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the fossil’s significance. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: cetaceans, which include whales, dolphins, and porpoises, live entirely in water. cetaceans are descended not from fish but from four-legged, land-dwelling mammals. egyptian paleontologist ahmed hassan recently unearthed a fossil with four legs, a long flattened tail, and the skull of an early whale. hassan refers to his rare find as a “transitional fossil.” the fossil illustrates an early stage in the evolution of cetaceans from their land-dwelling ancestors.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Investigative journalists are reporters who conduct in-depth research t…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the thoroughness of nellie bly’s investigation of blackwell’s island asylum. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: investigative journalists are reporters who conduct in-depth research to uncover wrongdoing. nellie bly was an investigative journalist who exposed conditions inside the blackwell’s island asylum in 1887. she went undercover by feigning mental illness in order to be committed to the asylum. she lived alongside the patients for ten days, recording firsthand observations of food, staff conduct, and treatment of inmates. her newspaper series and book ten days in a mad-house (1887) prompted public outcry and a grand jury investigation.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: When law students discuss real cases publicly online, they may violate…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the study’s methodology. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: when law students discuss real cases publicly online, they may violate attorney-client privilege. hannah park led a study to determine how many law schools have student policies that address online discussion of cases. park and her team reviewed 178 law school websites, examining publicly available student handbooks. only twenty-three law schools had guidelines that explicitly mention online case discussion, and only nine defined what constitutes acceptable conduct.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: 1845: British explorer John Franklin and his crew embarked on an expedi…
searchText: the student wants to provide a historical overview of the franklin expedition. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: 1845: british explorer john franklin and his crew embarked on an expedition to map the northwest passage in the canadian arctic. 1846: franklin’s ships erebus and terror became trapped in pack ice off king william island. 1848: after more than a year stranded in the ice, the surviving crew abandoned the ships and perished while attempting to reach the mainland on foot. 1859: a search party led by francis mcclintock recovered written records confirming the expedition’s fate. 2014: the wreck of erebus was located in queen maud gulf. 2016: the wreck of terror was discovered south of king william island.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Ichthyosaurs were marine reptiles that existed millions of years ago. I…
searchText: the student wants to present the study and its findings. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: ichthyosaurs were marine reptiles that existed millions of years ago. in a 2022 study, priya subramanian analyzed fragments of ichthyosaur vertebrae located in the atacama desert of chile. she was initially unsure whether the bones belonged to a single individual or to multiple individuals. she used isotope-ratio analysis to determine that all the fragments had identical chemical signatures. she concluded that the bones belonged to one large adult.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Traditionally, paper manufacturers have whitened pulp by bleaching it w…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference between the two approaches to whitening paper pulp. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: traditionally, paper manufacturers have whitened pulp by bleaching it with chlorine compounds. chlorine bleaching produces wastewater that contains toxic byproducts harmful to aquatic life. materials engineer daria volkov developed a process for whitening pulp without chlorine. volkov treated the pulp with ozone gas, which breaks down lignin without producing toxic residues. ozone treatment leaves only oxygen as a byproduct in the wastewater.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Indian astronomer Aryabhata studied the motions of celestial bodies. He…
searchText: the student wants to specify when aryabhata published his rotation theory. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: indian astronomer aryabhata studied the motions of celestial bodies. he proposed that earth rotates on its own axis, causing the apparent daily motion of the stars. aryabhata published his rotation theory in 499 ce.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The growth rate of mangrove forests depends on tidal flooding patterns.…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between mangroves and seagrass meadows along belize’s coast. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the growth rate of mangrove forests depends on tidal flooding patterns. in the rainy season, mangrove forests along belize’s coast capture and store atmospheric carbon at a rapid pace. the growing mangroves contribute to coastal stabilization each rainy season. adjacent seagrass meadows also capture and store atmospheric carbon offshore. in 2019, marine biologist diego ramirez found that seagrass growth depends not on tidal flooding but on consistent water clarity. because water clarity is consistent, growing seagrass meadows contribute to coastal stabilization all year.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Tromsø is a city located about 200 miles north of the Arctic Circle in…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize how long the center has existed. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: tromsø is a city located about 200 miles north of the arctic circle in northern norway. it is the traditional home of many sámi people. the sámi cultural heritage center was established by community members in 1985. its mission is stated on its website: “as a heritage center for the sámi people and by the sámi people, we work to provide learning experiences that emphasize sámi ways of knowing while exploring modern concepts of cultural transmission.”

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The mode of a piece of music is the underlying scale pattern of its mel…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference between the modes of the two pieces. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the mode of a piece of music is the underlying scale pattern of its melodies. ionian mode is structured by a pattern of whole and half steps that produces a bright, major sound. phrygian mode is structured by a pattern that places a half step between the first and second scale degrees, producing a darker sound. the renaissance hymn tallis’s canon uses ionian mode. the spanish flamenco piece soleá uses phrygian mode.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Just like cities have city flags, some neighborhoods have neighborhood…
searchText: the student wants to make and support a generalization about the effect of adopting a neighborhood flag. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: just like cities have city flags, some neighborhoods have neighborhood flags. over forty us neighborhoods have adopted new flags since 2018. the highland park neighborhood in detroit, michigan, adopted a new flag after residents organized a community design contest. highland park’s new flag features local landmarks and colors selected by residents. residents consider the new flag to be a meaningful symbol of neighborhood identity.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Mira Lehr was an American multimedia artist. She often explored themes…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference between the two installations. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: mira lehr was an american multimedia artist. she often explored themes of nature and ecology in her work. a multimedia work combines two or more artistic mediums. lehr completed the installation a walk in the garden in 2017, using burned paper and acrylic. she completed the installation high water mark in 2021, using ink and gunpowder.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Rebecca Belmore is an Anishinaabe multidisciplinary artist. The Anishin…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the order in which two of rebecca belmore’s installations were displayed. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: rebecca belmore is an anishinaabe multidisciplinary artist. the anishinaabe are an indigenous people of north america. belmore’s installations address themes of identity and place and have appeared in more than 60 exhibitions. her 2014 installation trace was displayed at the canadian museum for human rights. her 2019 installation turbulent water was displayed at the remai modern.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Few records survive describing the life of Italian violinist Maddalena…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the effect modern scholarship had on building lombardini sirmen’s reputation. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: few records survive describing the life of italian violinist maddalena lombardini sirmen (1745–1818). she was trained at the ospedale dei mendicanti in venice and gained regional recognition as a performer and composer. she published six string quartets and several violin concertos during her career. her reputation has grown substantially in recent decades thanks to scholarly recordings and editions of her compositions by modern musicologists. in 2018, the ensemble allegra released an acclaimed recording of her quartets that reached new audiences worldwide.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: When a massive star exhausts its nuclear fuel, its core can collapse an…
