searchText: the student wants to recount the sequence of events proposed by the astronomers. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: when a massive star exhausts its nuclear fuel, its core can collapse and trigger a supernova explosion. in a 2021 study, astronomers proposed that a star they named vega-7 underwent such a collapse roughly 12,000 years ago. their simulations indicated that vega-7’s core would have collapsed first, releasing a burst of neutrinos. shortly afterward, a shock wave would have torn through the star’s outer layers. the astronomers hypothesized that the ejected gas later formed the nebula now observed in that region.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In a 2019 study, researchers in Oregon investigated how often urban red…
searchText: the student wants to present the methods used in the 2019 study. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in a 2019 study, researchers in oregon investigated how often urban red foxes returned to known foraging sites during the autumn season. the researchers established 24 monitored bait stations across the study area and fitted 92 red foxes with gps collars. the collars logged each visit a fox made to a bait station. 41 foxes (44.6%) returned to only one station. 11 foxes (12.0%) returned to 8 or more stations. foxes with smaller home ranges tended to return to multiple stations.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Iridescence is a phenomenon in which certain butterfly wings appear to…
searchText: the student wants to compare the causes of iridescence proposed in the two hypotheses. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: iridescence is a phenomenon in which certain butterfly wings appear to shift in color when viewed from different angles. to explain how this happens, biologist liana voss proposes the structural-grating hypothesis. according to voss’s hypothesis, microscopic ridges on the wing scales scatter light to produce the color shift. chemist daichi mori posits the pigment-layer hypothesis. according to mori’s hypothesis, thin pigment layers within the scales selectively absorb and re-emit light, producing the color shift.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Malapportionment is the over- or underrepresentation (relative to popul…
searchText: the student wants to refute a claim that malapportionment in the argentine senate favors densely populated provinces. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: malapportionment is the over- or underrepresentation (relative to population size) of electoral districts in a governing body. it occurs in many representative legislatures. there are 100 seats in the senate of the argentine congress. seats are distributed by a formula that allocates exactly three senators to each of the 23 provinces and the autonomous city of buenos aires, regardless of population. densely populated provinces, such as buenos aires province, receive the same number of senators as sparsely populated provinces, such as tierra del fuego.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Geologists estimate that the number of active and dormant volcanoes on…
searchText: the student wants to make and support a generalization about the summits of seafloor volcanoes. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish these goals? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: geologists estimate that the number of active and dormant volcanoes on earth's seafloor is in the tens of thousands. loihi seamount, off the coast of hawaii, is one of many seafloor volcanoes whose summit has shifted upward over time. loihi seamount's summit was once located more than 1,500 meters below the ocean's surface. the seamount's summit is now positioned roughly 975 meters below the surface and continues to rise.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The Cajun are a group of French-speaking people who have lived in parts…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the duration and purpose of boudreaux's and landry's work. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the cajun are a group of french-speaking people who have lived in parts of southern louisiana since the 18th century. cajun culture is influenced by french acadian, west african, and indigenous traditions. marie boudreaux is a cajun musician, educator, and preservationist. she founded the cajun music heritage center of eunice, louisiana, in 1998. étienne landry is a cajun chef, oral historian, and preservationist. he founded the cajun foodways institute of lafayette, louisiana, in 1998.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: An atmospheric river is a long, narrow region of intense water vapor tr…
searchText: the student wants to present the study's findings to an audience already familiar with atmospheric rivers. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: an atmospheric river is a long, narrow region of intense water vapor transport in the atmosphere. in 2023, a team of researchers analyzed records of forty large precipitation events along the pacific coast of north america. atmospheric rivers can occur in subtropical and temperate latitudes. the team found that precipitation events fueled by atmospheric rivers were also likely to coincide with elevated coastal sea-surface temperatures. one explanation for this relationship is that warmer coastal waters supply additional water vapor that the atmospheric rivers then transport inland.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Online recipe-recommendation systems suggest dishes based on user prefe…
searchText: the student wants to explain a drawback of ingredient-based recipe-recommendation systems. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: online recipe-recommendation systems suggest dishes based on user preferences. ingredient-based systems rely on the chemical compounds present in ingredients. these compounds can shift dramatically depending on ripeness, sourcing, and storage. recommendation engines based on chemical profiles may therefore mismatch dishes that users actually find appealing. cuisine-tag-based systems, which classify dishes by stable cultural categories, tend to be steadier predictors of user satisfaction.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: A supply chain is the linked sequence of producers, transporters, and s…
searchText: the student wants to provide an overview of the vanilla supply chain. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: a supply chain is the linked sequence of producers, transporters, and sellers that brings a product to market. geographer lisa carbone studies the modern supply chain of vanilla. at one end of the vanilla chain are smallholder farmers in madagascar. at the other end are bakeries and dessert manufacturers in france that purchase the high-priced cured vanilla. according to carbone, this chain emerged in the 1990s as european demand surged.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Leaders of the Republic of Texas declared independence from Mexico in M…
searchText: the student wants to place the 1836 texas declaration of independence in the context of childress's shifting political beliefs. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: leaders of the republic of texas declared independence from mexico in march 1836. the accompanying texas declaration of independence was drafted by lawyer george childress. earlier in his career, childress had publicly opposed any break with the mexican government. the mexican government's 1835 dissolution of the federalist 1824 constitution alarmed childress. after this dissolution, childress reversed his position and joined the independence movement.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: American writers Ralph Waldo Emerson and Henry David Thoreau frequently…
searchText: the student wants to make a generalization about the kind of study conducted by patel, owusu, and reyes. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: american writers ralph waldo emerson and henry david thoreau frequently exchanged journals and essays in the 1840s. some essays now attributed to one writer may have included substantial passages by the other. the precise authorship of certain paragraphs in essays such as thoreau's "walking" is disputed. computational linguists patel, owusu, and reyes used statistical stylometry to analyze word-frequency patterns in the contested texts. they concluded that the opening of "walking" was most likely written by thoreau alone.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Harper's Weekly magazine began publication in 1857. The magazine covere…
searchText: the student wants to introduce mei-ling carter's book to an audience already familiar with harper's weekly . which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: harper's weekly magazine began publication in 1857. the magazine covered politics, current events, and illustration. in 2018, historian mei-ling carter published the book frontline reporters of "harper's weekly" . the book examines women correspondents whose dispatches appeared in the magazine during the 1860s. one reporter featured is sarah talbot, whose civil war dispatches were widely read.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The traditional Maltese luzzu is a small fishing boat recognizable by i…
searchText: the student wants to make a generalization about the materials used in new luzzus . which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the traditional maltese luzzu is a small fishing boat recognizable by its bright paint and protective eye motifs on the bow. luzzus were used primarily for coastal fishing around the maltese islands. contemporary boatbuilders in malta use both modern and traditional materials when constructing new luzzus . most components used are traditional, such as locally cut wood and hand-stitched sailcloth. some components, such as marine-grade epoxies and synthetic ropes, are modern.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Limnologists are scientists who study inland bodies of water. Some limn…
searchText: the student wants to make and support a generalization about limnologists' definition of a lake. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish these goals? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: limnologists are scientists who study inland bodies of water. some limnologists define a lake as any inland body of standing water. using that definition, they determined that finland has roughly 188,000 lakes. other limnologists require a lake to be at least 1 hectare in surface area and at least 3 meters deep. using that definition, they determined that finland has roughly 56,000 lakes.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: French scholar Georges Cuvier described fossilized mammoth bones in his…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the significance of the 2008 discovery to cuvier's reputation. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: french scholar georges cuvier described fossilized mammoth bones in his 1796 paper on extinct species. cuvier earned a reputation as the first scientist to formally identify the woolly mammoth as a distinct extinct species. in 2008, archaeologists in yakutia, siberia, uncovered a carved mammoth tusk in a cave that had been seasonally used by humans. researcher anya volkov dated stone tools nearby and concluded that local indigenous groups had identified and worked with mammoth remains as early as 9000 bce.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Embodied cognition is the idea that thinking is grounded in the body's…
searchText: the student wants to compare varela's hypothesis with wu's. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: embodied cognition is the idea that thinking is grounded in the body's interactions with the environment. 1991: cognitive scientist francisco varela proposed that perception and concept formation depend on bodily action. in varela's view, only certain cognitive processes—chiefly sensorimotor ones—are essentially embodied. 2007: philosopher diane wu published the broad embodiment hypothesis (beh). according to beh, all cognitive systems, including abstract reasoning, are essentially embodied.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Neuroscientists Mira Bansal and Tomás Reyes sought to better understand…
searchText: the student wants to summarize the study's findings. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: neuroscientists mira bansal and tomás reyes sought to better understand how the auditory cortex processes complex sounds. they built a study using computational models of cortical activity. they found that specific cortical fibers let the brain switch between two listening modes. in one mode, cells in the auditory cortex capture a snapshot of the sound at a given moment. in the other mode, those cells track changes in the sound across time.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In a 2024 study, agricultural ecologist Ravi Mehta and colleagues teste…
searchText: the student wants to present the findings of the study. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in a 2024 study, agricultural ecologist ravi mehta and colleagues tested the use of intercropped lavender rows in california vineyards. intercropping involves planting a secondary crop between rows of a primary crop. finding: vineyards with lavender rows showed 48% greater beneficial-insect activity than control vineyards. finding: each square meter of lavender row produced approximately 70 grams of marketable dried lavender.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Factors influencing brood size (the number of eggs laid at one time) ha…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the aim of the research study. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: factors influencing brood size (the number of eggs laid at one time) have been well studied in songbirds but not in salamanders. a team led by hana park of the university of oslo investigated which factors influence salamander brood size. park's team gathered brood-size and habitat data for more than 2,800 salamander species. the team analyzed the data using statistical regression. larger brood size was associated with cooler, more seasonally variable environments.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Archaeologist Renée Souza and colleagues argue that early Polynesian se…
searchText: the student wants to summarize the star-path hypothesis. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: archaeologist renée souza and colleagues argue that early polynesian settlers reached remote islands by following stellar navigation routes. they propose that early voyagers used a network of stars, prevailing winds, and ocean currents that linked tonga and samoa to distant island chains. many of the wayfinding stations along these routes were later lost as oral traditions were disrupted. the researchers describe these routes as “star paths threaded across the open pacific.”

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Reykjavík is the capital of Iceland. The city's population is 139,875.…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the relative sizes of the two capitals' populations. which choice most effectively uses information from the given sentences to emphasize the relative sizes of the two capitals' populations? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: reykjavík is the capital of iceland. the city's population is 139,875. reykjavík contains 36.42 percent of iceland's population. tokyo is the capital of japan. the city's population is 13,960,236. tokyo contains 11.14 percent of japan's population.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 1990, Cree communities in northern Quebec staged protests against th…
searchText: the student wants to make and support a generalization about the great whale campaign. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 1990, cree communities in northern quebec staged protests against the proposed great whale hydroelectric project. the project would have flooded traditional hunting and trapping grounds. the project was ultimately shelved, but the great whale campaign had a lasting effect. it drew international attention to indigenous land rights. it contributed to a 2002 agreement formally recognizing cree authority over local resource management.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In 1968, experimental composer Cornelius Cardew created The Great Learn…
searchText: the student wants to provide an explanation and an example of cardew's the great learning . which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in 1968, experimental composer cornelius cardew created the great learning . the great learning is not a conventional score but a series of seven text-based paragraphs. each paragraph gives performers instructions to whistle, hum, recite, or remember sounds. the instructions for paragraph 2 state, “sustain a single tone for the length of a slow exhalation, then listen to its echo.” the instructions for paragraph 6 state, “imagine a sound you have never heard, then describe it to your neighbor.”

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Some powerful works of literature have prompted lawmakers to pass new l…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a difference between the two books. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: some powerful works of literature have prompted lawmakers to pass new legislation. twelve years a slave (1853) is the memoir of solomon northup, who was kidnapped and enslaved in louisiana. northup's book strengthened public support for the legal reforms that culminated in the thirteenth amendment. silent spring (1962) is rachel carson's nonfiction critique of agricultural pesticide use. carson's book contributed to the passage of the 1972 federal environmental pesticide control act.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The 1927 Solvay Conference on Physics gathered twenty-nine leading scie…
searchText: the student wants to place schrödinger's argument within its historical context. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the 1927 solvay conference on physics gathered twenty-nine leading scientists to discuss quantum theory. the conference featured a now-famous debate between werner heisenberg and erwin schrödinger. heisenberg argued that the position and momentum of a particle cannot both be precisely known. schrödinger pushed back, arguing that a complete wave description preserved a more deterministic physical picture. heisenberg's interpretation eventually became the more widely held view.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The League of United Latin American Citizens (LULAC) was founded in Cor…
searchText: the student wants to identify an accomplishment of lulac. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the league of united latin american citizens (lulac) was founded in corpus christi, texas, in 1929 by representatives of several mexican american civic organizations. lulac was established to advance the civil rights of latino communities in the united states. ben garza served as lulac's first national president. in 1948, lulac supported the legal team in delgado v. bastrop isd , a federal case challenging school segregation of mexican american students in texas. the court's ruling required texas to end its practice of segregating mexican american students.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Sled-dog teams pulling a loaded sled can use up to 33% less energy when…
searchText: the student wants to present the study and its methodology. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: sled-dog teams pulling a loaded sled can use up to 33% less energy when running in single-file formation than when running side by side. the physics behind this savings has not been well understood. engineer sofía hernández used computational fluid dynamics to study airflow around a lead dog and its trailing teammates. the simulations showed that the trailing dogs experience reduced air resistance because they run inside a low-pressure pocket created by the lead dog. this effect reduces drag on the trailing dogs by about 45%.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: One of the most influential medieval libraries was the Library of Toled…
searchText: the student wants to explain how the library of toledo preserved scholarly knowledge. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: one of the most influential medieval libraries was the library of toledo in toledo, spain. it was founded in the twelfth century with the goal of preserving and transmitting scholarly knowledge. scholars at the library of toledo gathered arabic and greek manuscripts from across the mediterranean and translated them into latin. translations included works by the greek physician galen and the persian polymath avicenna. the library's translation school produced thousands of latin parchment copies that circulated throughout europe.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: A small number of US Navy sailors of Pacific Islander descent served du…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the historical significance of henry kaleo's enlistment date. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: a small number of us navy sailors of pacific islander descent served during the spanish-american war (1898). henry kaleo was born in hawaii around 1862. he enlisted in the us navy in march 1898. joseph maile was born in hawaii around 1859. he enlisted in the us navy in october 1894.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The arctic skua ( Stercorarius parasiticus ) is a seabird that feeds ma…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two ways an arctic skua obtains food. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the arctic skua ( stercorarius parasiticus ) is a seabird that feeds mainly on fish. it is unusual among seabirds in that it rarely dives below the surface to catch fish on its own. one way it obtains food is by chasing other birds in flight until they drop or regurgitate their catch. another way it obtains food is by skimming the surface and snatching small fish driven up by feeding whales.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Two competing theories of magnetism divided natural philosophers for ce…
searchText: the student wants to provide a historical overview of the two theories. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: two competing theories of magnetism divided natural philosophers for centuries. the ancient greek philosopher thales (circa 600 bce) endorsed an animistic theory. that theory held that lodestones contain a kind of soul or life-force that attracts iron. the roman writer lucretius (circa 50 bce) endorsed a corpuscular theory. that theory held that lodestones emit invisible particles that mechanically push and pull iron objects. in the 1600s, william gilbert's experiments largely settled the debate in favor of a non-animistic, field-based account.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Peru's Chan Chan was built in the 1300s as the capital of the Chimor ki…
searchText: the student wants to present cárdenas's research to an audience unfamiliar with chan chan. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: peru's chan chan was built in the 1300s as the capital of the chimor kingdom. the city's adobe walls feature intricate relief sculptures of marine animals and geometric patterns. photometric stereo imaging is a recent technique that uses multiple light directions to reveal fine surface detail in photographs. archaeologist manuel cárdenas applied photometric stereo imaging to photographs he had taken of chan chan's eroded adobe walls. the technique revealed dozens of relief sculptures that had been worn nearly smooth and were previously invisible to the naked eye.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In southern Australia, mallee shrublands have expanded into areas that…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the aim of the research study. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: in southern australia, mallee shrublands have expanded into areas that were once heathlands. aisha khan and liam o'connor of the university of adelaide investigated whether mallee expansion is linked to changes in fire regimes. khan and o'connor analyzed growth rings from mallee shrubs on a site that was previously heathland and matched the ring data to fire-history records. they found that periods of mallee population growth tended to follow long intervals between major fires.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Issa Rae (born 1985) is an actor, writer, and producer from the United…
searchText: the student wants to begin a narrative about rae's award-nominated web series. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: issa rae (born 1985) is an actor, writer, and producer from the united states. she is best known for the comedy web series the misadventures of awkward black girl . awkward black girl premiered in 2011 on youtube. the series was praised for its sharp humor and its frank depiction of an underrepresented experience. it was nominated for a shorty award for best web show.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: As natural structures, many spider webs combine remarkable elasticity w…
searchText: the student wants to present the primary aim of the research study. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: as natural structures, many spider webs combine remarkable elasticity with high tensile strength. a research team led by aditya ramesh wanted to better understand how these competing properties arise from the silk. ramesh's team built laboratory models simulating the geometric arrangement of silk strands within an orb web. the team measured how stress propagated through the strand junctions under controlled loads.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Méllinoë, a name drawn from Greek mythology, is an exoplanet that orbit…
searchText: the student wants to make and support a generalization about exoplanets. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: méllinoë, a name drawn from greek mythology, is an exoplanet that orbits the star kepler-186, also known as adair. méllinoë's mass is 0.04 times that of jupiter, or 0.04 jupiter masses. cernunnos, named for a celtic deity, is an exoplanet that orbits the star hd 89744, also known as iliad. cernunnos's mass is 7.9 jupiter masses.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Selective Laser Sintering (SLS) is a form of additive manufacturing tha…
searchText: the student wants to describe how sls produces 3d objects. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: selective laser sintering (sls) is a form of additive manufacturing that uses a laser to fuse powdered material into 3d objects. step 1: a thin layer of powdered nylon is spread across a build platform. step 2: a laser traces the cross-section of the object, fusing powder where the laser strikes. step 3: the build platform lowers, and a fresh layer of powder is spread on top. step 4: after the part is complete, it is removed from the powder bed and brushed clean for inspection.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Researchers in a 2022 study wanted to determine the rate at which 14 sp…
searchText: the student wants to present an overview of the study's findings. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: researchers in a 2022 study wanted to determine the rate at which 14 spoken languages encoded information and produced phonemes. they calculated the bits of information conveyed per second (the ir). the ir was approximately constant across the 14 languages (an average of 41 bits per second). they calculated the phonemes spoken per second (the pr). the pr varied widely (from 6.1 phonemes per second in thai to 13.8 in basque).

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: African American women played key roles in the labor movement, includin…
searchText: the student wants to compare the two women's contributions to the montgomery bus boycott. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: african american women played key roles in the labor movement, including in the 1955 montgomery bus boycott. civil rights organizer jo ann robinson, an english professor, helped draft and circulate the boycott's first call to action. civil rights activist mary fair burks led a women's political council that helped sustain the boycott. robinson worked behind the scenes to mobilize ministers and printers. burks was one of the boycott's most visible public spokespeople at community rallies.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Talia Ferreira is a coral biologist. She conducted a study of reef shar…
searchText: the student wants to emphasize the aim of the research study. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: talia ferreira is a coral biologist. she conducted a study of reef sharks in mozambique's bazaruto archipelago. she wanted to know how reef temperature affects where reef sharks hunt for small fish in that area. she found that during a marine heat wave, the sharks hunted farther from the inner reefs than they had during cooler periods. the outer reefs, which were cooler than the inner reefs during the heat wave, supported larger schools of bait fish.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Naomi Beckwith is a curator at the Guggenheim Museum in New York. She c…
searchText: the student wants to describe the exhibition that beckwith curated in 2019. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: naomi beckwith is a curator at the guggenheim museum in new york. she curated the guggenheim's 2019 exhibition implicit tensions: mapplethorpe now . this exhibition featured photographs by mid-career black artists working in portraiture. she also curated the guggenheim's 2021 exhibition off the record , which gathered conceptual works by long-established artists examining archives.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Volcanoes form on the seafloor when magma erupts through cracks in the…
searchText: the student wants to explain what a dormant submarine volcano is. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: volcanoes form on the seafloor when magma erupts through cracks in the ocean crust. a volcano in a phase of rapid eruptive activity is called an active submarine volcano. dormancy is a process in which a submarine volcano stops erupting and cools. a submarine volcano that no longer erupts is called a dormant submarine volcano. a dormant submarine volcano has entered the post-eruptive phase.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The background colors of US and Japanese highway exit signs are used to…
searchText: the student wants to contrast how green backgrounds are used in us and japanese road signs. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: the background colors of us and japanese highway exit signs are used to indicate each sign's purpose. directional signs include information such as exit numbers, route names, and destination distances. highways, major roadways, and minor roadways in the us generally use green for directional signs. highways in japan generally use green for directional signs. major roadways in japan generally use blue for directional signs.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Political scientist Mira Donovan is known for her “late-empire trap” th…
searchText: the student wants to use a quotation to challenge polybius's explanation of the conflict between rome and carthage. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: political scientist mira donovan is known for her “late-empire trap” theory. donovan's theory states that whenever “an aging power refuses to share regional influence,” conflict tends to follow. the theory is grounded in polybius's account of the punic wars between rome and carthage. polybius wrote that “rome's reluctance to accept carthage's resurgence made open conflict unavoidable.” historian jonas werth challenges polybius, noting that “commercial competition over mediterranean trade routes,” not refusal to share influence, drove rome to war.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Car-share programs offer vehicles for shared, short-term use. In statio…
searchText: the student wants to compare some disadvantages of station-based and free-floating car-share programs. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: car-share programs offer vehicles for shared, short-term use. in station-based car sharing, members reserve a car at a designated lot and return it to the same lot. station-based programs are predictable and easy to manage but need dedicated lots and capital investment. in free-floating car sharing, members find a car anywhere within a zone and leave it on the street within the zone. free-floating programs are flexible and inexpensive to launch but can lead to vehicles clustering inconveniently or being parked in problematic spots.

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: A villanelle is a nineteen-line poetic form. It uses two repeating rhym…
searchText: the student wants to use one of the poems to illustrate the villanelle form. which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? while researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: a villanelle is a nineteen-line poetic form. it uses two repeating rhyme sounds and two repeating refrain lines according to a set pattern. “late hours” is a villanelle by naomi whitfield. its refrain lines are “the lamp burns slowly down” and “sleep follows what was found.” “tide-bound” is a villanelle by naomi whitfield. its refrain lines are “the shore forgets the sea” and “the day returns to me.”

preview: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The popular “ocean garbage island” image suggests that floating plastic…

"the memory vault," an acclaimed science fiction novella by south korean author min-jun park, takes place in an alternate version of seoul where personal memories are a valuable resource that can be extracted and stored in private databases. ______ blank citizens must pay subscription fees to access their own childhood recollections.

preview: The diplomats of the Republic of Venice were famous for their sharp, witty rejoinders, a quality they maintained even when negotiating with…

searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? the traditional process of indian block printing generally proceeds like this: first, the artisan carves the desired design into a wooden block. next, the artisan dips the carved face of the block into a tray of natural dye. ______ blank the artisan presses the inked block firmly onto cotton fabric, transferring the design.

preview: Yuko Nishimura is a Japanese paper artist who often collaborates with other artists. ______ blank she has worked with French textile design…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? yuko nishimura is a japanese paper artist who often collaborates with other artists. ______ blank she has worked with french textile designer camille vacher several times. together, nishimura and vacher have created paper-and-cloth installations such as folded garden and light through threads .

preview: Researchers believe that timbers recovered from a Florida riverbed are from a French merchant vessel that sank in 1565. Oral histories pass…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? researchers believe that timbers recovered from a florida riverbed are from a french merchant vessel that sank in 1565. oral histories passed down among the area's timucua descendants support this belief. ______ blank timucua accounts describe how barrels of wine, an item the ship had been carrying, drifted onto nearby shores in the months following the wreck.

preview: Although memoirs and novels are considered distinct literary forms, many authors have created hybrid works that incorporate elements of bot…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? although memoirs and novels are considered distinct literary forms, many authors have created hybrid works that incorporate elements of both. maxine hong kingston's the woman warrior , ______ blank is an autobiographical work that weaves real memories of the author's chinese american girlhood together with reimagined folktales and dreamlike scenes that resemble fiction.

preview: Renowned Japanese ceramicist Shōji Hamada (1894–1978) created his signature high-gloss stoneware using a glazing technique called salt firi…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? renowned japanese ceramicist shōji hamada (1894–1978) created his signature high-gloss stoneware using a glazing technique called salt firing. this technique involves throwing rock salt into the kiln at peak temperatures. ______ blank the vapor settles on the clay surface, creating a glossy, mottled glaze.

preview: Indigo was a highly valued dye throughout the kingdoms of West Africa. Yoruba weavers in particular were famous for using this plant-based…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? indigo was a highly valued dye throughout the kingdoms of west africa. yoruba weavers in particular were famous for using this plant-based dye to color their fabrics a striking deep blue. ______ blank the word "indigo" itself, some linguists claim, may have entered european languages through yoruba trade routes.

preview: When following ballet scores, professional dancers like principal Misty Copeland take movement directions from descriptive notations, typic…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? when following ballet scores, professional dancers like principal misty copeland take movement directions from descriptive notations, typically in french, that appear alongside the choreography. ______ blank these descriptive terms might instruct the performer to leap grand jeté (a large jump) or move adagio (slowly and gracefully).

preview: Music historians agree that the rock guitarist Chuck Berry was exaggerating when he claimed to have single-handedly invented rock and roll.…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? music historians agree that the rock guitarist chuck berry was exaggerating when he claimed to have single-handedly invented rock and roll. no one can dispute, ______ blank that berry's distinctive guitar riffs and energetic stage presence helped define rock and roll as a genre during its formative years.

preview: Juvenile sea turtles spend their first years drifting in open-ocean currents, where they rely chiefly on their sense of smell to locate flo…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? juvenile sea turtles spend their first years drifting in open-ocean currents, where they rely chiefly on their sense of smell to locate floating mats of food. newly hatched freshwater terrapins, ______ blank rely primarily on visual cues to locate sheltered marshes within the first weeks of life. this reliance on different senses likely reflects the very different habitats the two groups must navigate during their earliest stages.

preview: Consider a wooden chair that a family has used for decades before donating it to a thrift store. This chair represents secondhand reuse, as…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? consider a wooden chair that a family has used for decades before donating it to a thrift store. this chair represents secondhand reuse, as it changes owners while still functioning in its original form. ______ blank the wooden planks rescued from a demolished barn and refashioned into a new chair represent upcycling, as the original material is reshaped into something with a distinct identity.

preview: The Alpine Climate Observatory in Switzerland is known for translating glacier data into musical compositions. One such project pairs recor…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? the alpine climate observatory in switzerland is known for translating glacier data into musical compositions. one such project pairs recordings of cracking ice with measurements of glacial retreat captured over a decade. as the rate of retreat accelerates, the pitch of the accompanying melody rises; ______ blank audiences can "hear" the pace at which the glacier is shrinking.

preview: In her 1962 essay Patterns of Trade and Settlement , Canadian historian Harriet Vance pushed back against the prevailing assumption that ri…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? in her 1962 essay patterns of trade and settlement , canadian historian harriet vance pushed back against the prevailing assumption that rivers alone dictated where early communities formed across the northern plains. ______ blank vance argued that, rather than being determined entirely by waterways, settlement decisions were shaped just as much by negotiated trade routes maintained by indigenous nations.

preview: The Atacama Solar Array project is expected to deliver 3.4 gigawatts of capacity, enough to supply electricity to nearly 1.5 million househ…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? the atacama solar array project is expected to deliver 3.4 gigawatts of capacity, enough to supply electricity to nearly 1.5 million households. as its name suggests, the project—currently under construction—consists of photovoltaic panels installed within chile's atacama desert. ______ blank the project blueprint specifies more than 9 million panels arranged across a 40-square-kilometer site east of the city of antofagasta.

preview: The protein resilin not only gives fleas their remarkable jumping power but also enables certain insects to survive sudden, sharp impacts.…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? the protein resilin not only gives fleas their remarkable jumping power but also enables certain insects to survive sudden, sharp impacts. resilin behaves like a near-perfect rubber, absorbing kinetic energy and then releasing it almost without loss. ______ blank tissues containing resilin can withstand repeated, high-frequency mechanical stress without breaking down over the insect's lifetime.

preview: Noticing that pond water teeming with microscopic life appeared to spontaneously develop maggots when left covered with cloth, seventeenth-…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? noticing that pond water teeming with microscopic life appeared to spontaneously develop maggots when left covered with cloth, seventeenth-century naturalists did not conclude that flies had laid eggs on the cloth; instead, many accepted the older view that life could arise from nonliving matter through a process called spontaneous generation. ______ blank when francesco redi designed his famous sealed-jar experiment in 1668, he framed it as a direct test of that theory.

preview: In 1988, Brazilian agronomist Beatriz Carvalho and Indian agronomist Surya Patel demonstrated that a particular rotation of cover crops sha…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? in 1988, brazilian agronomist beatriz carvalho and indian agronomist surya patel demonstrated that a particular rotation of cover crops sharply reduced topsoil erosion on tropical farmland. their findings proved highly influential in shaping sustainable agriculture policy. ______ blank they served as the basis for a 1994 regional agreement that incentivized the practice across more than a dozen countries.

preview: Because a parabolic mirror is curved along a single axis, light striking it from a faraway point converges at one focal point. A spherical…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? because a parabolic mirror is curved along a single axis, light striking it from a faraway point converges at one focal point. a spherical mirror, ______ blank can be likened to the inside of a polished bowl: it produces a blurred band of light rather than a single sharp point, since rays striking its edges focus differently from those striking its center.

preview: In 1971, the Twenty-Sixth Amendment to the US Constitution was ratified. The amendment prohibits the federal government and states from set…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? in 1971, the twenty-sixth amendment to the us constitution was ratified. the amendment prohibits the federal government and states from setting a voting age higher than 18 in any election, lowering the age from the previous standard of 21. ______ blank the amendment guarantees that voting-age citizens cannot be denied registration based on age in primary contests held by political parties.

preview: When, in the early 1900s, oceanographers first proposed that ocean basins are continually renewed by molten material rising from mid-ocean…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? when, in the early 1900s, oceanographers first proposed that ocean basins are continually renewed by molten material rising from mid-ocean ridges, some suggested that this renewal could be tracked through the magnetic record locked in seafloor rocks. the deep ocean floor is so inaccessible, though, that recovering the necessary samples requires both ships capable of long voyages and specialized drilling equipment. ______ blank researchers were unable to verify the theory of seafloor renewal until the 1960s.

preview: In 2020, archaeologist Beatrice Lim and ethnobotanist Daniel Okafor reconstructed a beverage from a 2,500-year-old residue scraped from a c…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? in 2020, archaeologist beatrice lim and ethnobotanist daniel okafor reconstructed a beverage from a 2,500-year-old residue scraped from a clay vessel uncovered in west africa. when they sampled the drink, known locally as sòkà ("to gather strength"), they found that it had a warming taste and reportedly heightened alertness. ______ blank the researchers, aware that beverages of that era were sometimes named after the effects they produced on drinkers, proposed that the drink's name, "to gather strength," pointed to its likely use as a stimulant given to laborers before long journeys.

preview: Sarah Winnemucca (1844–1891) adjusted her subject matter and voice depending on the audience she was addressing. In her published memoir, s…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? sarah winnemucca (1844–1891) adjusted her subject matter and voice depending on the audience she was addressing. in her published memoir, she used measured, formal prose to describe the displacement of her northern paiute community to a non-native readership; in her public lectures, ______ blank she relied on vivid storytelling and direct personal appeals to move listeners toward immediate political action.

preview: With his immersive installation The Garden That Listens , Senegalese artist Modibo N'Dour succeeds in creating an environment that is both…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? with his immersive installation the garden that listens , senegalese artist modibo n'dour succeeds in creating an environment that is both unsettling and deeply inviting. ______ blank when visitors step among the suspended brass leaves and hear the faint murmur that emerges only when they pause, they find themselves drawn closer despite a creeping sense that the room itself is aware of them—as if they have entered a space that quietly returns their attention.

preview: Researchers long puzzled over the origin of tektites, small glassy stones found scattered across regions far from any obvious volcanic sour…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? researchers long puzzled over the origin of tektites, small glassy stones found scattered across regions far from any obvious volcanic source. over the decades, competing theories were advanced, ranging from cometary debris to volcanic glass ejected from deep within earth's mantle, but none could account for all the chemical evidence. ______ blank investigators uncovered convincing evidence that tektites form when large meteorite impacts melt and hurl terrestrial rock high into the atmosphere.

preview: Aliyah Chen's textile work HARVEST/HOMETOWN , a denim factory uniform reworked with porcelain buttons and gold thread used in traditional H…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? aliyah chen's textile work harvest/hometown , a denim factory uniform reworked with porcelain buttons and gold thread used in traditional hmong embroidery, weaves together—literally and figuratively—materials drawn from both her family's heritage and the industrial garment trade that long defined her hometown. ______ blank chen's work also unsettles the line between heirloom craft and mass-produced labor.

preview: In the late 1960s, ceramicist Maren Tovar began firing her sculptures on what was then an unconventional surface for high-temperature work:…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? in the late 1960s, ceramicist maren tovar began firing her sculptures on what was then an unconventional surface for high-temperature work: discarded steel sheets reclaimed from shipyards. ______ blank maren's daughter pilar has chosen an unusual canvas for her ongoing project, driftlines . in this striking series, the artist paints fading mineral pigments onto sun-bleached driftwood gathered along the pacific coast of chile, producing images that appear to dissolve back into the wood as they age.

preview: In the late nineteenth century, Mary Church Terrell worked to expand civil rights for African Americans through multiple channels. As a lec…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? in the late nineteenth century, mary church terrell worked to expand civil rights for african americans through multiple channels. as a lecturer who traveled widely throughout the united states, she delivered speeches calling on white audiences to confront racial injustice in their own communities. ______ blank she helped found the national association of colored women, an organization devoted to advancing the welfare of black women across the country.

preview: Yiyun Li's story collection A Thousand Years of Good Prayers features multiple stories about family members struggling to understand one an…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? yiyun li's story collection a thousand years of good prayers features multiple stories about family members struggling to understand one another. in "the princess of nebraska," a young expectant mother considers her uncertain future while drifting through the streets of chicago with a near-stranger. ______ blank in the title story, a retired father visits his adult daughter in the american midwest and finds that years of unspoken distance are not easily bridged in conversation.

preview: For years, engineers have tested mycelium-based composites in place of synthetic foams for shock-absorbing packaging, and the results have…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? for years, engineers have tested mycelium-based composites in place of synthetic foams for shock-absorbing packaging, and the results have been encouraging overall. ______ blank these fungal materials—which can be grown around custom-shaped forms in a matter of days and then composted at the end of their service life—have outperformed petroleum-derived foams in repeated drop and compression tests.

preview: Maintaining the International Prototype of the Kilogram standards is no simple job. Each year, calibration data from national mass-measurem…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? maintaining the international prototype of the kilogram standards is no simple job. each year, calibration data from national mass-measurement laboratories around the world are gathered and transmitted to the bureau international des poids et mesures (bipm) in sèvres, france. ______ blank bipm physicists carry out the painstaking work of reconciling these slightly varying figures into a single, internationally agreed-upon mass standard.

preview: To identify which native grain varieties were cultivated in Peru's Sacred Valley before the spread of large-scale commercial farming, archa…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? to identify which native grain varieties were cultivated in peru's sacred valley before the spread of large-scale commercial farming, archaeologist renata quispe frequently consults colonial-era tax registers that recorded harvest tributes in detail. ______ blank she examines church murals from the same era, as they sometimes depict accurate likenesses of grains that have since disappeared from local cultivation.

preview: Voting members of the 2016 World Music Awards were captivated by Tinariwen's album Elwan and its contribution to the desert blues genre, a…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? voting members of the 2016 world music awards were captivated by tinariwen's album elwan and its contribution to the desert blues genre, a form of west african guitar music featuring intertwining electric melodies and call-and-response vocals that first emerged among tuareg communities along the sahara's southern edge in the late twentieth century. ______ blank they presented the group with the best world album award.

preview: When, in 2014, biologists Dr. Amara Okonjo and Dr. Felix Bauer began planning a new field initiative together, one of their central goals w…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? when, in 2014, biologists dr. amara okonjo and dr. felix bauer began planning a new field initiative together, one of their central goals was for early-career researchers from underrepresented regions to lead their own coastal monitoring studies. ______ blank they established the shoreline fellows program, a multi-year fellowship in which each participating researcher designs and directs an independent monitoring project along a coastline of their choosing.

preview: While reviewing seafloor sediment cores in 2018, marine geologist Yuki Tanaka identified an unusual layer of microscopic glass beads dating…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? while reviewing seafloor sediment cores in 2018, marine geologist yuki tanaka identified an unusual layer of microscopic glass beads dating to roughly 12,800 years ago, suggesting that a fragmenting cosmic body had showered the region with debris. ______ blank the rare deposit was named the "tanaka horizon" in recognition of the geologist who first characterized it.

preview: Following the unification of Italy in the 1860s, regional cuisines on the peninsula underwent a sweeping reorganization, driven largely by…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? following the unification of italy in the 1860s, regional cuisines on the peninsula underwent a sweeping reorganization, driven largely by surging urban demand for shelf-stable staples. the industrial production, branding, and rail distribution of dried pasta and canned tomatoes, ______ blank recast the country's loosely connected village foodways into a recognizably national table.

preview: An exit survey conducted after the 1992 mayoral debate in Riverdale indicated that challenger Mara Velasquez had stumbled: only 18 percent…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? an exit survey conducted after the 1992 mayoral debate in riverdale indicated that challenger mara velasquez had stumbled: only 18 percent of attendees who listened to the audio broadcast rated her the stronger speaker. ______ blank the debate was widely reported afterward as a turning point for the photogenic challenger, who polled markedly higher than the incumbent among viewers watching the new local cable feed.

preview: In the spring of 1971, sculptor Tomás Aguirre was living in what must have looked like the ideal setting for a rising artist: Florence. He…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? in the spring of 1971, sculptor tomás aguirre was living in what must have looked like the ideal setting for a rising artist: florence. he was studying the bronze-casting practices of italian renaissance workshops firsthand and beginning to earn favorable mention in regional art journals. ______ blank aguirre yearned to return to his hometown of guadalajara; only there, he believed, could his work meaningfully engage with the materials and traditions that mattered most to him.

preview: Roughly every few minutes, a plume of superheated brine erupts from a deep-sea hydrothermal vent on the Juan de Fuca Ridge before cooling a…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? roughly every few minutes, a plume of superheated brine erupts from a deep-sea hydrothermal vent on the juan de fuca ridge before cooling and dispersing into near-freezing water—a cycle that, on its face, would appear utterly hostile to living organisms. ______ blank as marine biologist adaeze onuora notes, "these chimneys are…something like nurseries for unusual life," hosting dense communities of tubeworms and chemosynthetic bacteria that flourish in the mineral-rich flow.

preview: Several members of the city planning commission have pushed back against proposals to livestream zoning hearings, worried that participants…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? several members of the city planning commission have pushed back against proposals to livestream zoning hearings, worried that participants would play to the camera and shift the deliberations toward soundbites. ______ blank the commissioners fear that most residents would never watch the full hearings, viewing only brief excerpts that could be edited to distort the discussion.

preview: In the 1920s, electrical engineer Granville Woods improved upon existing designs for the railway telegraph used to communicate with moving…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? in the 1920s, electrical engineer granville woods improved upon existing designs for the railway telegraph used to communicate with moving trains. ______ blank woods refined the induction system so that messages could be transmitted reliably between stations and a train traveling at speed. with this advance, woods became one of the most prolific black inventors to shape the safety of american railroads.

preview: According to a well-documented pattern in linguistic typology, languages whose verbs come at the very end of the clause tend strongly towar…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? according to a well-documented pattern in linguistic typology, languages whose verbs come at the very end of the clause tend strongly toward postpositions, in which grammatical markers follow the noun they govern. ______ blank languages whose verbs sit between the subject and object generally favor prepositions, in which those same grammatical markers appear in front of the noun.

preview: Optical telescopes capture the wavelengths of light visible to the human eye. Radio telescopes, ______ blank capture wavelengths far longer…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? optical telescopes capture the wavelengths of light visible to the human eye. radio telescopes, ______ blank capture wavelengths far longer than visible light, providing critical information to radio astronomers like beatriz salinas, who studies pulsar timing from a high-altitude observatory in the atacama.

preview: In a 2021 review of 320 case histories, clinicians observed a broad pattern of stimulus-driven plasticity in which musicians showed faster…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? in a 2021 review of 320 case histories, clinicians observed a broad pattern of stimulus-driven plasticity in which musicians showed faster cross-modal recruitment— ______ blank the recruitment of brain regions ordinarily devoted to one sense in the processing of input from another. string players (e.g., violinists) showed the largest effect; vocalists (e.g., choristers), meanwhile, showed the smallest.

preview: Anthropologist Joaquín Herrera explains why the seventh-century stepped platform at El Pinar in northern Honduras was likely the funerary m…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? anthropologist joaquín herrera explains why the seventh-century stepped platform at el pinar in northern honduras was likely the funerary monument of a regional ruler. first, the imported jadeite ornaments arranged on the platform suggest that the person interred there was a wealthy and influential leader. ______ blank the enormous labor required to quarry and stack the platform's limestone blocks would almost certainly have been mustered only for a ruler.

preview: Ida B. Wells, an investigative journalist of the post-Reconstruction era, earned a lasting reputation as a pioneering civil rights advocate…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? ida b. wells, an investigative journalist of the post-reconstruction era, earned a lasting reputation as a pioneering civil rights advocate after challenging segregation in the railroads of the american south. ______ blank in 1884, she sued the chesapeake, ohio and southwestern railroad after being forcibly removed from a first-class car designated for white passengers, a case she won at trial before the tennessee supreme court ultimately overturned the verdict.

preview: Earth's lightning—dramatic electrical discharges seen during storms—results, broadly speaking, from collisions inside thunderclouds. ______…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? earth's lightning—dramatic electrical discharges seen during storms—results, broadly speaking, from collisions inside thunderclouds. ______ blank rising ice crystals and falling graupel pellets collide inside the cloud and exchange charge, leaving the upper cloud positively charged and the lower cloud negatively charged. these separated charges then build to the point of producing a lightning stroke between regions of the cloud or between the cloud and the ground.

preview: Mahasweta Devi's novel Hajar Churashir Maa (1974) explores the political upheavals of late 1960s West Bengal through the grief of a mother…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? mahasweta devi's novel hajar churashir maa (1974) explores the political upheavals of late 1960s west bengal through the grief of a mother whose son has been killed. with its unflinching social criticism, polyphonic narration, and dexterous incorporation of bengali oral storytelling, the novel galvanized a generation of south asian writers. ______ blank those who took up its style and concerns are often grouped together as the mahasweta school novelists.

preview: Although those who flocked to the Klondike in 1897 imagined pulling nuggets from gravel bars with a simple pan, the region's richest gold l…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? although those who flocked to the klondike in 1897 imagined pulling nuggets from gravel bars with a simple pan, the region's richest gold lay frozen in permafrost layers far below the surface, well beyond the reach of solitary miners. ______ blank by 1900, most had abandoned their independent claims and hired on with one of the few syndicates capable of running the dredges and hydraulic operations the terrain demanded.

preview: A 2023 study by researchers Ines Quaresma and Bayo Ogundimu examined the relationship between community libraries' programming budgets and…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? a 2023 study by researchers ines quaresma and bayo ogundimu examined the relationship between community libraries' programming budgets and visitor turnout. ______ blank the researchers analyzed the correlation between how much 14,562 public libraries spent on outreach activities—programs such as author talks and after-school workshops—and how many residents walked through their doors (a proxy for overall reach).

preview: In 2019, a simulation developed by climate scientist Priya Iyengar and her research group indicated that the same Atlantic meridional overt…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? in 2019, a simulation developed by climate scientist priya iyengar and her research group indicated that the same atlantic meridional overturning currents responsible for moderating northwestern european winters—a circulation pattern stretching from the equator to the subpolar north atlantic—are also likely responsible for shaping nutrient delivery to high-latitude fisheries. ______ blank the simulation traced how shifts in the circulation drew nutrient-rich deep water toward the surface in a handful of well-defined zones, fueling the plankton blooms on which those fisheries depend.

preview: Most of the exoplanets confirmed by transit photometry orbit relatively close to their host stars, with orbital periods of only a few days.…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? most of the exoplanets confirmed by transit photometry orbit relatively close to their host stars, with orbital periods of only a few days. in 2018, ______ blank researchers confirmed a planet orbiting kepler-1654, a sun-like star, with an orbital period stretching beyond two earth years. called kepler-1654 b, it is one of the longest-period planets confirmed by the kepler mission.

preview: In 1928, German chemist Anke Vogel demonstrated that protein molecules are built from chains of amino acids linked in fixed, repeating sequ…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? in 1928, german chemist anke vogel demonstrated that protein molecules are built from chains of amino acids linked in fixed, repeating sequences, a finding that overturned earlier assumptions about how proteins are organized. ______ blank chemists had assumed that proteins were loose colloidal aggregates of small molecules rather than long, ordered polymer chains made of distinct units.

preview: Working together with the Hopi Tribe's Water Resources Program, Dr. Marisol Quimayousie analyzed data about groundwater recharge in the Bla…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? working together with the hopi tribe's water resources program, dr. marisol quimayousie analyzed data about groundwater recharge in the black mesa basin. she found that recharge (the rate at which water seeps into the aquifer) was highest in late summer at lower-elevation washes. at higher-elevation washes, ______ blank recharge was highest in early autumn.

preview: The Sumerian ruler Shulgi accomplished a great deal during his nearly half-century on the throne of Ur. He standardized weights and measure…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? the sumerian ruler shulgi accomplished a great deal during his nearly half-century on the throne of ur. he standardized weights and measures across his kingdom, established a network of post stations on the royal road, and oversaw a flourishing of literary and administrative writing. today, ______ blank he is mainly remembered for a single innovation: the highly trained civil bureaucracy he assembled to administer the third dynasty of ur.

preview: With her warehouse-scale installation Tideline Archive , Nigerian Canadian artist Folake Adesanya managed to build a setting that felt, as…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? with her warehouse-scale installation tideline archive , nigerian canadian artist folake adesanya managed to build a setting that felt, as she had described it in advance, both "oceanic and architectural." ______ blank with a wall of cast-glass panels arrayed along the far end of a salt-streaked concrete hall, the installation called to mind a coastal observation post—albeit one moored deep inside a converted shipyard.

preview: Sicily, the central Mediterranean island that now sits at the southern tip of Italy, was governed by various Arabic-speaking emirates over…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? sicily, the central mediterranean island that now sits at the southern tip of italy, was governed by various arabic-speaking emirates over roughly two centuries beginning in the ninth century. ______ blank many arabic words, such as "zibibbo"—the name of a grape variety still cultivated on the island—worked their way into the sicilian language.

preview: Researchers Sven Ahlqvist, Karina Boateng, and Diego Reyes report that while astronomers may have traditionally produced research papers wi…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? researchers sven ahlqvist, karina boateng, and diego reyes report that while astronomers may have traditionally produced research papers within small national teams, the available data point to a steady shift in the opposite direction. ______ blank astronomers are forming sprawling international collaborations—a trend made vivid by a sharp rise in the number of astronomy publications with author lists drawn from more than ten countries.

preview: While studying lionfish, a striped predatory species originally from the Indo-Pacific, University of Florida researchers wondered whether t…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? while studying lionfish, a striped predatory species originally from the indo-pacific, university of florida researchers wondered whether the rapid spread of the fish across the caribbean reflected unusually voracious feeding behavior. ______ blank they discovered that lionfish are surprisingly patient hunters that often spend long stretches hovering nearly motionless along the reef, waiting for prey to drift within range.

preview: Botanists were able to grow viable seedlings of the silver thistle within a single season of identifying its native habitat in 1903. ______…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? botanists were able to grow viable seedlings of the silver thistle within a single season of identifying its native habitat in 1903. ______ blank the propagation process proved far slower for the dwarf sandalwood, which botanists managed to cultivate only nine years after first documenting the plant in the wild.

preview: First proposed by ecologist Robert Paine to describe species whose influence on their environment is disproportionate to their numbers, the…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? first proposed by ecologist robert paine to describe species whose influence on their environment is disproportionate to their numbers, the term "keystone species" can apply to predators as well as to certain plants. in a predator keystone system, a single carnivore restrains the populations of multiple prey, keeping the community in balance. ______ blank in a plant keystone system, a single fruit-bearing tree sustains diverse animal populations through lean seasons.

preview: The Maya of Mesoamerica carved elaborate stone monuments called stelae to commemorate countable events, such as royal accessions and milita…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? the maya of mesoamerica carved elaborate stone monuments called stelae to commemorate countable events, such as royal accessions and military victories. ______ blank they may have used stelae to preserve more elaborate content, including dynastic genealogies and astronomical observations, according to recent epigraphers.

preview: My fascination with historic covered bridges has prompted me to track them down whenever I travel through a new region of New England. ____…
searchText: which choice completes the text with the most logical transition? my fascination with historic covered bridges has prompted me to track them down whenever i travel through a new region of new england. ______ blank i could explore every county in new england and still not find the longest covered bridge in north america. that bridge, the hartland bridge, spans the saint john river in new brunswick, canada.

preview: The more intricate and varied a bird species' nest-building behavior, the larger and more metabolically costly its forebrain region tends t…
