searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? pigments give pottery glazes their color. cobalt is a metal-based pigment used to produce several hues, including deep blue. cobalt-blue glaze gets its color from cobalt oxide. glazes can also be ash-based; ash-based glazes contain a high level of potassium. in a 2022 study, archaeologists tested the blue pigment on decorated stoneware shards produced by potters of the joseon dynasty in korea roughly 500 years ago. the test showed that the pigment on several shards contained no cobalt but had a high level of potassium. this finding led the researchers to conclude that ______ blank

preview: Some school administrators believe that when students are required to switch tasks during a lesson, they suffer a drop in focus and learnin…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? some school administrators believe that when students are required to switch tasks during a lesson, they suffer a drop in focus and learning outcomes. however, a team led by linnea karlsson, who studies educational psychology, has found that brief, well-timed task switches can have a cognitive component that strengthens students' working memory, resulting in better retention that benefits learners and teachers alike. therefore, schools should recognize that ______ blank

preview: The Mexican Plan of Iguala was issued in 1821, bringing to a close the eleven-year war for independence from Spain that had begun in 1810.…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the mexican plan of iguala was issued in 1821, bringing to a close the eleven-year war for independence from spain that had begun in 1810. written in spanish, which was not the first language of many indigenous people in the country but which was the official language of administration across the former spanish empire, the plan affirmed that mexico would respect existing property rights, pledged guarantees to european-born residents who chose to remain—widely understood as a reassurance to former colonial elites—and held up mexico as a model for emerging governments in the americas (an implicit appeal to neighboring regions still under colonial rule). so even though the plan is explicitly addressed to the mexican people, it is reasonable to conclude that ______ blank

preview: To investigate the history of continental glaciation—when massive ice sheets repeatedly advanced across landmasses—Mei-Ling Chao and collea…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? to investigate the history of continental glaciation—when massive ice sheets repeatedly advanced across landmasses—mei-ling chao and colleagues compared ancient sediments from the vaalbara craton in southern africa to modern glacial deposits. using mineral grain analysis, the researchers found that vaalbara sediments dating to about 2.9 billion years ago (bya) most strongly resemble modern sediments deposited in warm, shallow seas. by contrast, they found that vaalbara sediments dating to about 2.4 bya and 2.2 bya have a grain composition that closely matches that of modern glacial till. chao's team therefore concluded that ______ blank

preview: In documents called peer-review reports, scientific referees explain the reasoning behind their recommendations on submitted manuscripts, a…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? in documents called peer-review reports, scientific referees explain the reasoning behind their recommendations on submitted manuscripts, and in those explanations they sometimes cite and discuss prior empirical studies. science studies researcher mira halpern argues that while referees are naturally inclined to cite studies whose conclusions support their own assessment, the strongest peer-review reports anticipate and answer potential objections; discussing prior studies whose results conflict with the referee's view could therefore ______ blank

preview: In a study of the mechanisms underlying motor learning—or the ability to acquire and refine new physical skills through practice—neuroscien…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? in a study of the mechanisms underlying motor learning—or the ability to acquire and refine new physical skills through practice—neuroscientists tomás reyes, hana park, and others observed rats running through obstacle courses. the team discovered that purkinje cells, a type of cell found in the cerebellum, are required for the consolidation of newly learned motor sequences. they also found that purkinje cell activity requires serotonin, a chemical the brain produces in response to rhythmic exercise. consequently, engaging in rhythmic exercise should likely help to ______ blank

preview: Apomixis is a form of reproduction in which a plant produces seeds without pollination. The resulting seed contains an embryo whose DNA, or…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? apomixis is a form of reproduction in which a plant produces seeds without pollination. the resulting seed contains an embryo whose dna, or genetic material, comes entirely from the parent plant. among flowering plants, apomixis has been documented in a small number of species, including dandelions and some blackberries. when botanists at the royal adelaide botanic garden analyzed the dna of the garden's collection of hieracium plants (a genus of hawkweeds), they discovered that two individuals were not genetically similar enough to any of the pollen-producing plants in the surrounding bed to be their offspring. however, both had grown from seeds produced by parent plants in that bed. thus, the botanists concluded that ______ blank

preview: In 2019 evolutionary biologist Yuki Tanaka and her team examined the evolution of the plica semilunaris, a small fold of tissue at the inne…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? in 2019 evolutionary biologist yuki tanaka and her team examined the evolution of the plica semilunaris, a small fold of tissue at the inner corner of the eye that is present in some mammals, including humans, but is widely considered vestigial. studying 412 mammal species, the team found that the plica semilunaris has emerged independently across multiple lineages on separate occasions and, notably, has not disappeared after emerging in those lineages. moreover, the team determined that species that possess the fold tend to have higher concentrations of mucin-secreting cells, which protect the corneal surface, at the inner eye. therefore, the team hypothesized that the plica semilunaris likely ______ blank

preview: Planetary scientists examined the Tharsis Montes region of Mars because it appears to contain large channel networks that may have been car…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? planetary scientists examined the tharsis montes region of mars because it appears to contain large channel networks that may have been carved by ancient flowing water. in their investigations of tharsis montes, the researchers found mineral deposits in quantities and patterns consistent with sustained water flow. however, dust storms and lava flows over billions of years could have substantially obscured or transported original surface features. therefore, ______ blank

preview: Pueraria montana (kudzu) is an East Asian vine that has become invasive in the southeastern United States, where it smothers native trees a…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? pueraria montana (kudzu) is an east asian vine that has become invasive in the southeastern united states, where it smothers native trees and weakens soil stability. p. montana can be controlled with broad-spectrum herbicides, but that approach can also harm beneficial plants growing nearby. recent research on deploying species-specific fungal pathogens to suppress invasive plants may offer a path toward exclusively targeting p. montana , consequently ______ blank

preview: The Etruscan civilization flourished in what is now central Italy from approximately 900 BCE to 100 BCE. Many examples of the civilization'…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the etruscan civilization flourished in what is now central italy from approximately 900 bce to 100 bce. many examples of the civilization's inscriptions exist, but scholars have only partially deciphered the etruscan language and remain uncertain about much of its grammar. nevertheless, archaeologists have recovered material remains, such as painted tomb walls and bronze utensils, that provide information about the civilization's funerary practices and trade networks. the archaeologists' findings therefore suggest that ______ blank

preview: Several wall paintings discovered in tombs of New Kingdom Egypt depict a male figure tending a vineyard with a falcon perched nearby. Some…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? several wall paintings discovered in tombs of new kingdom egypt depict a male figure tending a vineyard with a falcon perched nearby. some scholars have argued that the figure is the god horus, since the falcon is known to have been linked with horus in egyptian iconography, but university of cambridge egyptologist halima ndiaye suggests that falcons may also have been associated with vineyard agriculture in general. the fact that a falcon is shown near the male figure, therefore, ______ blank

preview: Although former competitive athletes make up a small share of the total workforce in Canada, they hold a noticeably higher share of managem…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? although former competitive athletes make up a small share of the total workforce in canada, they hold a noticeably higher share of management positions in physical-therapy clinics. one possible explanation for this disproportionate representation is that competitive athletic careers familiarize people with structured rehabilitation protocols that are also central to physical-therapy clinic operations, and this familiarity thus ______ blank

preview: It is common for flamenco performers and audiences to describe certain palos as having "duende," indicating that the performances stir a po…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? it is common for flamenco performers and audiences to describe certain palos as having "duende," indicating that the performances stir a powerful emotional response, such as a chill along the spine or sudden tears. the exact rhythmic properties that give a piece duende, however, have long been thought to be undefinable. to investigate duende, a team led by musicologist esteban vargas accelerated the contratiempos and tightened the compás patterns in flamenco guitar solos and asked flamenco performers to compare the intensity of duende in each modified piece with the intensity of duende in the original piece. they found that participants were nearly six times likelier to characterize the modified pieces as having duende than to characterize the original versions as having duende, suggesting that ______ blank

preview: Edo-period Japanese woodblock prints from the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries frequently showed townspeople gathered around bowls of ri…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? edo-period japanese woodblock prints from the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries frequently showed townspeople gathered around bowls of rice or platters of pickled vegetables. some art historians, noting that rice and pickles were a major component of the edo urban diet, interpret these depictions as reflections of everyday edo eating habits. however, a group of researchers recently reviewed hundreds of food-related prints and found that imported sugar—which could only be acquired in edo japan at great expense, since it had to be brought from southern regions through controlled trade—appears in edo prints of the period more than four times as frequently as rice or pickles do, thereby casting doubt on the idea that ______ blank

preview: By running computer simulations of the formation of a young star system designated HD-2174, Priya Banerjee, Hideo Suzuki, and colleagues co…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? by running computer simulations of the formation of a young star system designated hd-2174, priya banerjee, hideo suzuki, and colleagues concluded that the central star may have been surrounded by three distinct debris belts before the planets started to form. the researchers suggest that the materials in the innermost belt produced the three small rocky planets closest to the star, the materials in the middle belt formed the rest of the planets, and the materials in the outermost belt created the comets and icy bodies in the system's far reaches. in one simulation, the researchers delayed the initial formation of the middle belt, causing oversized rocky giants to begin developing from the innermost belt. the researchers therefore hypothesize that ______ blank

preview: The Norwegian Mother, Father and Child Cohort Study (MoBa) tracks health outcomes among more than 100,000 families in Norway over the cours…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the norwegian mother, father and child cohort study (moba) tracks health outcomes among more than 100,000 families in norway over the course of decades. as is true of most cohort studies, this need for years of repeated data collection results in high costs. by contrast, a relatively straightforward nutrition survey, such as one that is merely trying to identify the percentage of college students in a city who eat breakfast, may not need a large budget because ______ blank

preview: Scholars are increasingly examining the communication and preservation of ecological knowledge through Indigenous oral traditions (e.g., Ma…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? scholars are increasingly examining the communication and preservation of ecological knowledge through indigenous oral traditions (e.g., maori karakia about coastal currents and sámi joiks about reindeer migration). in one study, ethnobotanist aroha pereira received insight from wiremu te atu, an oral-history keeper for the ngāti porou people in aotearoa, into chants referencing the people's use of seasonal seabed-stone weirs along estuary mouths for the cultivation of eel for consumption. archaeological evidence of significant increases in eel size and abundance in that area concurrent with the documented past implementation of the method described in the chants supports the conclusion that ______ blank

preview: Over 300 languages are spoken in greater London in addition to English—one can find Bengali spoken in the area of Tower Hamlets, or Yoruba…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? over 300 languages are spoken in greater london in addition to english—one can find bengali spoken in the area of tower hamlets, or yoruba in peckham. most speakers of spanish languages reside in the area of elephant and castle (part of london's borough of southwark) and in seven sisters, in the borough of haringey. new arrivals from the andean region of colombia, where many people speak spanish with regional pacific influences, tend to settle in seven sisters, while new arrivals from coastal ecuador, where many people speak spanish with quichua loanwords, tend to settle in elephant and castle. it can therefore be inferred that ______ blank

preview: Scholars have noted that the photographer Alfred Stieglitz's curatorial vision was likely shaped in part by his marriage to the painter Geo…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? scholars have noted that the photographer alfred stieglitz's curatorial vision was likely shaped in part by his marriage to the painter georgia o'keeffe, but many do not recognize o'keeffe as a curator in her own right. indeed, o'keeffe organized several major exhibitions herself, including the 1946 retrospective at the museum of modern art and numerous traveling shows of southwestern modernism. thus, those who primarily view o'keeffe as a muse for stieglitz's photographs and exhibitions ______ blank

preview: When the Saint-Denis Chronicle, a parchment manuscript purported to date to the early 1300s, came to light in 1962, some historians believe…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? when the saint-denis chronicle, a parchment manuscript purported to date to the early 1300s, came to light in 1962, some historians believed it demonstrated that french scholarly knowledge of certain byzantine court ceremonies predated the fall of constantinople in 1453. in 2019, a team including conservators iona macleod and pierre caron and materials scientist yuna halevi conducted a thorough analysis of the manuscript and the ink used. they found that the ink contains synthetic indigo, a compound that was first introduced in ink manufacturing in the late 1800s. therefore, the team concluded that ______ blank

preview: The Late Ordovician mass extinction, occurring roughly 444 million years ago, eliminated nearly 85% of marine species. Some researchers arg…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the late ordovician mass extinction, occurring roughly 444 million years ago, eliminated nearly 85% of marine species. some researchers argue that a nearby supernova’s gamma-ray burst stripped earth’s ozone layer and triggered the event. others remain skeptical, partly because no isotopic signature characteristic of a supernova event has been securely dated to the boundary. in 2021, a team led by reiko tanaka identified an unusual iron-60 enrichment in deep-sea sediment cores recovered off the patagonian shelf. researchers who favor the supernova hypothesis cite this enrichment as supporting evidence. however, tanaka’s team has not yet been able to determine the depositional age of the iron-60 layer. consequently, the team cautions that ______ blank

preview: Bronze bells cast in workshops in Hangzhou, China, during the Southern Song dynasty (1127–1279) produce a tone that contemporary metallurgi…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? bronze bells cast in workshops in hangzhou, china, during the southern song dynasty (1127–1279) produce a tone that contemporary metallurgists describe as exceptionally pure compared with bells of similar size cast today. some specialists had attributed the difference to the metal alloy used, but modern and song-era bells are made from the same proportions of copper and tin. recent microstructural analysis, however, has revealed that the song-era bells were subjected to a multistep annealing process during cooling, prompting researchers to propose that ______ blank

preview: The Norte Chico civilization formed along the arid Pacific coast of present-day Peru around 3000 BCE, near the mouths of several river vall…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the norte chico civilization formed along the arid pacific coast of present-day peru around 3000 bce, near the mouths of several river valleys. the dry desert climate made it nearly impossible to grow most food crops without irrigation, but the same dryness preserved harvested goods unusually well. to work around the climate, the norte chico people built canals from the rivers to their fields and farmed cotton, squash, and beans. that system performed so reliably that the harvests routinely exceeded what families needed in a season. as a result, the norte chico people ______ blank

preview: Helena María Viramontes’s 1995 novel Under the Feet of Jesus follows a teenage migrant farmworker in 1970s California through fragmented, i…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? helena maría viramontes’s 1995 novel under the feet of jesus follows a teenage migrant farmworker in 1970s california through fragmented, image-heavy prose—a structure viramontes has compared to a series of overlapping snapshots. reading the novel in seminar, several students complained that the timeline felt confusing and the labor disputes it depicts seemed remote from their own lives. their instructor, however, drew the class’s attention to the novel’s plain present-tense diction and to ongoing debates about farmworker conditions that viramontes’s scenes anticipate, suggesting that under the feet of jesus ______ blank

preview: Settled by Portuguese mariners in the late 1400s, the Cabo Verde islands developed a creole language, Kabuverdianu, whose vocabulary preser…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? settled by portuguese mariners in the late 1400s, the cabo verde islands developed a creole language, kabuverdianu, whose vocabulary preserves many sixteenth-century portuguese forms that mainland portuguese has long since reshaped. why would kabuverdianu have preserved them? the islands lay far from the main atlantic shipping lanes that connected lisbon to brazil and west africa, so few new colonists arrived after the initial settlement. that separation in turn limited speakers’ exposure to the shifts in pronunciation and grammar spreading through portuguese in europe. thus, the present-day distinctiveness of kabuverdianu suggests the extent to which ______ blank

preview: In Norway, the rugged Atlantic puffin is widely treated as an emblem of seafaring resilience. Although puffins can endure harsh storms, the…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? in norway, the rugged atlantic puffin is widely treated as an emblem of seafaring resilience. although puffins can endure harsh storms, they are not noticeably hardier or harder to observe than several other cliff-nesting seabirds along the norwegian coast. historian ingrid halvorsen has shown that the popular view of puffins crystallized in the late 1800s, when guided coastal tours became a fashionable pursuit. local boat captains spread the idea that puffin colonies could be reached only by venturing onto sheer, weather-battered headlands where the seas were perilous. halvorsen says these claims were self-interested. she suggests that the boat captains presented puffins in this way in order to ______ blank

preview: The practice of prescribed burning (intentionally setting controlled, low-intensity fires) is often viewed as being in tension with wildfir…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the practice of prescribed burning (intentionally setting controlled, low-intensity fires) is often viewed as being in tension with wildfire prevention efforts. however, a large field study across 480,000 hectares of mixed-conifer landscape in arizona’s coconino national forest calls that view into question. so far, results of the study indicate that stands subjected to periodic prescribed burning (the planned removal of accumulated leaf litter and small trees) may be more resistant to severe wildfire than stands that have not been burned at all. these results, in turn, suggest that ______ blank

preview: Microbiologist Inés García-Martín and colleagues sampled the naturally occurring fungal communities living on the bark and within the root…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? microbiologist inés garcía-martín and colleagues sampled the naturally occurring fungal communities living on the bark and within the root tissue of wild quercus suber (cork oak) trees. the researchers detected many of the same fungal genera both on the bark surface and inside the roots. to explain this, the researchers pointed to the cork oak’s low, spreading canopy and noted that windblown soil particles could lift fungal spores onto the bark. alternatively, they observed that decomposing leaf litter, which contains fungi shed from the canopy above, could be drawn down by rainfall into the rhizosphere where the roots grow. either explanation suggests that ______ blank

preview: Spotted hyenas are highly social carnivores that live in tightly organized clans across sub-Saharan Africa. Some clans hold elaborate “gree…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? spotted hyenas are highly social carnivores that live in tightly organized clans across sub-saharan africa. some clans hold elaborate “greeting ceremonies” in which individuals lift a hind leg, like dogs at a fire hydrant, in carefully chosen sequences. these ceremonies are familiar to researchers, but many clans never perform them and their function is unclear. one team proposed in 2018 that the greeting ceremony helps clan members confirm rank during periods when fights over food become unusually frequent. behavioral ecologist maya dlamini disagrees with that idea, though. she reasons that if the greeting ceremony plays such an important role for hyenas, then it’s most likely that ______ blank

preview: In her 2019 article “Bus Stops as Archives,” scholar Priya Chandrasekaran describes Hema Iyer (1901–1978), a devoted collector of bus and r…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? in her 2019 article “bus stops as archives,” scholar priya chandrasekaran describes hema iyer (1901–1978), a devoted collector of bus and rail tickets, transit timetables, and printed station advertisements gathered during decades of commuting through mumbai. iyer recognized that university libraries considered her enormous collection of urban ephemera to be of little academic value—indeed, it wasn’t until 1984, after iyer’s death, that the bombay city archives accepted the collection, having come to see its potential usefulness to social historians studying daily commuter life. hence, the example of iyer serves to ______ blank

preview: Tarte Tatin has been a favorite French dessert for generations, but the recipe’s origins are disputed. A small hotel in Lamotte-Beuvron, ru…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? tarte tatin has been a favorite french dessert for generations, but the recipe’s origins are disputed. a small hotel in lamotte-beuvron, run by two sisters, asserted that one of them accidentally created the upside-down apple tart in the 1880s. a pastry chef working in orléans claimed in the 1930s to have developed the recipe years earlier, citing handwritten notes. a parisian café near les halles also publicly maintained that its kitchen had served an essentially identical tart since the 1870s. no clear documentary evidence has emerged to favor any one of these accounts over the others, however. it thus seems that ______ blank

preview: Frogs learn the calls of their species shortly after metamorphosis. With each generation, small acoustic differences accumulate, producing…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? frogs learn the calls of their species shortly after metamorphosis. with each generation, small acoustic differences accumulate, producing distinct call types—referred to as dialects—among geographically separated populations of the same species. a research study examined whether four-week-old hyperolius marmoratus (painted reed frog) juveniles prefer local dialects over the unfamiliar calls of nonlocal h. marmoratus populations: the more orientation responses the juveniles made toward a played call, the stronger their preference. the researchers found that juveniles oriented more frequently toward their own local dialect than toward nonlocal dialects. since call preference is known to influence mate choice in reed frogs, the finding suggests that ______ blank

preview: Mail art is a form of correspondence in which artists send original drawings, collages, and printed pieces through the postal system. The p…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? mail art is a form of correspondence in which artists send original drawings, collages, and printed pieces through the postal system. the practice has been around since the new york correspondance school, founded by ray johnson in the early 1960s. since then, mail art has appealed to artists looking for an inexpensive form of expression to share with a chosen network of recipients. mail artists often blend handmade imagery with social commentary and work outside the conventional gallery system. at first, email and social media seemed poised to replace the postal exchange, but this old form persists. today, there are enough mail artists in north america to support annual mail-art exhibitions. this suggests that ______ blank

preview: The Triassic reptile Tanystropheus hydroides is known for having an exceptionally elongated neck nearly as long as its body and tail combin…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the triassic reptile tanystropheus hydroides is known for having an exceptionally elongated neck nearly as long as its body and tail combined. until an almost complete fossil skeleton was recovered in switzerland, paleontologists had been able to examine only skull specimens and isolated cervical vertebrae from t. hydroides , though it was generally presumed that, like other large-necked reptiles of the period, the species was a fast pursuit predator chasing prey across open water. examining the more complete skeleton in 2020, stephan spiekman and his colleagues determined that t. hydroides had stout limbs, hollow lightweight neck vertebrae, and small flattened teeth—features that, taken together with the creature’s long stiff neck, would have made sustained swimming costly and ambush from concealment relatively effective. based on these findings the team suggests that t. hydroides likely ______ blank

preview: Many tick repellents include natural compounds that work by stimulating multiple chemosensory receptors on the ticks’ legs. As ticks develo…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? many tick repellents include natural compounds that work by stimulating multiple chemosensory receptors on the ticks’ legs. as ticks develop resistance, new repellents are needed. adesh mehta and his team found that nootkatone, a compound extracted from alaska yellow-cedar, can repel ticks by stimulating just one chemosensory receptor—and this receptor, cr12 , is present in every tick species known to transmit lyme disease. therefore, the researchers suggest that in developing new repellents, it would be most useful to ______ blank

preview: In dialects of Portuguese spoken in northern Portugal, the “v” sound and the “b” sound are commonly merged in everyday speech, so words lik…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? in dialects of portuguese spoken in northern portugal, the “v” sound and the “b” sound are commonly merged in everyday speech, so words like vaca and baca sound nearly identical. portuguese dialects of southern brazil’s rio grande do sul region show a similar merging of these two sounds in casual conversation. historical records indicate that rio grande do sul was settled in the 1700s primarily by colonists whose families had originated in northern portugal. thus, linguists have concluded that ______ blank

preview: To better understand the nest-building behavior of Cathartes aura (the turkey vulture), some early studies relied on plaster casting to cap…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? to better understand the nest-building behavior of cathartes aura (the turkey vulture), some early studies relied on plaster casting to capture detailed measurements of nest cavities in hollow logs and rock crevices. plaster casting involves filling the empty cavity with a liquid plaster that hardens into a three-dimensional model; however, freeing the model requires breaking apart the surrounding log or stone, destroying the cavity in the process. in a 2023 study, ornithologist daniela marquez and colleagues showed that a portable lidar scanner can accurately record a cavity's dimensions both at a single moment and across the entire nesting season, something that is impossible with plaster casting because ______ blank

preview: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) has rapidly become the most powerful infrared observatory ever launched, producing landmark images of…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the james webb space telescope (jwst) has rapidly become the most powerful infrared observatory ever launched, producing landmark images of distant galaxies and exoplanet atmospheres; even so, only a small fraction of its observing time is allotted to objects within our own solar system. astrophysicist pablo restrepo and colleagues argue that a smaller, dedicated infrared telescope optimized for solar-system targets would allow continuous monitoring of icy moons and a comprehensive survey of trans-neptunian objects. restrepo and colleagues' recommendation therefore implies that the jwst ______ blank

preview: The body plan of brittle stars (class Ophiuroidea) — slender, highly flexible arms radiating from a small central disk — complicates straig…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the body plan of brittle stars (class ophiuroidea) — slender, highly flexible arms radiating from a small central disk — complicates straightforward comparisons with most other animals, whose anatomies are organized along a bilateral head-to-tail axis. particularly puzzling is the location of nervous-system structures: in most bilateral animals these are concentrated in a discrete head, but brittle stars are descended from bilateral ancestors yet appear headless. mapping the expression of well-characterized head-, trunk-, and tail-region patterning genes from a closely related bilateral relative onto the brittle star amphiura filiformis , researchers found strong activity of head-associated genes throughout each arm and only weak, peripheral activity of trunk- and tail-associated genes, suggesting that ______ blank

preview: The growing use of automated machinery in textile factories during the early decades of the twentieth century reduced the overall cost of m…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the growing use of automated machinery in textile factories during the early decades of the twentieth century reduced the overall cost of manufacturing a garment. those savings, however, were concentrated almost entirely in the cutting, sewing, and finishing stages, which take place after the garment's fabric has been produced and its pattern designed. this suggests that during the early decades of the twentieth century, ______ blank

preview: Edmonia Lewis's 1876 marble sculpture The Death of Cleopatra , which depicts the Egyptian queen in the moment after her suicide, is widely…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? edmonia lewis's 1876 marble sculpture the death of cleopatra , which depicts the egyptian queen in the moment after her suicide, is widely recognized as a turning point in the history of work by black american sculptors. scholars should be careful, however, about reading present-day political or ideological commitments into the piece: the assumptions and frameworks that seem self-evident today may have been foreign to lewis and her nineteenth-century audience, just as ideas central to them may be unfamiliar to us. scholars who lose sight of this when discussing the death of cleopatra therefore ______ blank

preview: Like many groups of freshwater fishes, North American minnows have suffered substantial population declines in recent decades, driven by ha…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? like many groups of freshwater fishes, north american minnows have suffered substantial population declines in recent decades, driven by habitat loss, pollution, and altered stream flows. minnows are also among the most thoroughly studied freshwater fishes, surpassed only by salmonids in the volume of research dedicated to them. as a result, fisheries biologists have drawn many of their conclusions about freshwater-fish declines from minnow data. in a 2022 paper, ichthyologist maya ortiz notes that minnows are among the relatively few freshwater-fish groups that form large mixed-species shoals and exhibit broad dietary flexibility, two traits that are linked with increased tolerance to certain types of environmental disturbance. ortiz therefore contends that ______ blank

preview: A team of plant biologists led by Hana Okabe, Diego Ramirez, and Léa Fontaine studied the mechanism by which Lemna minor (common duckweed)…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? a team of plant biologists led by hana okabe, diego ramirez, and léa fontaine studied the mechanism by which lemna minor (common duckweed) accelerates its rate of vegetative reproduction under increased levels of dissolved nitrogen. they replaced the regulatory protein nrx2 in the duckweed with a structurally similar protein from a closely related species, spirodela polyrhiza , which does not show accelerated reproduction at elevated nitrogen levels. at low nitrogen concentrations, unmodified and modified l. minor plants reproduced at the same rate, but at high nitrogen concentrations, the unmodified plants reproduced markedly faster while the modified plants did not, which suggests that ______ blank

preview: In a four-year study of Strepsiptera parasites infecting Polistes dominula paper wasps, entomologist Léa Carvalho and colleagues observed a…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? in a four-year study of strepsiptera parasites infecting polistes dominula paper wasps, entomologist léa carvalho and colleagues observed a counterintuitive pattern: rather than shortening its host's life, as parasites usually do, strepsiptera infection markedly prolonged the lifespan of an infected worker wasp and appeared to delay the typical signs of aging. the infected wasps also stopped foraging, brood-tending, and nest-construction duties and were instead fed and groomed by their uninfected nestmates as they remained inside the nest. by contrast, the researchers found that uninfected workers in parasitized colonies had distinctly shorter lifespans than uninfected workers in colonies free of strepsiptera , most likely because the ______ blank

preview: A metallurgical analysis by Sergio Trujillo and colleagues of coins minted in the Iberian city of Gadir during the third and second centuri…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? a metallurgical analysis by sergio trujillo and colleagues of coins minted in the iberian city of gadir during the third and second centuries bce shows a steady change in their composition: a coin from approximately 260 bce contains about 96% silver and 2% tin, while a coin from 195 bce (toward the close of the rule of magistrate bodmilkar) contains 71.4% silver and 26.8% tin, giving it a noticeably duller sheen that merchants would have detected. because coins with a silver content below 80% were widely regarded as unfit for long-distance trade, trujillo et al. argue that a confidence crisis in the gadiran currency emerged around 195 bce — one that was eventually eased, despite gadir's ongoing tribute obligations, by bodmilkar's successor hannobaal's decision to ______ blank

preview: Under the constitutional monarchy of Sweden during the early nineteenth century, the right to vote in elections for the lower house of the…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? under the constitutional monarchy of sweden during the early nineteenth century, the right to vote in elections for the lower house of the riksdag required, in part, that an individual pay at least a specified minimum in direct taxes to the crown. the four most common direct taxes were levied on agricultural land; on forested timberland; on the appraised value of dwellings; and on registered trades such as ironworking and milling. (capital invested abroad was either entirely exempt or subject to only minimal duties.) although relatively few people paid the tax on agricultural land, that tax served as the principal route to voter eligibility and accounted for more than two-thirds of the crown's tax revenue during this period, suggesting that during the early nineteenth century ______ blank

preview: In 2023, behavioral economist Marisol Ortega and her team examined how different decision-making styles affected commuters' willingness to…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? in 2023, behavioral economist marisol ortega and her team examined how different decision-making styles affected commuters' willingness to enroll in a city's optional ride-sharing program, which lowered fares but required slightly longer travel times than driving alone. commuters who described themselves as relying on a values mode or a community mode—styles that emphasize personal ideals or social belonging, respectively—were more likely to enroll in the program. by contrast, commuters using a utility mode—a style centered on minimizing both monetary expense and lost time—were more likely to keep driving alone, even though enrolling would have lowered their monthly fares. this pattern suggests that commuters using a utility mode ______ blank

preview: The fishing communities of the Pacific coast have long carried specialized knowledge about local marine life, including details about the s…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the fishing communities of the pacific coast have long carried specialized knowledge about local marine life, including details about the seasonal movements and feeding habits of many species. according to a 2020 study, more than two-thirds of the behavioral patterns documented for fish native to a particular stretch of coastline are encoded in the everyday vocabulary of a single coastal indigenous language. however, as younger speakers increasingly turn to a national language for daily communication, their ancestral languages are spoken less and less. these facts give particular weight to coastal communities’ efforts to revitalize their languages. by keeping languages such as kwak’wala, nuu-chah-nulth, and others in active daily use, coastal communities are also ______ blank

preview: Earlier studies conducted within individual European countries had identified consistent links between personality traits—measured along fi…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? earlier studies conducted within individual european countries had identified consistent links between personality traits—measured along five broad dimensions—and the kinds of food flavors people prefer. to test whether these links extend beyond a single national context, anika müller and colleagues surveyed more than 40,000 people across forty-eight countries on six continents about their personality traits and their flavor preferences for european-style cuisines (e.g., savory, smoky, herbal, tangy, sweet). the cross-cultural data confirmed that the five-dimension personality framework captured participants’ preferences for these european-style cuisines well and revealed broadly similar patterns linking traits to flavor preferences from country to country. these results suggest that ______ blank

preview: A recent international convention obligates pharmaceutical firms to share profits with the local communities whose traditional remedies and…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? a recent international convention obligates pharmaceutical firms to share profits with the local communities whose traditional remedies and botanical knowledge informed the development of new medicines. however, the convention also allows the firms to keep the specific terms of these benefit-sharing contracts undisclosed. some community advocates worry that this confidentiality provision could end up ______ blank

preview: Researcher Hideo Tanaka and colleagues studied how two kinds of human-generated noise affect the foraging behavior of beluga whales near bu…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? researcher hideo tanaka and colleagues studied how two kinds of human-generated noise affect the foraging behavior of beluga whales near busy shipping channels. they compared the whales’ deep-dive feeding behavior under three conditions: natural ocean sound, the rumble of cargo-ship engines alone, and the rumble of cargo-ship engines combined with the high-frequency sonar pings used by some vessels for navigation. both engine rumble alone and engine rumble combined with sonar pings disrupted the whales’ foraging compared to natural conditions, but—unexpectedly—the team found essentially no behavioral difference between the two engine-noise conditions. one reasonable explanation for this surprising finding is that ______ blank

preview: Phage-display peptides—short protein fragments engineered on the surface of bacteriophages to recognize and stick to specific molecular tar…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? phage-display peptides—short protein fragments engineered on the surface of bacteriophages to recognize and stick to specific molecular targets—can be used to detect harmful microbes in beverages, though how reliably they avoid false alarms (their specificity, i.e., the probability of giving a negative result when the target microbe is absent) in real-world drinks has remained unclear. diana marquez and colleagues created a strip that incorporates phage-display peptides designed to recognize strain st131 of the bacterium klebsiella pneumoniae ; the strip turns from yellow to green when the peptides bind to their target. the team tested the strip in store-bought apple cider that had been spiked with k. pneumoniae st131, with other strains of k. pneumoniae , or with other bacterial species. after exposure, the strip from the st131 sample turned green while the strips from the other samples remained yellow, suggesting that ______ blank

preview: Until recently, many historians believed that the city-states of the ancient Hittite world lacked formal craft markets during the Late Bron…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? until recently, many historians believed that the city-states of the ancient hittite world lacked formal craft markets during the late bronze age (roughly 1400–1200 bce). part of the reason for this belief was that these historians mistook the workshops of the region as roughly interchangeable in what they could produce. markets generally develop because individuals or groups want to acquire goods that they themselves do not produce. historians substantially overlooked the technical diversity of hittite craftsmanship and so assumed that ______ blank

preview: The freshwater pupfish Cyprinodon diabolis survives only in a single limestone cavern in the Mojave Desert, and the extreme narrowness of i…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the freshwater pupfish cyprinodon diabolis survives only in a single limestone cavern in the mojave desert, and the extreme narrowness of its range makes it acutely vulnerable to extinction. the conventional single-species framework for conservation centers on protecting individual species most at risk, like c. diabolis , but a growing number of conservation biologists have argued that an ecosystem-based framework—one that takes into account the many interactions among water chemistry, geology, and the various organisms of a given area—may yield better outcomes for all the species that share a location. if this newer view is correct, the single-species framework applied to c. diabolis could thus ______ blank

preview: For the 1981 performance piece Fence Line , the Pinoy art collective Pintig blindfolded members Marisol Reyes and Jaime Domingo and led the…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? for the 1981 performance piece fence line , the pinoy art collective pintig blindfolded members marisol reyes and jaime domingo and led them along a chain-link border on the edge of an industrial district in san diego. the work is plainly an exploration of restriction, but most commentators focus exclusively on reyes and the labor restrictions imposed on filipina migrant workers at the time, an emphasis that is understandable but offers no explanation for the participation of reyes’s male collaborator domingo. instead, we should consider that in 1981 the u.s. gallery system recognized filipino american artists almost exclusively as makers of traditional documentary photography, leaving conceptual and performance-based filipino american artists, including pintig, with little institutional visibility. this perspective suggests that ______ blank

preview: The water buffalo was domesticated in South Asia approximately 5,000 years ago, but which wild species was its ancestor remained contested.…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the water buffalo was domesticated in south asia approximately 5,000 years ago, but which wild species was its ancestor remained contested. a study led by priya nair concluded that the domestic water buffalo descends from the wild asian buffalo, finding that 71 percent of the modern water buffalo's genome derives from that wild ancestor and the remaining 29 percent comes from domestic zebu cattle. the zebu, in turn, derives 96 percent of its genome from its own wild ancestor, the aurochs, and the remaining 4 percent from the water buffalo. assuming nair's team is correct that this cross-species genomic sharing arose from a sustained period of interbreeding between the two domesticated populations after their initial domestication, it can be inferred that ______ blank

preview: Many species of fish consume foods containing melanin precursors, which the body converts into the dark pigments that color scales. Pigment…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? many species of fish consume foods containing melanin precursors, which the body converts into the dark pigments that color scales. pigmentation tends to be especially intense in male fish, and because melanin precursors are also linked to immune-system robustness, the darkest coloration generally serves as what biologists call an honest signal of a male fish's overall health to potential mates. however, marine biologist renata costa and colleagues have observed that males in several wrasse species of the genus halichoeres use specialized arrangements of guanine crystals in their scales to manipulate light, generating an appearance of saturation that is not, in fact, supported by the actual melanin content of those scales. these findings suggest that ______ blank

preview: In an investigation of the problem-solving abilities of long-tailed macaques ( Macaca fascicularis ), researchers failed to control for the…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? in an investigation of the problem-solving abilities of long-tailed macaques ( macaca fascicularis ), researchers failed to control for the physical difficulty of the tasks they used to evaluate the monkeys. the problem-solving abilities of macaques given tasks requiring minimal dexterity, such as pushing a lever to release a treat, were judged by the same criteria as those of macaques given physically demanding tasks, such as untwisting a cap and inserting a finger to retrieve a treat. the findings of the investigation, therefore, ______ blank

preview: In the late nineteenth century, some settler-descendant ranchers on the southern Great Plains adopted range-management methods that the Com…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? in the late nineteenth century, some settler-descendant ranchers on the southern great plains adopted range-management methods that the comanche people had developed many decades earlier, but it appears that few of those ranchers had ever spent time on comanche-managed land themselves. setting aside the unlikely possibility that several ranchers of the period independently invented techniques that the comanche people had already created, these facts most strongly suggest that ______ blank

preview: Robin Hood legends—tales centered on the figure of the outlaw archer Robin Hood—come down to us from a number of sources, including the bal…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? robin hood legends—tales centered on the figure of the outlaw archer robin hood—come down to us from a number of sources, including the ballad robin hood and the monk , preserved in a manuscript from around 1450, and the printed compilation a gest of robyn hode , dating to roughly 1495. one of the most influential sources, however, was the chronicler john major’s history of greater britain , written in 1521; portions of that work were later reworked by the playwright anthony munday into the play the downfall of robert, earl of huntington in 1598. but munday didn’t simply adapt major’s material—he added to it as well, introducing for the first time the idea that robin hood was a dispossessed nobleman of the huntington line, an idea that became central to later versions of the legend. from this, it follows that ______ blank

preview: During their larval phase, many species of intertidal sea anemone are attracted to surfaces with elevated levels of trace metals. To better…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? during their larval phase, many species of intertidal sea anemone are attracted to surfaces with elevated levels of trace metals. to better understand how trace-metal pollution from coastal runoff might affect such species, researchers studied the effect of exposure to low levels of dissolved copper on the reproductive success of the starlet sea anemone ( nematostella vectensis ). while exposure to low levels of dissolved copper had no significant effect on adult spawning rates or egg fertilization in n. vectensis , the development of fertilized eggs in the presence of dissolved copper was completely arrested before the larval stage. these findings suggest that ______ blank

preview: Standard accounts of persuasive communication hold that information attributed to credentialed authorities is judged to be more credible th…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? standard accounts of persuasive communication hold that information attributed to credentialed authorities is judged to be more credible than information from non-experts. when health-communication researcher maya adekoya and her colleagues tested public-service announcements promoting routine cancer screenings, however, they found that study participants rated announcements featuring former screening patients as substantially more credible than announcements featuring oncologists (that is, credentialed experts). one explanation may be that the oncologists’ status as credentialed experts wasn’t disregarded but was instead outweighed by another factor: ______ blank

preview: A pigeon-borne dispatch is a method of sending written messages—usually short notes on lightweight paper—by attaching them to the legs of t…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? a pigeon-borne dispatch is a method of sending written messages—usually short notes on lightweight paper—by attaching them to the legs of trained homing pigeons. pigeon-borne dispatches were used for relatively rapid communication over large distances for military, commercial, and journalistic purposes during the nineteenth and early twentieth centuries, but they were largely effective only over established routes, and the success of a flight depended on factors such as the weather and the presence of predatory birds. for these reasons, pigeon-borne dispatches were eventually replaced by technology that ______ blank

preview: In a 2019 essay, historian Diego Albarracín argues that in eighteenth-century Buenos Aires, members of the city’s gremios (trade associatio…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? in a 2019 essay, historian diego albarracín argues that in eighteenth-century buenos aires, members of the city’s gremios (trade associations) participated in a civic culture in which gift giving both signaled and conferred social standing. earlier research on this culture had concentrated on the philanthropic donations of the city’s wealthiest gremios. for his part, albarracín looks instead at the gifting of handmade objects (such as ornately tooled leather goods and silver-inlaid hardware) within the artisan gremios, whose members were “both highly discerning makers and highly discerning judges of work in their own craft.” this narrower lens leads albarracín to conclude that, for artisan gremio members, the exchange of such finely worked objects within the gremio ______ blank

preview: A widely recognized social norm of gift giving holds that the amount of time spent obtaining a present is taken as a measure of how thought…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? a widely recognized social norm of gift giving holds that the amount of time spent obtaining a present is taken as a measure of how thoughtful the gift is. consumer psychologists aiko tanaka, maria delgado, and rohan kapoor examined this norm in their studies of digital flower deliveries, noting that while recipients usually view same-day floral deliveries arranged through a smartphone app (which can be ordered with a few taps from anywhere) as functionally equivalent or even superior to deliveries arranged through a traditional florist (which often require a phone call or an in-person visit), 91% of gift givers in the study chose the traditional-florist option when sending flowers for important occasions. the researchers’ data lead them to conclude that ______ blank

preview: Shape-memory polymers are a class of lightweight materials engineered to return to a programmed shape after being deformed and then exposed…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? shape-memory polymers are a class of lightweight materials engineered to return to a programmed shape after being deformed and then exposed to a triggering stimulus, such as heat. these polymers are often used in deployable medical stents and protective gear because they can flex and re-form under stress; however, conventional shape-memory polymers gradually lose their ability to recover their original shape after many cycles of deformation, owing to internal fatigue. now, hiroto nakamura of kyoto university and colleagues have developed a shape-memory polymer with unusually durable molecular cross-links. unlike earlier shape-memory polymers, nakamura’s team’s material maintains nearly its full shape-recovery capacity even after extended cycling. these findings suggest that ______ blank

preview: Online streaming platforms have given musicologists an unprecedented opportunity to observe stylistic shifts in popular music as they happe…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? online streaming platforms have given musicologists an unprecedented opportunity to observe stylistic shifts in popular music as they happen. a striking recent case involves the chord pattern known as the "plagal cadence," which surged in indie pop tracks uploaded to streaming services beginning around 2017. musicologists watched the cadence first appear in short demo recordings shared by independent artists; from there, it spread to full studio releases distributed on the same platforms, and within a few years it had begun appearing in live performances by major touring acts. researchers have documented comparable patterns in other genres, suggesting that streaming platforms ______ blank

preview: If certain pottery shards recovered from excavations of the highland settlement of Verra Alta, in present-day Peru, date from the ninth cen…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? if certain pottery shards recovered from excavations of the highland settlement of verra alta, in present-day peru, date from the ninth century ce, that may lend support to claims that the settlement was established before or around that time. there is other evidence, however, strongly indicating a tenth century ce founding date for verra alta. if both the pottery dates and the tenth century ce founding date are correct, that would imply that ______ blank

preview: The musicologist Susan Carrow has argued that in dramatic theater, dialogue spoken in close physical proximity between two actors encourage…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the musicologist susan carrow has argued that in dramatic theater, dialogue spoken in close physical proximity between two actors encourages audience members to perceive the characters as emotionally intimate. this effect is intensified by what she calls "naturalistic blocking," in which the actors' movements appear so unforced that the staging seems to disappear. conversely, carrow proposes that dialogue exchanged across great physical distance or with stylized, mannered blocking would prompt audiences to view the characters as emotionally estranged. consider, for example, the parlor confrontation in henrik ibsen's hedda gabler as staged in a recent production: the actors deliver their lines from opposite ends of a long room, with elaborate, ritualized gestures. according to carrow's logic, this staging should affect viewers by ______ blank

preview: Marisol Aguilar and colleagues analyzed the genomes of more than 150 domesticated horses excavated from sites in the Andean highlands datin…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? marisol aguilar and colleagues analyzed the genomes of more than 150 domesticated horses excavated from sites in the andean highlands dating from 4,000 years ago to the present. they found that the horses formed a genetically isolated population of highland breeds until roughly 600 years ago, at which point there was substantial interbreeding with coastal horse breeds. moreover, starting around 600 years ago, some sites began to contain artifacts consistent with a coastal lowland origin, such as carved seashell ornaments, but the people of these highland sites show no evidence of having traveled extensively to the coast. from this, aguilar and colleagues concluded that around 600 years ago ______ blank

preview: Core samples obtained by a submersible probe at the Pelagia Basin on the floor of the South Atlantic suggest unusual depositional condition…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? core samples obtained by a submersible probe at the pelagia basin on the floor of the south atlantic suggest unusual depositional conditions far below the ocean surface. to characterize the nature of the depositional environment, hiroshi nakamura and colleagues analyzed the grain size of basin sediment, finding that although there are intervals of large, well-rounded pebbles, most of the sediment consists of very fine clays that show signs of layered, calm settling interrupted by brief disturbances. nakamura and colleagues concluded that the pebbles are gravel deposited by powerful turbidity currents, whereas the fine clays are deposited by slow settling out of still seawater, leading the researchers to posit that ______ blank

preview: "Improvisation" in jazz is typically understood as melodic invention generated in the moment of performance. In the 1990s, Brazilian saxoph…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? "improvisation" in jazz is typically understood as melodic invention generated in the moment of performance. in the 1990s, brazilian saxophonist lia branco assembled a complex system of pre-arranged loops and pedal-triggered samples that allowed her, in concert, to layer entire passages by activating a single switch, producing the elaborate "stack" recordings of that decade. branco described this process as making an entire performance from "one decision," signaling a sharp departure from the centrality of moment-to-moment invention that defined her work in the 1980s. some music critics claim that this shift represents "removing improvisation" from her practice altogether. therefore, regardless of whether triggering the loop system constitutes improvisation, both branco and these critics likely agree that ______ blank

preview: Some Heliotrigona neotropica , a meadow-nesting bee found in southern Brazil, have colonized urban rooftops in the region. Although there i…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? some heliotrigona neotropica , a meadow-nesting bee found in southern brazil, have colonized urban rooftops in the region. although there is little genetic difference between meadow and rooftop h. neotropica and all members of the species can perform the same range of waggle dances, biologist pedro aragão and colleagues found that the meaning and context of those dances vary by location—e.g., the rapid figure-eight that meadow-dwelling h. neotropica use to indicate nectar sources is used by rooftop bees to recruit guards—and the choreography of rooftop bee dances shows some rooftop-specific variations as well. aragão and colleagues note that differences in dance communication could accumulate to the point of inhibiting interbreeding among bees from different locations, suggesting that ______ blank

preview: Many birds, including songbirds, must molt their feathers periodically, and feather molting is known to have a role in everything from regu…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? many birds, including songbirds, must molt their feathers periodically, and feather molting is known to have a role in everything from regulating body temperature to refreshing the bright plumage used in mating displays. but some scientists argue that, from an evolutionary standpoint, going several weeks with damaged or partially absent flight feathers leaves a bird so exposed to predators and so impaired in foraging that the known benefits of molting seem insufficient to explain why it became so widespread among bird species. these scientists therefore imply that ______ blank

preview: Some legal theorists hold that the justice of a court's decision depends solely on whether the ruling follows from established rules, irres…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? some legal theorists hold that the justice of a court's decision depends solely on whether the ruling follows from established rules, irrespective of the particular relationships and obligations that bound the parties before the dispute arose. scholar adaeze nwosu has shown that surviving records of pre-colonial igbo customary law express a very different view. nwosu reveals that these records describe a legal system in which a person's actions are evaluated in light of the kinship bonds the person was party to and the way the outcome affects the cohesion of the wider lineage. to the extent that these records are representative of pre-colonial igbo legal thought, nwosu's analysis suggests that ______ blank

preview: Many of the operas composed by Giuseppe Verdi address sweeping themes that still resonate with today's audiences. For instance, La Traviata…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? many of the operas composed by giuseppe verdi address sweeping themes that still resonate with today's audiences. for instance, la traviata , which is set in the parisian high society of verdi's century, takes on the themes of social judgment versus individual love and sacrifice versus self-interest, and the opera continues to be staged and recorded around the world. but appreciating verdi's so-called risorgimento operas—works like nabucco and i lombardi —can require familiarity with the complex politics of nineteenth-century italian unification. consequently, ______ blank

preview: Scientists studying Venus long thought the history of its atmosphere was relatively simple. One reason for this is that spectroscopic data…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? scientists studying venus long thought the history of its atmosphere was relatively simple. one reason for this is that spectroscopic data collected by orbiting probes showed that the atmosphere was overwhelmingly composed of carbon dioxide, likely as a result of sustained volcanic outgassing that filled the atmosphere with greenhouse gases, producing the planet's intense surface heat. a study led by adèle marchetti focused on additional information—further analysis of probe data and ultraviolet wavelengths detected from venus's cloud tops—that revealed the presence of surprisingly large amounts of free oxygen in certain altitude bands above the planet. since a planetary atmosphere produced entirely by volcanic outgassing would be unlikely to contain significant amounts of free oxygen, marchetti concluded that ______ blank

preview: The bottle gourd ( Lagenaria siceraria ) descends from a wild plant native to sub-Saharan Africa. It is also widely found in coastal South…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the bottle gourd ( lagenaria siceraria ) descends from a wild plant native to sub-saharan africa. it is also widely found in coastal south america, where archaeological evidence confirms that indigenous peoples were using bottle gourds as containers and floats well before any seafaring contact occurred across the thousands of miles of ocean separating them from africa. to explain how the bottle gourd was first introduced into south america, botanist eleni karagiannis and colleagues analyzed the dna of numerous varieties of the plant, concluding that south american varieties diverged from african ones more than 100,000 years ago. given that south america was peopled only in the last twenty thousand years, the team concluded that ______ blank

preview: The Nuragic peoples, the civilization from which many present-day Sardinians culturally descend, emerged as early as 1800 B.C.E. in what is…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the nuragic peoples, the civilization from which many present-day sardinians culturally descend, emerged as early as 1800 b.c.e. in what is now central sardinia and dispersed suddenly in the early 700s b.c.e., abandoning established stone-tower villages where they maintained systems for cultivating barley and raising sheep. recent analysis comparing sheep remains at the su nuraxi site in central sardinia to samples from modern sheep populations on the aegean island of lemnos determined that the lemnos sheep descended in part from sheep raised at su nuraxi, with shared genetic markers first appearing only after 720 b.c.e. thus, researchers concluded that ______ blank

preview: Lichens can struggle in alpine tundra environments because they require enough sunlight for photosynthesis but not so much wind-driven expo…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? lichens can struggle in alpine tundra environments because they require enough sunlight for photosynthesis but not so much wind-driven exposure that they risk severe desiccation. researchers aila lindstrom and mei tanaka found several patches of umbilicaria americana , a leaf-like rock lichen, growing beneath thin slabs of weathered slate above the treeline in the cascade range. to evaluate whether these partially translucent rocks benefited the lichen, the researchers compared thallus surface area, a measure of lichen growth, when u. americana was growing exposed on bare granite versus when it was growing beneath the slate slabs. they found that thallus surface area was 58% greater for lichens growing beneath slate compared to lichens on exposed granite, suggesting that ______ blank

preview: Researchers recently found that occasional pauses during an enjoyable experience, such as brief intermissions inserted during a live theate…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? researchers recently found that occasional pauses during an enjoyable experience, such as brief intermissions inserted during a live theater performance, often increase audiences' reported enjoyment of the show. suspecting that pauses during an unpleasant experience would have the opposite effect, the researchers had participants watch a continuous 25-minute video of slowly dripping motor oil and anticipated that those whose viewing experience was frequently interrupted with short breaks of blank screen would thus ______ blank

preview: Petroglyphs are images carved or pecked into rock surfaces. The Coso petroglyphs were carved into volcanic basalt in the Coso Range of east…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? petroglyphs are images carved or pecked into rock surfaces. the coso petroglyphs were carved into volcanic basalt in the coso range of eastern california by indigenous peoples over a period of many millennia. american archaeologist lucia ortega specializes in these petroglyphs. at a japanese exhibit about north american rock art, she saw an old photograph of a large petroglyph depicting a swordfish-like figure and was surprised that she didn't recognize it. ortega returned to california and used a high-resolution aerial scanner mounted on a helicopter to survey a wide area, looking for the figure from the air. this approach suggests that ortega thought that if she hadn't already seen it, the swordfish-like petroglyph ______ blank

preview: Assembled in the early 1700s largely through the efforts of native court scribes, the Bayan al-Wuzara is the most important collection of h…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? assembled in the early 1700s largely through the efforts of native court scribes, the bayan al-wuzara is the most important collection of historical prose in classical wolof, the principal language of the jolof confederation in west africa. the chronicles portray jolof society before the arrival of french colonial administrators in the region, and marginal notes in the bayan al-wuzara indicate that much of the collection's content predates that arrival. nonetheless, some of the chronicles contain inarguable references to administrative practices and legal customs common in france during this era. thus, some scholars have concluded that ______ blank

preview: Heavy rains can sweep large quantities of fine silt onto a freshwater wetland, smothering healthy reeds and producing a silt flat—a low, pl…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? heavy rains can sweep large quantities of fine silt onto a freshwater wetland, smothering healthy reeds and producing a silt flat—a low, plant-free zone that tends to pond shallow water—within the wetland's central basin. ecologist adekunle adebayo and colleagues found that tunneling crayfish living within these silt flats improve drainage by churning the sediment, leading the flats to shrink as wetland reeds move back in. along the wetland's outer banks, however, crayfish-induced sediment churning can promote bank loss by accelerating undercutting, suggesting that the tunneling action of crayfish ______ blank

preview: The ratio of dissolved oxygen to other gases in a body of water—represented by a measure called the dissolved oxygen fraction—influences a…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? the ratio of dissolved oxygen to other gases in a body of water—represented by a measure called the dissolved oxygen fraction—influences a variety of marine biological phenomena, notably plankton productivity and the depth at which fish concentrate. for europa, jupiter's smallest galilean moon, the in situ data that exist beneath the icy crust are too sparse and discrepant to fully constrain the range of the dissolved oxygen fraction at various depths within the subsurface ocean. hiroshi sato and colleagues point out that outputs of the eur-sim oceanographic model of europa, which track closely to in situ measurements in some respects, reflect how the model's developers responded to this challenge: by prescribing a uniform dissolved oxygen fraction for the topmost level of the subsurface ocean. it is therefore important to note that ______ blank

preview: One theory behind whale echolocation speculates that it originated in a coastal, surface-feeding cetacean ancestor that relied primarily on…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? one theory behind whale echolocation speculates that it originated in a coastal, surface-feeding cetacean ancestor that relied primarily on vision in shallow, sunlit waters, like modern dolphins do near shore, and eventually evolved into a finely tuned system for hunting in the open ocean. but recently, researchers observed beaked whales, another relative of dolphins, generating rapid clicks while diving in completely dark deep-water canyons in pursuit of squid. these observations may suggest that ______ blank

preview: With the ongoing expansion of telehealth, patients are expecting shorter and shorter wait times for video consultations, but telehealth pro…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? with the ongoing expansion of telehealth, patients are expecting shorter and shorter wait times for video consultations, but telehealth providers continue to struggle with intake-stage triage (the initial step in routing patients to the right clinician) due to challenges such as fragmented medical histories and inconsistent insurance information. innovations to mitigate these challenges have been emerging—the use of ai-driven symptom checkers, for example—but these innovations tend to engender their own complications (e.g., regulatory uncertainty about ai making preliminary diagnostic decisions), leading researchers to conclude that ______ blank

preview: Overbrowsing by white-tailed deer has caused many eastern North American hardwood forests to be replaced by browse-line glades—forest under…
searchText: which choice most logically completes the text? overbrowsing by white-tailed deer has caused many eastern north american hardwood forests to be replaced by browse-line glades—forest understories stripped of saplings and covered in invasive shrubs that deer avoid eating. invasive shrubs in browse-line glades persist in a state of low palatability that lessens their food value—and thus their appeal—to many large herbivores. maria vianello and colleagues penned american bison, a once-abundant herbivore species that has been reintroduced to several eastern preserves only in recent years, in paddocks that each contained a palatable shrub and an unpalatable shrub. the researchers found that the bison selected the palatable shrub in 44.1% of trials and the unpalatable shrub in 39.8% of trials, suggesting that ______ blank
